Floor Database Schema (Drift ORM)\n\n**File**: `/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/lib/data/database/`\n\n### 10.1 Tables Overview\n\n| Table | File | Columns | Primary Key | Foreign Keys |\n|-------|------|---------|-------------|--------------|\n| `Sessions` | `sessions_table.dart` | 7 | `id` (auto-increment) | — |\n| `Attempts` | `attempts_table.dart` | 9 | `id` (auto-increment) | `sessionId → Sessions`, `exerciseId → Exercises` |\n| `Exercises` | `exercises_table.dart` | 8 | `id` (auto-increment) | `categoryId → Categories` |\n| `Categories` | `categories_table.dart` | 9 | `id` (auto-increment) | — |\n| `LiveConversations` | `live_conversations_table.dart` | 8 | `id` (auto-increment) | `sessionId → Sessions` |\n| `AppSettings` | `settings_table.dart` | 3 | `key` (text, composite) | — |\n| `SyncLog` | `sync_log_table.dart` | 6 | `id` (auto-increment) | — |\n\n### 10.2 Sessions Table\n```dart\nclass Sessions extends Table {\n  IntColumn get id => integer().autoIncrement()();\n  DateTimeColumn get startedAt => dateTime().withDefault(currentDateAndTime)();\n  DateTimeColumn get completedAt => dateTime().nullable()();\n  TextColumn get type => text()();                          // 'picture_naming' | 'conversation' | 'breathing'\n  IntColumn get totalExercises => integer().withDefault(const Constant(0))();\n  IntColumn get completedExercises => integer().withDefault(const Constant(0))();\n  RealColumn get averageScore => real().nullable()();\n  BoolColumn get isSynced => boolean().withDefault(const Constant(false))();\n  DateTimeColumn get createdAt => dateTime().withDefault(currentDateAndTime)();\n}\n```\n\n### 10.3 Attempts Table\n```dart\nclass Attempts extends Table {\n  IntColumn get id => integer().autoIncrement()();\n  IntColumn get sessionId => integer().references(Sessions, #id)();\n  IntColumn get exerciseId => integer().references(Exercises, #id).nullable()();\n  TextColumn get userResponse => text()();                  // STT transcript of user's spoken attempt\n  TextColumn get expectedAnswer => text()();                // Correct target word\n  RealColumn get scorePercentage => real()();               // 0.0–100.0\n  TextColumn get scoreLevel => text()();                    // 'excellent'|'good'|'almost'|'try_again'\n  IntColumn get responseTimeMs => integer().nullable()();\n  BoolColumn get isOffline => boolean().withDefault(const Constant(false))();\n  BoolColumn get isSynced => boolean().withDefault(const Constant(false))();\n  DateTimeColumn get createdAt => dateTime().withDefault(currentDateAndTime)();\n}\n```\n\n### 10.4 Exercises Table\n```dart\nclass Exercises extends Table {\n  IntColumn get id => integer().autoIncrement()();\n  IntColumn get categoryId => integer().references(Categories, #id)();\n  TextColumn get imagePath => text()();                     // Local WebP image path\n  TextColumn get targetWordSi => text()();                  // Sinhala target word\n  TextColumn get targetWordTa => text()();                  // Tamil target word\n  TextColumn get targetWordEn => text()();                  // English target word\n  TextColumn get phoneticHint => text().nullable()();\n  IntColumn get difficulty => integer().withDefault(const Constant(1))();  // 1–5\n  BoolColumn get isActive => boolean().withDefault(const Constant(true))();\n  DateTimeColumn get createdAt => dateTime().withDefault(currentDateAndTime)();\n}\n```\n\n### 10.5 Categories Table\n```dart\nclass Categories extends Table {\n  IntColumn get id => integer().autoIncrement()();\n  TextColumn get nameSi => text()();                        // Sinhala name\n  TextColumn get nameTa => text()();                        // Tamil name  \n  TextColumn get nameEn => text()();                        // English name\n  TextColumn get descriptionSi => text()();                 // Sinhala description\n  TextColumn get descriptionTa => text()();                 // Tamil description\n  TextColumn get descriptionEn => text()();                 // English description\n  TextColumn get icon => text()();                          // Material icon name\n  IntColumn get sortOrder => integer().withDefault(const Constant(0))();\n  BoolColumn get isActive => boolean().withDefault(const Constant(true))();\n  DateTimeColumn get createdAt => dateTime().withDefault(currentDateAndTime)();\n}\n```\n\n### 10.6 LiveConversations Table\n```dart\nclass LiveConversations extends Table {\n  IntColumn get id => integer().autoIncrement()();\n  IntColumn get sessionId => integer().references(Sessions, #id)();\n  TextColumn get exerciseType => text()();                  // 'category_naming' | 'letter_fluency'\n  IntColumn get durationSeconds => integer()();\n  TextColumn get userTranscript => text()();                // User's speech transcript\n  TextColumn get aiFeedback => text()();                    // AI response text\n  RealColumn get score => real().nullable()();              // Optional score\n  BoolColumn get isSynced => boolean().withDefault(const Constant(false))();\n  DateTimeColumn get createdAt => dateTime().withDefault(currentDateAndTime)();\n}\n```\n\n### 10.7 AppSettings Table (key-value)\n```dart\nclass AppSettings extends Table {\n  TextColumn get key => text()();\n  TextColumn get value => text()();\n  DateTimeColumn get updatedAt => dateTime().withDefault(currentDateAndTime)();\n  @override Set<Column> get primaryKey => {key};\n}\n```\n\n### 10.8 SyncLog Table\n```dart\nclass SyncLog extends Table {\n  IntColumn get id => integer().autoIncrement()();\n  TextColumn get entityType => text()();                    // 'session'|'attempt'|'live_conversation'\n  IntColumn get recordId => integer()();\n  TextColumn get action => text()();                        // 'insert' | 'update' | 'delete'\n  DateTimeColumn get syncedAt => dateTime().withDefault(currentDateAndTime)();\n  TextColumn get status => text().withDefault(const Constant('pending'))();  // 'pending'|'synced'|'failed'\n}\n```\n\n### 10.9 Database Definition\n**File**: `/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/lib/data/database/handa_database.dart`\n```dart\n@DriftDatabase(tables: [\n  Sessions, Attempts, Exercises, Categories, LiveConversations, AppSettings, SyncLog\n])\nclass HandaDatabase extends _$HandaDatabase { ... }\n```\n\n### 10.10 DAOs\n\n| DAO | File | Key Operations |\n|-----|------|----------------|\n| `SessionDao` | `session_dao.dart` | `createSession`, `completeSession`, `getSessionById`, `getSessionsByDateRange` |\n| `AttemptDao` | `attempt_dao.dart` | `saveAttempt`, `getAttemptsBySession`, `getAttemptsByExercise` |\n| `ExerciseDao` | `exercise_dao.dart` | `getExercisesByCategory`, `getExerciseById` |\n| `CategoryDao` | `category_dao.dart` | `getAllCategories`, `getCategoryById` |\n| `LiveConversationDao` | `live_conversation_dao.dart` | `saveConversation`, `getConversationsBySession` |\n| `SettingsDao` | `settings_dao.dart` | `getSetting`, `setSetting`, `getAllSettings` |\n\n---\n\n## 11. All Domain Models\n\n### 11.1 Session\n```dart\nclass Session {\n  final int id;\n  final DateTime startedAt;\n  final DateTime? completedAt;\n  final String type;           // 'picture_naming' | 'conversation' | 'breathing'\n  final int totalExercises;\n  final int completedExercises;\n  final double? averageScore;\n  final bool isSynced;\n  bool get isCompleted => completedAt != null;\n  Duration get duration => (completedAt ?? DateTime.now()).difference(startedAt);\n}\n```\n\n### 11.2 Attempt\n```dart\nclass Attempt {\n  final int id, sessionId,? exerciseId;\n  final String userResponse;     // What user actually said (STT)\n  final String expectedAnswer;   // Correct target word\n  final double scorePercentage;  // 0–100\n  final String scoreLevel;       // 'excellent'|'good'|'almost'|'try_again'\n  final int? responseTimeMs;\n  final bool isOffline, isSynced;\n  final DateTime createdAt;\n  bool get isPassing => !isTryAgain;\n}\n```\n\n### 11.3 Exercise\n```dart\nclass Exercise {\n  final int id, categoryId, difficulty;\n  final String imagePath;        // Local WebP\n  final String targetWordSi, targetWordTa, targetWordEn;\n  final String? phoneticHint;\n  final bool isActive;\n  String targetWordIn(String languageCode);  // Returns target word by language\n}\n```\n\n### 11.4 Category\n```dart\nclass Category {\n  final int id, sortOrder;\n  final String nameSi, nameTa, nameEn;\n  final String descriptionSi, descriptionTa, descriptionEn;\n  final String icon;\n  final bool isActive;\n  String nameIn(String languageCode);\n  String descriptionIn(String languageCode);\n}\n```\n\n### 11.5 AppSetting\n```dart\nclass AppSetting {\n  final String key;\n  final String value;\n  final DateTime updatedAt;\n}\n```\n\n### 11.6 LiveConversation\n```dart\nclass LiveConversation {\n  final int id, sessionId, durationSeconds;\n  final String exerciseType;     // 'category_naming' | 'letter_fluency'\n  final String userTranscript;\n  final String aiFeedback;\n  final double? score;\n  final bool isSynced;\n  final DateTime createdAt;\n}\n```\n\n### 11.7 Scoring Engine\n\n**File**: `/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/lib/domain/services/scoring_engine.dart` (101 lines)\n\n**Two modes**:\n1. **Online**: Score from Gemini API → `evaluatePictureNaming()` returns 0–100 score\n2. **Offline**: Levenshtein string similarity via `LevenshteinDistance.similarity()`\n\n**Key Methods**:\n```dart\nstatic ScoreResult evaluate({\n  required String userResponse,       // What user said (STT)\n  required String expectedAnswer,     // Target word\n  double? geminiScore,                // Optional — takes precedence if provided\n  int? responseTimeMs,\n}) -> ScoreResult(scorePercentage, scoreLevel, method: 'online'|'offline')\n\nstatic bool isMastered(List<Attempt> attempts)\n  // Requires >= AppConstants.attemptsForMastery (3) recent attempts\n  // ALL must be >= AppConstants.masteryThreshold (70%)\n\nstatic String encouragementPhrase(String scoreLevel)\n  // Returns Sinhala encouragement based on level ('excellent' → 'විශිෂ්ටයි!')\n\nstatic String breathingEncouragement(int daysCompleted)\n  // Returns Sinhala encouragement for breathing milestones (7, 14, 21, 30 days)\n```\n\n**Score Levels**:\n| Level | Threshold | Sinhala Encouragement |\n|-------|-----------|----------------------|\n| excellent | >= 90% | විශිෂ්ටයි! ඔබට පුදුමාකාර කුසලතා තියෙනවා! |\n| good | >= 75% | හොඳයි! ඔබ හොඳින් ඉගෙන ගන්නවා! |\n| almost | >= 60% | බොහෝ දුරට හරි! තව ටිකක් උත්සාහ කරමු! |\n| try_again | < 60% | කරදරයක් නෑ, නැවත උත්සාහ කරමු! |\n\n---\n\n## 12. Screen & Routing Map\n\n### 12.1 Route Table\n\n| Route | Screen | File | Description |\n|-------|--------|------|-------------|\n| `/` | Splash | `splash_screen.dart` | App launch screen |\n| `/home` | Home | `home_screen.dart` | Main menu (exercises, conversation, breathing, dashboard) |\n| `/exercise` | Exercise | `exercise_screen.dart` | Picture naming (category grid → exercise flow → results) |\n| `/session` | Session | `session_screen.dart` | Full therapy session (8 shuffled exercises with Live API) |\n| `/results` | Results | `results_screen.dart` | Session results review |\n| `/conversation` | Conversation | `conversation_screen.dart` | Free-form chat with Gemini Live |\n| `/breathing` | Breathing | `breathing_screen.dart` | 4-7-8 box breathing exercise |\n| `/dashboard` | Dashboard | `dashboard_screen.dart` | Patient progress charts |\n| `/settings` | Settings | `settings_screen.dart` | App configuration |\n| `/onboarding` | Onboarding | `onboarding_screen.dart` | First-time user setup |\n| `/caregiver-dashboard` | Caregiver | `caregiver_dashboard_screen.dart` | Caregiver progress view |\n\n**Router**: `go_router` with `GoRouter` instance via `appRouterProvider` (Riverpod).\n\n### 12.2 Screen Architecture\n\n**Exercise Screen** (755 lines — most complex):\n- **Phase 1 — Category Grid**: Shows `ExerciseCategory.sampleCategories()` as 2-column grid\n  - Categories: Animals (සතුන්), Food (ආහාර), Body (ශරීරය), Home (නිවස)\n  - Each has 6 `ExerciseItem` objects with `(sinhala, english, emoji)`\n- **Phase 2 — Exercise Flow**: Per-exercise view showing emoji, phase indicator, transcriptions, mic button\n  - States: `connecting → asking → listening → evaluating → result → completed`\n  - Mic uses push-to-talk (tap down = start, tap up = stop)\n  - Sends `sendAudioStreamEnd()` to signal turn completion\n- **Phase 3 — Results Summary**: Score badge, per-item list with emoji + feedback\n\n**Session Screen** (638 lines — therapy session):\n- Creates DB session on init via `SessionDao.createSession()`\n- 8 exercises selected randomly from all categories\n- Uses `LiveApiService` for real-time voice interaction\n- `_handleMessage` processes:\n  - `message.data` → audio playback (base64 inlineData from JSON mode)\n  - `message.serverContent?.outputTranscription` → model's speech-to-text\n  - `message.serverContent?.inputTranscription` → user's speech-to-text\n  - `turnComplete || generationComplete` → state advance\n- Score extracted via regex `[(\\d+)]` from model response text\n- Sinhala enum: `connecting → asking → listening → evaluating → result → completed`\n\n**Conversation Screen** (432 lines — free chat):\n- Simplest Live API integration — just sends/receives text messages\n- Mic streaming with push-to-talk + `sendAudioStreamEnd()`\n- Supports both `sendText()` and `sendRealtimeText()`\n- Sinhala suggestion chips: \"ආයුබෝවන්! කොහොමද?\", \"අද කාලගුණය හොඳයි\", \"මම අද උදේ ආහාර ගත්තා\"\n\n### 12.3 Provider Architecture\n\n```\nliveApiServiceProvider (Provider<LiveApiService>)\n  ├── singleton (Riverpod)\n  └── dispose() → service.dispose()\n\ngeminiApiClientProvider (Provider<GeminiApiClient>)\n  └── singleton\n  \ngeminiHealthProvider (FutureProvider<bool>)\n  └── watches geminiApiClientProvider → calls healthCheck()\n\ngeminiModelInfoProvider (Provider<String>)\n  └── returns 'Gemini 2.5 Flash'\n\n_connectionStateProvider (StateProvider) [screen-local]\n_messagesProvider (StateProvider<List<ChatMessage>>) [screen-local]\n_sessionStepProvider (StateProvider<int>) [screen-local]\n_sessionExercisesProvider (Provider) [screen-local]\n_sessionResultsProvider (StateProvider) [screen-local]\n```\n\n### 12.4 Audio Player (live_audio_player.dart)\n\n```dart\nclass LiveAudioPlayer {\n  final AudioPlayer _player;\n  final List<int> _pcmBytes = [];\n  \n  appendBase64Chunk(String base64Chunk)  // Accumulates PCM from base64\n  playBufferedAudio()                    // Adds WAV header @24kHz, plays via audioplayers\n  stop() / clear() / dispose()\n  \n  static Uint8List _addWavHeader(Uint8List pcmBytes, {sampleRate: 24000})\n  // Builds 44-byte WAV header for the accumulated PCM bytes\n}\n```\n\n**Critical:** This player expects `message.data` (base64 from `inlineData` in JSON mode). It does NOT handle raw binary PCM frames from binary mode. The fork MUST add raw binary PCM → WAV conversion.\n\n---\n\n## 13. Complete Test Script Catalog (22 scripts, 2070 lines)\n\nAll scripts in `/tmp/test_*.js`. Common structure:\n```javascript\n// 1. Get OAuth2 token via gcloud\n// 2. Connect to wss://us-central1-aiplatform.googleapis.com/ws/...\n// 3. Bearer token + X-Goog-User-Project headers\n// 4. Send setup with model = projects/.../publishers/google/models/gemini-live-2.5-flash-native-audio\n// 5. Listen for messages (binary frames, detect JSON by first byte 0x7b)\n// 6. Timeout after 10–35s\n```\n\n### 13.1 Script Index\n\n| # | Script | Lines | Purpose | Key Finding |\n|---|--------|-------|---------|-------------|\n| 1 | `test_live_ws.js` | 123 | **First successful connection** | Bearer token + `us-central1` + full model path = ✅ setupComplete. Initial discovery of binary frames. |\n| 2 | `test_live_ws2.js` | — | Variant | — |\n| 3 | `test_live_ws3.js` | — | Variant | — |\n| 4 | `test_live_ws4.js` | — | Variant | — |\n| 5 | `test_live_ws5.js` | — | Check `responseModalities` at setup root | **Rejected** — should be in `generationConfig` |\n| 6 | `test_live_ws6.js` | — | Variant | — |\n| 7 | `test_live_ws7.js` | — | Variant | — |\n| 8 | `test_live_ws8.js` | — | Variant | — |\n| 9 | `test_raw.js` | 59 | Binary frame hex dump analysis | **ALL frames are binary (opcode 2)**. No length-prefix framing. First byte `0x7b` = JSON, rest = audio. |\n| 10 | `test_bincheck.js` | 69 | Minimal setup, detect small binary messages | Auto-response from system instruction → JSON mode (audio in `serverContent.modelTurn.parts[].inlineData`). |\n| 11 | `test_correct.js` | 142 | **Definitive test** — camelCase + `languageCodes` + binary routing | `outputAudioTranscription: { languageCodes: [\"en-US\"] }` caused **entire setup rejection**. `outputAudioTranscription: {}` works but silences JSON. |\n| 12 | `test_detailed.js` | 118 | Full 35s test with transcription tracking | Detailed timing. Setup with `outputAudioTranscription`, multiple prompts. Tracks all JSON messages. |\n| 13 | `test_transcription.js` | 86 | Transcription-focused test (snake_case keys) | Used snake_case `output_audio_transcription` — server accepted empty object. No text frames returned. |\n| 14 | `test_clean.js` | — | Clean minimal test | — |\n| 15 | `test_client_content.js` | — | `clientContent` send format | Confirmed `clientContent.turns[].parts[].text` + `turnComplete` triggers audio response |\n| 16 | `test_live_text.js` | — | Text-only input | — |\n| 17 | `test_live_input.js` | — | `realtimeInput` format | — |\n| 18 | `test_live_full.js` | — | Full session simulation | — |\n| 19 | `test_live_transcribe.js` | — | Transcription variant | — |\n| 20 | `test_official.js` | — | Official SDK test | — |\n| 21 | `test_grpc_decode.js` | — | gRPC-Web framing decode | Confirmed **NO gRPC-Web framing** — just raw binary |\n| 22 | `test_reenable.js` | — | Reconnection test | — |\n\n### 13.2 Critical Empirical Discoveries From Tests\n\n**Test 3 (test_raw.js) — Binary Frame Discovery:**\n```\n#1 BINARY (48B):\n  hex: 7b227365747570436f6d706c657465223a...  ← starts with 0x7b = '{'\n  asc: {\"setupComplete\":{...                   ← It's JSON!\n```\n\n**Test 4 (test_bincheck.js) — Two Response Modes:**\n1. You're patient, encouraging, and warm.\nYour patient is recovering from a stroke and has aphasia/apraxia.\n\n[Core Interaction Rules — 6 rules]\n[Session Structure — 3 phases: Check-in → Exercise → Cool-down]\n[Cueing Protocol — 4 levels of cues, escalate only when needed]\n[Scoring Guidelines — 0-100 scale, generous for speech therapy]\n[Widget Control — 10 function declarations for UI state changes]\n[Background Agent — Silent context manager runs in parallel]\n[Session Memory Format — Structured JSON for long-term memory]\n```\n\n### 15.2 11 Function Declarations (Full JSON Schema)\n\nEach function has JSON Schema for args. Key schemas:\n\n**`show_breathing_widget`**:\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"show_breathing_widget\",\n  \"description\": \"Display the 4-7-8 box breathing exercise with animated circle and haptic feedback\",\n  \"parameters\": {\n    \"type\": \"object\",\n    \"properties\": {\n      \"rounds\": { \"type\": \"integer\", \"description\": \"Number of breathing rounds (default 5)\" },\n      \"inhale\": { \"type\": \"integer\", \"description\": \"Inhale duration in seconds (default 4)\" },\n      \"hold\": { \"type\": \"integer\", \"description\": \"Hold duration in seconds (default 7)\" },\n      \"exhale\": { \"type\": \"integer\", \"description\": \"Exhale duration in seconds (default 8)\" }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**`show_naming_exercise`**:\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"show_naming_exercise\",\n  \"parameters\": {\n    \"type\": \"object\",\n    \"properties\": {\n      \"imageUrl\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n      \"targetWord\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n      \"language\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"enum\": [\"si\", \"ta\", \"en\"] },\n      \"difficulty\": { \"type\": \"integer\", \"minimum\": 1, \"maximum\": 5 }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**`provide_cue`**:\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"provide_cue\",\n  \"parameters\": {\n    \"type\": \"object\",\n    \"properties\": {\n      \"level\": { \"type\": \"integer\", \"enum\": [0, 1, 2, 3, 4] },\n      \"cueType\": {\n        \"type\": \"string\",\n        \"enum\": [\"verbal\", \"phonemic\", \"syllabic\", \"visual\"]\n      },\n      \"cueContent\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**`update_session_memory`**:\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"update_session_memory\",\n  \"parameters\": {\n    \"type\": \"object\",\n    \"properties\": {\n      \"patientName\": {},\n      \"mood\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"enum\": [\"happy\", \"frustrated\", \"tired\", \"neutral\", \"anxious\"] },\n      \"energyLevel\": { \"type\": \"integer\", \"minimum\": 1, \"maximum\": 10 },\n      \"struggleWords\": { \"type\": \"array\", \"items\": { \"type\": \"string\" } },\n      \"masteredWords\": { \"type\": \"array\", \"items\": { \"type\": \"string\" } },\n      \"notes\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### 15.3 Session Flow (3 Phases)\n\n```\nCHECK-IN PHASE (2-3 min):\n  1. Greeting + mood check\n  2. Review previous session highlights\n  3. Set today's goal\n\nEXERCISE PHASE (12-15 min):\n  4. Picture Naming (5-7 exercises)\n  5. Cueing ladder escalation (verbal → phonemic → syllabic → visual)\n  6. Scoring + encouragement\n\nCOOL-DOWN PHASE (3-5 min):\n  7. Review achievements\n  8. Breathing exercise (if needed)\n  9. Goodbye + set next session expectation\n```\n\n### 15.4 Background Agent Implementation\n\nTwo parallel agent streams:\n1. **Main agent** (Thilina) — visible voice interaction with patient\n2. **Background agent** — invisible, injects context via `realtimeInput.text`\n\n```javascript\n// Background agent injection (invisible to patient)\nsession.send(JSON.stringify({\n  realtimeInput: {\n    text: \"CONTEXT_UPDATE: Patient energy dropped from 7 to 4. \" +\n          \"Struggling with /r/ sounds. \" +\n          \"Suggest easier words and more encouragement.\"\n  }\n}));\n```\n\nThe background agent tracks:\n- Patient's emotional state over time (mood trend line)\n- Which words are mastered vs struggling (per-session memory)\n- Fatigue level (tracked via response time + accuracy trend)\n- Optimal time for breathing break (after 3+ wrong attempts)\n- Session pacing (adjust difficulty based on remaining time)\n\n### 15.5 Build Plan (from AURA Guide)\n\n```\nWEEK 1: Foundation\n  Day 1-2: Project scaffold + package fork\n  Day 3-4: Core WebSocket integration with binary frame handler\n  Day 5-7: Function declarations + widget routing\n\nWEEK 2: Voice Pipeline\n  Day 8-9: Microphone streaming (16kHz PCM) + audio playback (24kHz PCM)\n  Day 10-11: VAD tuning + turn management\n  Day 12-14: Transcription + evaluation loop\n\nWEEK 3: Therapy Content\n  Day 15-16: Cueing ladder implementation\n  Day 17-18: Scoring engine + database persistence\n  Day 19-21: Session memory + background agent\n\nWEEK 4: Polish\n  Day 22-23: Error handling + reconnection\n  Day 24-25: UI refinement + accessibility\n  Day 26-28: Testing + deployment\n```\n\n### 15.6 Key Design Decisions from AURA Guide\n\n1. **Single Widget Container**: All UI is controlled by the AI. The app has a single screen that renders whatever widget the model calls. No navigation bar during exercise.\n2. **Cueing Ladder**: 4 levels (0=no cue, 1=verbal, 2=phonemic, 3=syllabic, 4=visual). The model chooses the level, not the UI.\n3. **Emotional Regulation**: If the patient shows frustration (Level 3-4 struggle on MediaPipe face mesh), the model should: pause, validate feelings, offer breathing break.\n4. **MediaPipe Face Mesh**: Runs locally at 1fps. Detects 4 struggle levels via facial tension (brow furrow, lip tension, jaw clench).\n5. **Background Agent**: Runs as a parallel \"whisper\" stream — injects memory context, suggests actions, tracks session analytics. Invisible to the patient.\n6. **API Split**: `gemini-live-2.5-flash-native-audio` for voice (WebSocket), separate model for text scoring (REST).\n7. **Token Usage**: ~$0.07 per 20-min session. $300 credits ≈ 4,285 sessions. $2.40/month at 30 sessions.\n"
}

## Key Decisions
- **Use `gemini-live-2.5-flash-native-audio`** — confirmed working model from Google's own Agent Builder generated code and successful test
- **Use `us-central1` for WebSocket** location — Agent Builder backend rewrites `global` → `us-central1` for Live WS
- **Use OAuth2 Bearer token** in WebSocket headers (not API key) — `X-Goog-User-Project` header required
- **camelCase JSON field names** in wire format — per official docs and raw frame inspection of `setupComplete` response
- **No `responseModalities` in setup**: omitting it lets model default to audio output with text system instruction trigger
- **Binary frame protocol**: all messages are binary WebSocket frames — detect JSON vs audio by checking first byte (`{` = JSON)
- **Fork must serialize outgoing messages in camelCase**: either by editing `models.g.dart` (75+ key renames) OR by runtime key conversion in `sendMessage()` — **recommend both for correctness + safety net**
- **Incoming message classes already correct**: all 19 server-message classes use no `FieldRename` (default camelCase) + `createToJson: false` — no changes needed
- **Model split**: `gemini-live-2.5-flash-native-audio` for Live voice (WebSocket), `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview` for text scoring (HTTP REST)
- **No Vertex AI Express**: would require console signup; sticking with standard Vertex AI + OAuth2

## Critical Context
- **Vertex AI Live WebSocket works** ✅ — connection opens, audio streams, `clientContent.turns` message format confirmed
- **ALL server messages are binary frames** (opcode 2) — JSON detected by first byte `{` (0x7b), audio is everything else. No text frames, no length prefixes
- **JSON wire format = camelCase**: `setupComplete`, `serverContent`, `modelTurn`, `clientContent`, `turnComplete`, `inlineData`, `outputAudioTranscription`, `languageCodes` — confirmed by official docs and raw hex inspection
- **Audio format**: Output = 16-bit PCM, 24kHz, little-endian, mono. Input = 16-bit PCM, 16kHz, little-endian, mono. Raw binary, not WAV or mu-law
- **Two response modes**: (a) JSON mode — audio in `serverContent.modelTurn.parts[].inlineData` as base64 (auto-response to system instruction), (b) Binary mode — audio as separate binary frames (response to `clientContent`)
- **Dart package mismatch**: `gemini_live` v2026.6.6 uses 44 classes with `FieldRename.snake` (snake_case JSON), expects text frames, throws on `languageCodes`. All three must change for Vertex AI. **5 outgoing classes already use camelCase** (`Part`, `Blob`, `LiveClientMessage`, `ExecutableCode`, `CodeExecutionResult`). **19 incoming classes already camelCase** — no changes needed
- **Transcription config uncertain**: `{}` works but silences `serverContent`. `languageCodes: ["en-US"]` rejected the entire setup. Official docs say "no fields." SDK defines `List<String>? languageCodes` but `.g.dart` renders as `'language_codes'`. May need different API version or field name
- **Correct model**: `gemini-live-2.5-flash-native-audio` — audio-native (TEXT modality rejected)
- **Correct endpoint**: `wss://us-central1-aiplatform.googleapis.com/ws/google.cloud.aiplatform.v1beta1.LlmBidiService/BidiGenerateContent`
- **Full model path**: `projects/biz-studio-1779528000/locations/us-central1/publishers/google/models/gemini-live-2.5-flash-native-audio`
- **Auth**: Bearer token from `gcloud auth application-default print-access-token` + `X-Goog-User-Project` header
- **Owner account**: `mamanniggajay@gmail.com` — ADC re-authed with `set-quota-project biz-studio-1779528000`
- **$300 GCP credits**: ~$0.07 per 20-min Live session, ~$2.40/month for 30 sessions
- **AURA guide** at `/home/jay/Downloads/AURA_Vertex_AI_Implementation_Guide.md`: full system prompt, 11 function declarations with JSON schemas, 3-phase session flow, background agent design, build plan
- **22 test scripts** in `/tmp/test_*.js` (2070 total lines) — complete catalog of all protocol experimentation. Key scripts: `test_live_ws.js` (first success), `test_raw.js` (binary frame hex dump), `test_bincheck.js` (two response modes), `test_correct.js` (definitive camelCase + languageCodes test), `test_transcription.js` (snake_case transcription test), `test_detailed.js` (full 35s session with timing)
- **Complete knowledge base**: `/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/docs/codebase-knowledge-reference.md` (2300+ lines) — permanent reference for ALL findings: project structure, package internals, protocol, test results, fork plan, AURA architecture, DB schema, domain models, screen map, test catalog, key mapping table

# Skill: planning-research-engine

---
id: planning-research-engine
name: Planning & Research Engine
version: 1.0.0
author: OpenCode

tags: [planning, research, architecture, requirements, validation]
---

# Planning & Research Engine

## Purpose
Transform vague ideas into production-ready, research-backed, dependency-aware implementation plans with validation gates. Never allow jumping from idea to code without completing all 15 phases.

## Core Philosophy
> "Most AI systems fail because they jump directly into coding. This skill explicitly prevents that."

## The 15-Phase Workflow

### Phase 1 — Intent Discovery
**Goal:** Understand what the user truly wants, not what they said.

**Questions to answer:**
- What does the user want? (surface request)
- Why do they want it? (underlying motivation)
- Who will use it? (target audience)
- What problem does it solve? (pain point)
- How will success be measured? (KPIs)
- What is the business model? (revenue, cost, sustainability)
- What is the timeline? (urgency, milestones)

**Output:** `docs/intent-report.md`

**Validation Gate:** If any of the 7 questions cannot be answered, STOP and ask the user.

---

### Phase 2 — Gap Analysis
**Goal:** Become aggressively skeptical. Find everything missing.

**Checklist:**
- [ ] Target geography / locale / language?
- [ ] Authentication method?
- [ ] Payment/subscription model?
- [ ] Data storage requirements?
- [ ] Real-time vs async?
- [ ] Mobile vs desktop vs both?
- [ ] Offline support needed?
- [ ] Third-party integrations?
- [ ] Compliance requirements? (GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2)
- [ ] Accessibility requirements? (WCAG level)
- [ ] Performance expectations? (load time, concurrency)
- [ ] Security requirements? (encryption, MFA)
- [ ] Scalability expectations? (users, data volume)
- [ ] AI/ML components? (model, provider, cost)

**Output:** `docs/gap-analysis-report.md`

**Validation Gate:** If >3 gaps found, present to user and resolve before proceeding.

---

### Phase 3 — Contradiction Detection
**Goal:** Find logical impossibilities in requirements.

**Common contradictions:**
- "Real-time collaboration" + "no backend" = CONFLICT
- "Offline-first" + "live sync" = CONFLICT
- "Zero cost" + "AI-powered" = CONFLICT
- "Self-hosted" + "serverless" = CONFLICT
- "No login" + "personalized" = CONFLICT

**Output:** `docs/conflict-report.md`

**Validation Gate:** If unresolved conflicts exist, STOP. Do not proceed to architecture.

---

### Phase 4 — Research Phase
**Goal:** Gather evidence before making decisions.

**Research Categories:**

**4A. Technical Research**
- Frameworks (latest stable versions, LTS status)
- Libraries (bundle size, maintenance, community)
- APIs (rate limits, pricing, reliability)
- Databases (CAP theorem fit, scaling model)
- Infrastructure (cost, complexity, vendor lock-in)
- Security (OWASP, best practices, known vulnerabilities)

**4B. Market Research**
- Competitors (feature matrix, pricing, weaknesses)
- User reviews (pain points, feature requests)
- Business models (subscription, freemium, enterprise)

**4C. UX Research**
- Design patterns (industry standard, emerging)
- Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA minimum)
- Interaction patterns (micro-interactions, feedback loops)
- Device-specific considerations

**4D. Engineering Research**
- Scalability patterns (horizontal vs vertical)
- Performance (caching strategies, CDN, edge)
- Architecture (monolith vs microservices vs modular)
- Testing (unit, integration, e2e, visual regression)

**Research Output Format:**
```
| Topic | Evidence | Source | Reasoning | Recommendation | Tradeoffs |
```

**Output:** `docs/research-report.md`

**Validation Gate:** If research reveals a blocker (e.g., API deprecated, framework abandoned), flag immediately.

---

### Phase 5 — Requirements Documentation
**Goal:** Separate what from how.

**5A. Functional Requirements (FR)**
- What the system DOES
- User stories format: "As a [role], I want [action], so that [benefit]"
- Acceptance criteria for each FR

**5B. Non-Functional Requirements (NFR)**
- How the system BEHAVES
- Performance: load time < 2s, TTI < 3.5s
- Reliability: 99.9% uptime, graceful degradation
- Security: OWASP Top 10 mitigation, encryption at rest + in transit
- Scalability: handle 10x current load without redesign
- Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA
- Compatibility: target browsers, devices, OS versions

**Output:** `docs/requirements-document.md`

**Validation Gate:** Every FR must have acceptance criteria. Every NFR must be measurable.

---

### Phase 6 — Product Definition
**Goal:** Define the product identity.

**Document:**
- Vision (1 sentence, inspirational)
- Mission (1 sentence, actionable)
- Purpose (why this exists)
- Target Audience (demographics, psychographics, tech-savviness)
- User Personas (3-5 primary personas with goals, pain points, scenarios)
- User Journey (end-to-end flow, touchpoints, emotions)
- KPIs (5-7 metrics, with measurement method)
- Success Metrics (quantitative + qualitative)

**Output:** `docs/product-definition.md`

---

### Phase 7 — UX Planning
**Goal:** Design the experience before the interface.

**Flow:**
1. User Goals -> 2. User Tasks -> 3. User Flows -> 4. Wireframes -> 5. Information Architecture -> 6. UI Design

**Document:**
- Navigation structure (primary, secondary, footer, mobile hamburger)
- User flows (happy path, edge cases, error paths)
- Page/component inventory
- Content hierarchy
- Interaction patterns (hover, click, swipe, keyboard)
- Feedback loops (loading, success, error, empty states)
- Pain points and mitigation

**Output:** `docs/ux-plan.md`

**Validation Gate:** No UI component should be designed without a corresponding user task.

---

### Phase 8 — System Architecture
**Goal:** Design the system structure.

**8A. Frontend Architecture**
- Framework (React, Vue, Svelte, etc. with version)
- State management (Redux, Zustand, Context, etc.)
- Routing (file-based, config-based, nested)
- Form handling (React Hook Form, Formik, native)
- Component architecture (atomic design, compound components)
- Styling approach (CSS-in-JS, Tailwind, CSS modules, single CSS file)
- Animation strategy (CSS transitions, Framer Motion, GSAP)

**8B. Backend Architecture**
- Service pattern (MVC, Clean Architecture, Hexagonal)
- API design (REST, GraphQL, tRPC, gRPC)
- Authentication (JWT, OAuth2, Session, SSO)
- Authorization (RBAC, ABAC, ACL)
- Business logic layer
- Middleware (logging, rate limiting, CORS, compression)

**8C. Database Architecture**
- Database type (SQL, NoSQL, graph, time-series)
- Schema design (ER diagram, normalization level)
- Indexes (query patterns, composite indexes)
- Constraints (foreign keys, unique, check)
- Migration strategy
- Backup/recovery plan

**8D. Infrastructure Architecture**
- Hosting (VPS, serverless, edge, self-hosted)
- CDN (Cloudflare, AWS CloudFront, Vercel Edge)
- Storage (S3, local, distributed)
- Monitoring (uptime, performance, errors, analytics)
- CI/CD (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, manual)
- Environment strategy (dev, staging, prod, preview)

**Output:** `docs/architecture-document.md`

**Validation Gate:** Architecture must address all NFRs from Phase 5.

---

### Phase 9 — Device Analysis
**Goal:** Determine all target devices and their implications.

**Device Matrix:**
| Device | Priority | Screen Size | Input Method | Constraints | Special Considerations |
|--------|----------|-------------|--------------|-------------|------------------------|
| Desktop | | | | | |
| Tablet | | | | | |
| Mobile | | | | | |
| Web | | | | | |
| PWA | | | | | |
| Android Native | | | | | |
| iOS Native | | | | | |
| Kiosk | | | | | |
| Smart TV | | | | | |
| Wearable | | | | | |

**Output:** `docs/device-analysis.md`

**Validation Gate:** If mobile is target, mobile-first design is mandatory. If PWA, offline strategy is mandatory.

---

### Phase 10 — Dependency Mapping
**Goal:** Create implementation order based on actual dependencies.

**Dependency Tree Format:**
```
Database Schema
 \ Authentication Service
      \ User API
           \ User Profile UI
                \ Dashboard UI
                     \ Analytics
```

**Rules:**
- A parent must be implemented before its children
- Shared dependencies (Database, Auth) are P0
- Leaf nodes (UI features) are P1/P2
- Circular dependencies must be resolved (break with interfaces/mocks)

**Output:** `docs/dependency-map.md`

---

### Phase 11 — Priority Matrix
**Goal:** Categorize everything by must/should/could/won't.

**MoSCoW Method:**
- **M - Must Have:** System cannot function without this. (P0)
- **S - Should Have:** Important but system works without. (P1)
- **C - Could Have:** Nice to have, deliver if time permits. (P2)
- **W - Won't Have:** Explicitly excluded (document why).

**Output:** `docs/priority-matrix.md`

**Validation Gate:** Must Haves must be <40% of total scope. If >40%, scope is too large for MVP.

---

### Phase 12 — Implementation Roadmap
**Goal:** Convert architecture into time-bound milestones.

**Milestone Format:**
```
## Milestone N: [Name]
**Duration:** X days/weeks
**Goal:** [One sentence]
**Deliverables:**
- [ ] Feature A
- [ ] Feature B
**Success Criteria:**
- [ ] Criterion 1
- [ ] Criterion 2
**Dependencies:** Milestone N-1
**Risks:** [What could go wrong]
**Rollback Plan:** [How to undo if failed]
```

**Output:** `docs/implementation-roadmap.md`

---

### Phase 13 — Progress Tracking System
**Goal:** Every task must be trackable.

**Task Format:**
```
## Task: [Name]
**ID:** T###
**Status:** [Not Started | In Progress | Blocked | Review | Complete]
**Priority:** [P0 | P1 | P2]
**Milestone:** [M#]
**Dependencies:** [T###, T###]
**Estimated Effort:** [hours/days/story points]
**Actual Effort:** [hours/days/story points]
**Owner:** [AI agent / human / external]
**Completion %:** [0-100]
**Blockers:** [None | T### | external factor]
**Validation:** [What confirms this is done correctly]
**Files Modified:** [list]
**Tests Required:** [list]
```

**Output:** `docs/progress-tracker.md` (live document, updated after every task)

---

### Phase 14 — Validation Layer
**Goal:** Block implementation until ready.

**Pre-Implementation Checklist:**
- [ ] Intent Report complete and approved
- [ ] Gap Analysis resolved (<3 open gaps)
- [ ] Conflict Report resolved (0 open conflicts)
- [ ] Research Report complete (all categories)
- [ ] Requirements Document complete (FR + NFR)
- [ ] Product Definition complete
- [ ] UX Plan complete (flows + wireframes)
- [ ] Architecture Document complete (all 4 layers)
- [ ] Device Analysis complete
- [ ] Dependency Map complete (no cycles)
- [ ] Priority Matrix complete (Must Haves <40%)
- [ ] Implementation Roadmap complete (all milestones)
- [ ] Progress Tracker initialized

**If ANY checkbox is unchecked:**
> "Implementation blocked. [Phase Name] is incomplete. Completing now..."

**Output:** `docs/validation-gate-report.md`

---

### Phase 15 — Coding Order (Hierarchical Implementation)
**Goal:** Define the exact order of implementation to minimize rework.

**The Sacred Order:**

```
1.  Requirements          (Phase 5 output)
2.  Architecture          (Phase 8 output)
3.  Database Design       (Schema, migrations, seed data)
4.  Backend Foundation    (Project structure, middleware, config)
5.  Authentication        (Login, register, sessions, JWT)
6.  API Layer             (Endpoints, validation, docs)
7.  Core Business Logic   (The actual value proposition)
8.  Frontend Foundation   (Project structure, routing, state)
9.  UI Components         (Design system, atoms, molecules)
10. Feature Integration   (Wire frontend to backend)
11. Testing               (Unit, integration, e2e, visual)
12. Optimization          (Performance, bundle size, caching)
13. Security Review       (Audit, penetration testing mindset)
14. Deployment            (CI/CD, hosting, domain, SSL)
15. Monitoring            (Errors, performance, analytics, alerts)
```

**NEVER:**
- Design UI before backend API is defined
- Build UI before business logic is implemented
- Deploy before testing is complete
- Skip security review

**Output:** `docs/coding-order.md`

---

## Execution Rules

### Rule 1: No Code Before Phase 14
If the user asks to "just start coding," respond:
> "I cannot implement before validation. Let me complete the planning phases first. This prevents the exact problem you're trying to avoid: scattered files, lost context, and rework."

### Rule 2: Research Before Decisions
Every technology choice must cite:
- Source (official docs, benchmark, case study)
- Reasoning (why this over alternatives)
- Tradeoffs (what you lose by choosing this)

### Rule 3: Document Everything
Every phase produces a markdown file in `docs/`. No phase is complete until its file is written.

### Rule 4: Validate After Every Phase
Before proceeding to N+1, validate phase N against:
- User intent (does this still serve the original goal?)
- Consistency (does this contradict any previous phase?)
- Completeness (are there obvious gaps?)

### Rule 5: Progress is Public
After every task, update `docs/progress-tracker.md` with:
- What was done
- What changed
- What's next
- Blockers

---

## Integration with OpenCode Tools

### Using Pipeline
```bash
# After Phase 1 (Intent), init project
pipeline init <project> -d "<intent summary>"

# After each milestone
pipeline promote <project>
pipeline journal-entry <project> "Milestone N complete: <summary>"

# If blocked
pipeline journal-entry <project> "BLOCKED: <reason>"
```

### Using Memory
```bash
# After each phase, persist learnings
memory remember "<key finding>" --source planning --tags "research,<topic>"

# After technology decision
memory remember "Chose <tech> over <alt> because <reason>" --source planning --tags "decision,<tech>"
```

### Using Graph
```bash
# After architecture phase, build graph
graph build <project> --no-viz

# Query before implementing any feature
graph query "<feature> dependencies" -g <project>/graphify-out/graph.json
```

### Using Scaffold
```bash
# Create project with standard structure
scaffold create <name> --type <type>

# Before modifying any file, backup
scaffold backup <file>  # (if implemented)
```

---

| 2 | Gap Analysis | `docs/gap-analysis-report.md` |
| 3 | Conflict Report | `docs/conflict-report.md` |
| 4 | Research Report | `docs/research-report.md` |
| 5 | Requirements | `docs/requirements-document.md` |

| 11 | Priority Matrix | `docs/priority-matrix.md` |
| 12 | Roadmap | `docs/implementation-roadmap.md` |
| 13 | Progress Tracker | `docs/progress-tracker.md` (live) |
| 14 | Validation Gate | `docs/validation-gate-report.md` |
| 15 | Coding Order | `docs/coding-order.md` |

## Emergency Override
If the user explicitly demands to skip planning with "I understand the risks, code now":
1. Warn once: "Skipping planning increases risk of rework, data loss, and context loss."
2. If confirmed: Proceed but create `docs/EMERGENCY_SKIP.md` documenting:
   - What was skipped
   - Why
   - Risk acceptance
   - Mitigation plan
3. Run `pipeline journal-entry <project> "EMERGENCY: Planning phases skipped by user request"`

46:   /// - Setting custom timeouts or headers.
47:   ///
48:   /// If not provided, a default `http.Client` will be created internally.
49:   final http.Client? httpClient;
50: 
51:   /// The internal low-level client responsible for making API requests.
52:   ///
53:   /// This is marked as `late` because it is initialized in the constructor.
54:   late final ApiClient _apiClient;
55: 
56:   /// Provides access to the live, streaming services of the API.
57:   ///
58:   /// This is marked as `late` because it is initialized in the constructor.
59:   late final LiveService live;
60: 
61:   /// Creates a new instance of the [GoogleGenAI] client.
62:   ///
63:   /// [apiKey] is your Google AI API key, which is required for all requests.
64:   /// [httpClient] is an optional client to use for making HTTP requests.
65:   GoogleGenAI({
66:     required this.apiKey,
67:     this.httpClient,
68:     this.apiVersion = 'v1beta',
69:   }) {
70:     // Initialize the internal API client with the provided credentials and HTTP client.
71:     _apiClient = ApiClient(apiKey: apiKey, httpClient: httpClient);
72: 
73:     // Initialize the LiveService, which handles real-time interactions.
74:     live = LiveService(apiKey: apiKey, apiVersion: apiVersion);
75:   }
76: 
77:   /// Releases any resources held by the client.
78:   ///
79:   /// It is important to call this method when the [GoogleGenAI] instance is no
80:   /// longer needed to prevent resource leaks. This is especially crucial if an
81:   /// internal `httpClient` was created, as this method ensures it is properly closed.
82:   void close() {
83:     _apiClient.close();
84:   }
85: }

84:   final String apiKey;
85:   final String apiVersion;
86:   static const _functionResponseRequiresId =
87:       'FunctionResponse request must have an `id` field from the response of a ToolCall.functionCalls in Gemini Live.';
88: 
89:   static UnsupportedError _unsupportedAudioTranscriptionLanguageCodesError() {

109:   LiveService({
110:     required this.apiKey,
111:     this.apiVersion = 'v1beta',
112:     this.logger,
113:     WebSocketConnector? connector,
114:     Duration setupTimeout = const Duration(seconds: 10),

121:   static String dartVersion() {
122:     return runtime_info.dartVersion();
123:   }

125:   static GenerationConfig _normalizeGenerationConfig(GenerationConfig? config) {
126:     final responseModalities = config?.responseModalities;
127:     if (responseModalities != null && responseModalities.isNotEmpty) {
128:       return config!;
129:     }
130: 
131:     return GenerationConfig(
132:       temperature: config?.temperature,
133:       topK: config?.topK,
134:       topP: config?.topP,
135:       maxOutputTokens: config?.maxOutputTokens,
136:       responseModalities: const [Modality.AUDIO],
137:       mediaResolution: config?.mediaResolution,
138:       seed: config?.seed,
139:       speechConfig: config?.speechConfig,
140:       thinkingConfig: config?.thinkingConfig,
141:       enableAffectiveDialog: config?.enableAffectiveDialog,
142:       streamTranslationConfig: config?.streamTranslationConfig,
143:     );
144:   }
145: 
146:   static LiveClientMessage buildSetupMessage(LiveConnectParameters params) {
147:     if (params.sessionResumption?.transparent == true) {
148:       throw UnsupportedError(
149:         'transparent parameter is not supported in Gemini API.',
150:       );
151:     }
152: 
153:     if (params.explicitVadSignal != null) {
154:       throw UnsupportedError(
155:         'explicitVadSignal parameter is not supported in Gemini API.',
156:       );
157:     }
158: 
159:     if (params.inputAudioTranscription?.languageCodes != null) {
160:       throw _unsupportedAudioTranscriptionLanguageCodesError();
161:     }
162: 
163:     if (params.outputAudioTranscription?.languageCodes != null) {
164:       throw _unsupportedAudioTranscriptionLanguageCodesError();
165:     }
166: 
167:     if ((params.safetySettings ?? const <SafetySetting>[]).any(
168:       (setting) => setting.method != null,
169:     )) {
170:       throw _unsupportedSafetyMethodError();
171:     }
172: 
173:     final modelName = params.model.startsWith('models/')
174:         ? params.model
175:         : 'models/${params.model}';
176: 
177:     return LiveClientMessage(
178:       setup: LiveClientSetup(
179:         model: modelName,
180:         generationConfig: _normalizeGenerationConfig(params.config),
181:         systemInstruction: params.systemInstruction,
182:         tools: params.tools,
183:         realtimeInputConfig: params.realtimeInputConfig,
184:         sessionResumption: params.sessionResumption,
185:         contextWindowCompression: params.contextWindowCompression,
186:         inputAudioTranscription: params.inputAudioTranscription,
187:         outputAudioTranscription: params.outputAudioTranscription,
188:         proactivity: params.proactivity,
189:         avatarConfig: params.avatarConfig,
190:         safetySettings: params.safetySettings,
191:       ),
192:     );
193:   }
194: 
195:   static List<FunctionResponse> validateFunctionResponses(
196:     List<FunctionResponse> functionResponses,
197:   ) {
198:     if (functionResponses.isEmpty) {
199:       throw ArgumentError('functionResponses is required.');
200:     }
201: 
202:     for (final functionResponse in functionResponses) {
203:       if (functionResponse.name == null || functionResponse.name!.isEmpty) {
204:         throw ArgumentError('Each function response must include a name.');
205:       }
206:       if (functionResponse.response == null) {
207:         throw ArgumentError('Each function response must include a response.');
208:       }
209:       if (functionResponse.id == null || functionResponse.id!.isEmpty) {
210:         throw ArgumentError(_functionResponseRequiresId);
211:       }
212:     }
213: 
214:     return functionResponses;
215:   }

218:     Blob blob, {
219:     required String expectedPrefix,
220:   }) {
221:     if (!blob.mimeType.startsWith(expectedPrefix)) {
222:       throw ArgumentError('Unsupported mime type: ${blob.mimeType}');
223:     }
224:   }
225: 
226:   /// Handles incoming WebSocket data and converts it to LiveServerMessage
227:   void _handleWebSocketData(dynamic data, LiveCallbacks callbacks) {
228:     String jsonData;
229:     if (data is String) {
230:       jsonData = data;
231:     } else if (data is List<int>) {
232:       jsonData = utf8.decode(data);
233:     } else {
234:       callbacks.onError?.call(
235:         Exception(
236:           'Received unexpected data type from WebSocket: ${data.runtimeType}',
237:         ),
238:         StackTrace.current,
239:       );
240:       return;
241:     }
242: 
243:     try {
244:       final json = jsonDecode(jsonData);
245:       logger?.call('📥 Received JSON: $jsonData');
246:       final message = LiveServerMessage.fromJson(json);
247:       callbacks.onMessage?.call(message);
248:     } catch (e, st) {
249:       callbacks.onError?.call(e, st);
250:     }
251:   }
252: 
253:   void handleWebSocketDataForTesting(dynamic data, LiveCallbacks callbacks) {
254:     _handleWebSocketData(data, callbacks);
255:   }
256: 
257:   /// Establishes a WebSocket connection to the Live API
258:   Future<LiveSession> connect(LiveConnectParameters params) async {
259:     final usesEphemeralToken = apiKey.startsWith('auth_tokens/');
260:     if (usesEphemeralToken && apiVersion != 'v1alpha') {
261:       logger?.call(
262:         '⚠️ Warning: Ephemeral token support is only available on v1alpha. Current apiVersion: $apiVersion',
263:       );
264:     }
265: 
266:     final method = usesEphemeralToken
267:         ? 'BidiGenerateContentConstrained'
268:         : 'BidiGenerateContent';
269:     final keyName = usesEphemeralToken ? 'access_token' : 'key';
270: 
271:     final websocketUri = Uri(
272:       scheme: 'wss',
273:       host: 'generativelanguage.googleapis.com',
274:       path:
275:           '/ws/google.ai.generativelanguage.$apiVersion.GenerativeService.$method',
276:       queryParameters: {keyName: apiKey},
277:     );
278: 
279:     final userAgent =
280:         'google-genai-sdk/$_sdkVersion dart/${_dartVersionProvider()}';
281: 
282:     logger?.call('🔌 Connecting to WebSocket at $websocketUri');
283: 
284:     try {
285:       final headers = {
286:         'Content-Type': 'application/json',
287:         'x-goog-api-key': apiKey,
288:         'x-goog-api-client': userAgent,
289:         'user-agent': userAgent,
290:       };
291:       final channel = await _connector(websocketUri, headers);
292:       final session = LiveSession._(channel, logger: logger);
293:       final setupCompleter = Completer<void>();
294: 
295:       StreamSubscription? streamSubscription;
296:       streamSubscription = channel.stream.listen(
297:         (data) {
298:           final jsonData = data is String
299:               ? data
300:               : utf8.decode(data as List<int>);
301:           logger?.call('📥 Received: $jsonData');
302: 
303:           if (!setupCompleter.isCompleted) {
304:             try {
305:               setupCompleter.complete();
306:             } catch (e) {
307:               // Ignore parsing errors during setup
308:             }
309:           }
310:           _handleWebSocketData(data, params.callbacks);
311:         },
312:         onError: (error, stackTrace) {
313:           if (!setupCompleter.isCompleted) {
314:             setupCompleter.completeError(error, stackTrace);
315:           }
316:           params.callbacks.onError?.call(error, stackTrace);
317:         },
318:         onDone: () {
319:           params.callbacks.onClose?.call(
320:             channel.closeCode,
321:             channel.closeReason,
322:           );
323:           streamSubscription?.cancel();
324:         },
325:         cancelOnError: true,
326:       );
327: 
328:       params.callbacks.onOpen?.call();
329:       final setupMessage = LiveService.buildSetupMessage(params);
330:       session.sendMessage(setupMessage);
331: 
332:       await setupCompleter.future.timeout(
333:         _setupTimeout,
334:         onTimeout: () {
335:           throw TimeoutException(
336:             'WebSocket setup timed out after $_setupTimeout.',
337:           );
338:         },
339:       );
340: 
341:       return session;
342:     } catch (e) {
343:       logger?.call("Failed to connect or setup WebSocket: $e");
344:       rethrow;
345:     }
346:   }
347: }
348: 
349: /// Exception for timeout errors
350: class TimeoutException implements Exception {
351:   final String message;
352:   TimeoutException(this.message);
353:   @override
354:   String toString() => "TimeoutException: $message";
355: }
356: 
357: // ============================================================================
358: // Live Session
359: // ============================================================================
360: 
361: /// Represents an active Live API session
362: class LiveSession {
363:   final WebSocketChannel _channel;
364:   final void Function(String)? _logger;
365: 
366:   LiveSession._(this._channel, {void Function(String)? logger})
367:     : _logger = logger;
368: 
369:   factory LiveSession.forTesting(WebSocketChannel channel) =>
370:       LiveSession._(channel);
371: 
372:   /// Sends a message to the server
373:   void sendMessage(LiveClientMessage message) {
374:     if (_channel.closeCode != null) {
375:       _logger?.call(
376:         '⚠️ Warning: Attempted to send a message on a closed WebSocket channel.',
377:       );
378:       return;
379:     }
380:     final jsonString = jsonEncode(message.toJson());
381:     _logger?.call('📤 Sending: $jsonString');
382:     _channel.sink.add(jsonString);
383:   }
384: 
385:   /// Sends text content to the server
386:   void sendText(String text) {
387:     final message = LiveClientMessage(
388:       clientContent: LiveClientContent(
389:         turns: [
390:           Content(parts: [Part(text: text)]),
391:         ],
392:         turnComplete: true,
393:       ),
394:     );
395:     sendMessage(message);
396:   }
397: 
398:   /// Sends audio data to the server
399:   void sendAudio(List<int> audioBytes) {
400:     final base64Audio = base64Encode(audioBytes);
401:     final message = LiveClientMessage(
402:       realtimeInput: LiveClientRealtimeInput(
403:         audio: Blob(mimeType: 'audio/pcm', data: base64Audio),
404:       ),
405:     );
406:     sendMessage(message);
407:   }
408: 
409:   /// Sends video data to the server
410:   void sendVideo(List<int> videoBytes, {String mimeType = 'image/jpeg'}) {
411:     final base64Video = base64Encode(videoBytes);
412:     LiveService.validateRealtimeBlob(
413:       Blob(mimeType: mimeType, data: base64Video),
414:       expectedPrefix: 'image/',
415:     );
416:     final message = LiveClientMessage(
417:       realtimeInput: LiveClientRealtimeInput(
418:         video: Blob(mimeType: mimeType, data: base64Video),
419:       ),
420:     );
421:     sendMessage(message);
422:   }
423: 
424:   /// Sends client content with optional turns and turn completion flag
425:   void sendClientContent({List<Content>? turns, bool turnComplete = true}) {
426:     final message = LiveClientMessage(
427:       clientContent: LiveClientContent(
428:         turns: turns,
429:         turnComplete: turnComplete,
430:       ),
431:     );
432:     sendMessage(message);
433:   }
434: 
435:   /// Sends realtime input with various media types
436:   void sendRealtimeInput({
437:     List<Blob>? mediaChunks,
438:     Blob? audio,
439:     Blob? video,
440:     String? text,
441:     bool? audioStreamEnd,
442:     bool? activityStart,
443:     bool? activityEnd,
444:   }) {
445:     if (audio != null) {
446:       LiveService.validateRealtimeBlob(audio, expectedPrefix: 'audio/');
447:     }
448:     if (video != null) {
449:       LiveService.validateRealtimeBlob(video, expectedPrefix: 'image/');
450:     }
451: 
452:     LiveClientRealtimeInput? realtimeInput;
453: 
454:     if (mediaChunks != null ||
455:         audio != null ||
456:         video != null ||
457:         text != null ||
458:         audioStreamEnd != null ||
459:         activityStart != null ||
460:         activityEnd != null) {
461:       realtimeInput = LiveClientRealtimeInput(
462:         mediaChunks: mediaChunks,
463:         audio: audio,
464:         video: video,
465:         text: text,
466:         audioStreamEnd: audioStreamEnd,
467:         activityStart: activityStart == true ? ActivityStart() : null,
468:         activityEnd: activityEnd == true ? ActivityEnd() : null,
469:       );
470:     }
471: 
472:     if (realtimeInput != null) {
473:       final message = LiveClientMessage(realtimeInput: realtimeInput);
474:       sendMessage(message);
475:     }
476:   }
477: 
478:   /// Sends media chunks for realtime input
479:   void sendMediaChunks(List<Blob> mediaChunks) {
480:     final message = LiveClientMessage(
481:       realtimeInput: LiveClientRealtimeInput(mediaChunks: mediaChunks),
482:     );
483:     sendMessage(message);
484:   }
485: 
486:   /// Sends a signal indicating the end of audio stream
487:   void sendAudioStreamEnd() {
488:     final message = LiveClientMessage(
489:       realtimeInput: LiveClientRealtimeInput(audioStreamEnd: true),
490:     );
491:     sendMessage(message);
492:   }
493: 
494:   /// Sends realtime text input
495:   void sendRealtimeText(String text) {
496:     final message = LiveClientMessage(
497:       realtimeInput: LiveClientRealtimeInput(text: text),
498:     );
499:     sendMessage(message);
500:   }
501: 
502:   /// Marks the start of user activity (when automatic VAD is disabled)
503:   void sendActivityStart() {
504:     final message = LiveClientMessage(
505:       realtimeInput: LiveClientRealtimeInput(activityStart: ActivityStart()),
506:     );
507:     sendMessage(message);
508:   }
509: 
510:   /// Marks the end of user activity (when automatic VAD is disabled)
511:   void sendActivityEnd() {
512:     final message = LiveClientMessage(
513:       realtimeInput: LiveClientRealtimeInput(activityEnd: ActivityEnd()),
514:     );
515:     sendMessage(message);
516:   }
517: 
518:   /// Sends a tool response to the server
519:   void sendToolResponse({required List<FunctionResponse> functionResponses}) {
520:     final validatedResponses = LiveService.validateFunctionResponses(
521:       functionResponses,
522:     );
523:     final message = LiveClientMessage(
524:       toolResponse: LiveClientToolResponse(
525:         functionResponses: validatedResponses,
526:       ),
527:     );
528:     sendMessage(message);
529:   }
530: 
531:   /// Sends a single function response
532:   void sendFunctionResponse({
533:     required String id,
534:     required String name,
535:     required Map<String, dynamic> response,
536:   }) {
537:     sendToolResponse(
538:       functionResponses: [
539:         FunctionResponse(id: id, name: name, response: response),
540:       ],
541:     );

546:     await _channel.sink.close();
547:   }
548:

44:   HARM_CATEGORY_IMAGE_SEXUALLY_EXPLICIT,
45:   @JsonValue('HARM_CATEGORY_JAILBREAK')
46:   HARM_CATEGORY_JAILBREAK,
47: }
48: 
49: /// Safety blocking methods.
50: @JsonEnum(alwaysCreate: true)
51: enum HarmBlockMethod {
52:   @JsonValue('HARM_BLOCK_METHOD_UNSPECIFIED')
53:   HARM_BLOCK_METHOD_UNSPECIFIED,
54:   @JsonValue('SEVERITY')
55:   SEVERITY,
56:   @JsonValue('PROBABILITY')
57:   PROBABILITY,
58: }
59: 
60: /// Safety thresholds used to block unsafe content.
61: @JsonEnum(alwaysCreate: true)
62: enum HarmBlockThreshold {
63:   @JsonValue('HARM_BLOCK_THRESHOLD_UNSPECIFIED')
64:   HARM_BLOCK_THRESHOLD_UNSPECIFIED,
65:   @JsonValue('BLOCK_LOW_AND_ABOVE')
66:   BLOCK_LOW_AND_ABOVE,
67:   @JsonValue('BLOCK_MEDIUM_AND_ABOVE')
68:   BLOCK_MEDIUM_AND_ABOVE,
69:   @JsonValue('BLOCK_ONLY_HIGH')
70:   BLOCK_ONLY_HIGH,
71:   @JsonValue('BLOCK_NONE')
72:   BLOCK_NONE,
73:   @JsonValue('OFF')
74:   OFF,
75: }
76: 
77: /// Modalities that a request or response can contain.
78: @JsonEnum(alwaysCreate: true)
79: enum Modality {
80:   @JsonValue('MODALITY_UNSPECIFIED')
81:   MODALITY_UNSPECIFIED,
82:   @JsonValue('TEXT')
83:   TEXT,
84:   @JsonValue('IMAGE')
85:   IMAGE,
86:   @JsonValue('AUDIO')
87:   AUDIO,
88:   @JsonValue('VIDEO')
89:   VIDEO,
90: }
91: 
92: /// Media resolution presets for multimodal responses.
93: @JsonEnum(alwaysCreate: true)
94: enum MediaResolution {
95:   @JsonValue('MEDIA_RESOLUTION_UNSPECIFIED')
96:   MEDIA_RESOLUTION_UNSPECIFIED,
97:   @JsonValue('MEDIA_RESOLUTION_LOW')
98:   MEDIA_RESOLUTION_LOW,
99:   @JsonValue('MEDIA_RESOLUTION_MEDIUM')
100:   MEDIA_RESOLUTION_MEDIUM,
101:   @JsonValue('MEDIA_RESOLUTION_HIGH')
102:   MEDIA_RESOLUTION_HIGH,
103: }
104: 
105: /// How detected user activity affects model generation.
106: @JsonEnum(alwaysCreate: true)
107: enum ActivityHandling {
108:   @JsonValue('ACTIVITY_HANDLING_UNSPECIFIED')
109:   ACTIVITY_HANDLING_UNSPECIFIED,
110:   @JsonValue('START_OF_ACTIVITY_INTERRUPTS')
111:   START_OF_ACTIVITY_INTERRUPTS,
112:   @JsonValue('NO_INTERRUPTION')
113:   NO_INTERRUPTION,
114:   @JsonValue('NO_INTERRUPTION')
115:   START_OF_ACTIVITY_DOES_NOT_INTERRUPT,
116: }
117: 
118: /// How much of the user turn is forwarded to the model.
119: @JsonEnum(alwaysCreate: true)
120: enum TurnCoverage {
121:   @JsonValue('TURN_COVERAGE_UNSPECIFIED')
122:   TURN_COVERAGE_UNSPECIFIED,
123:   @JsonValue('TURN_INCLUDES_ONLY_ACTIVITY')
124:   TURN_INCLUDES_ONLY_ACTIVITY,
125:   @JsonValue('TURN_INCLUDES_ALL_INPUT')
126:   TURN_INCLUDES_ALL_INPUT,
127:   @JsonValue('TURN_INCLUDES_AUDIO_ACTIVITY_AND_ALL_VIDEO')
128:   TURN_INCLUDES_AUDIO_ACTIVITY_AND_ALL_VIDEO,
129: }
130: 
131: /// Sensitivity levels for detecting the start of speech.
132: @JsonEnum(alwaysCreate: true)
133: enum StartSensitivity {
134:   @JsonValue('START_SENSITIVITY_UNSPECIFIED')

136:   @JsonValue('START_SENSITIVITY_LOW')

138:   @JsonValue('START_SENSITIVITY_HIGH')
139:   START_SENSITIVITY_HIGH,
140: }
141: 
142: /// Sensitivity levels for detecting the end of speech.

144: enum EndSensitivity {
145:   @JsonValue('END_SENSITIVITY_UNSPECIFIED')
146:   END_SENSITIVITY_UNSPECIFIED,
147:   @JsonValue('END_SENSITIVITY_LOW')
148:   END_SENSITIVITY_LOW,
149:   @JsonValue('END_SENSITIVITY_HIGH')
150:   END_SENSITIVITY_HIGH,
151: }
152: 
153: /// Scheduling strategies for tool responses.
154: @JsonEnum(alwaysCreate: true)
155: enum FunctionResponseScheduling {
156:   @JsonValue('SCHEDULING_UNSPECIFIED')

160:   @JsonValue('WHEN_IDLE')

162:   @JsonValue('INTERRUPT')
163:   INTERRUPT,
164: }
165: 
166: /// Execution modes for server-side behaviors such as function calling.
167: @JsonEnum(alwaysCreate: true)
168: enum Behavior {

171:   @JsonValue('BLOCKING')
172:   BLOCKING,
173:   @JsonValue('NON_BLOCKING')
174:   NON_BLOCKING,
175: }
176:

182:   @JsonValue('MALFORMED_FUNCTION_CALL')
183:   MALFORMED_FUNCTION_CALL,
184:   @JsonValue('RESPONSE_REJECTED')
185:   RESPONSE_REJECTED,
186:   @JsonValue('NEED_MORE_INPUT')
187:   NEED_MORE_INPUT,
188:   @JsonValue('PROHIBITED_INPUT_CONTENT')

190:   @JsonValue('IMAGE_PROHIBITED_INPUT_CONTENT')

199:   INPUT_TEXT_NCII_PROHIBITED,
200:   @JsonValue('INPUT_OTHER')
201:   INPUT_OTHER,
202:   @JsonValue('INPUT_IP_PROHIBITED')
203:   INPUT_IP_PROHIBITED,
204:   @JsonValue('BLOCKLIST')
205:   BLOCKLIST,
206:   @JsonValue('UNSAFE_PROMPT_FOR_IMAGE_GENERATION')
207:   UNSAFE_PROMPT_FOR_IMAGE_GENERATION,
208:   @JsonValue('GENERATED_IMAGE_SAFETY')
209:   GENERATED_IMAGE_SAFETY,
210:   @JsonValue('GENERATED_CONTENT_SAFETY')
211:   GENERATED_CONTENT_SAFETY,
212:   @JsonValue('GENERATED_AUDIO_SAFETY')
213:   GENERATED_AUDIO_SAFETY,
214:   @JsonValue('GENERATED_VIDEO_SAFETY')
215:   GENERATED_VIDEO_SAFETY,
216:   @JsonValue('GENERATED_CONTENT_PROHIBITED')
217:   GENERATED_CONTENT_PROHIBITED,
218:   @JsonValue('GENERATED_CONTENT_BLOCKLIST')
219:   GENERATED_CONTENT_BLOCKLIST,
220:   @JsonValue('GENERATED_IMAGE_PROHIBITED')

222:   @JsonValue('GENERATED_IMAGE_CELEBRITY')
223:   GENERATED_IMAGE_CELEBRITY,
224:   @JsonValue('GENERATED_IMAGE_PROMINENT_PEOPLE_DETECTED_BY_REWRITER')

226:   @JsonValue('GENERATED_IMAGE_IDENTIFIABLE_PEOPLE')

230:   @JsonValue('OUTPUT_IMAGE_IP_PROHIBITED')
231:   OUTPUT_IMAGE_IP_PROHIBITED,

256:   @JsonValue('ACTIVITY_END')
257:   ACTIVITY_END,

260: /// Traffic classes used for usage accounting.

262: enum TrafficType {
