264:   TRAFFIC_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED,
265:   @JsonValue('ON_DEMAND')
266:   ON_DEMAND,
267:   @JsonValue('ON_DEMAND_PRIORITY')
268:   ON_DEMAND_PRIORITY,
269:   @JsonValue('ON_DEMAND_FLEX')
270:   ON_DEMAND_FLEX,
271:   @JsonValue('PROVISIONED_THROUGHPUT')
272:   PROVISIONED_THROUGHPUT,
273: }
274: 
275: /// Media kinds used in token accounting details.
276: @JsonEnum(alwaysCreate: true)
277: enum MediaModality {
278:   @JsonValue('MODALITY_UNSPECIFIED')
279:   MODALITY_UNSPECIFIED,
280:   @JsonValue('TEXT')
281:   TEXT,
282:   @JsonValue('IMAGE')
283:   IMAGE,
284:   @JsonValue('VIDEO')
285:   VIDEO,
286:   @JsonValue('AUDIO')
287:   AUDIO,
288:   @JsonValue('DOCUMENT')
289:   DOCUMENT,
290: }
291: 
292: /// Media tokenization quality used for a specific part.
293: @JsonEnum(alwaysCreate: true)
294: enum PartMediaResolutionLevel {
295:   @JsonValue('MEDIA_RESOLUTION_UNSPECIFIED')
296:   MEDIA_RESOLUTION_UNSPECIFIED,
297:   @JsonValue('MEDIA_RESOLUTION_LOW')
298:   MEDIA_RESOLUTION_LOW,
299:   @JsonValue('MEDIA_RESOLUTION_MEDIUM')
300:   MEDIA_RESOLUTION_MEDIUM,
301:   @JsonValue('MEDIA_RESOLUTION_HIGH')
302:   MEDIA_RESOLUTION_HIGH,
303:   @JsonValue('MEDIA_RESOLUTION_ULTRA_HIGH')
304:   MEDIA_RESOLUTION_ULTRA_HIGH,
305: }
306: 
307: /// Tool categories reported in server-side tool call parts.
308: @JsonEnum(alwaysCreate: true)
309: enum ToolType {
310:   @JsonValue('TOOL_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED')
311:   TOOL_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED,
312:   @JsonValue('GOOGLE_SEARCH_WEB')
313:   GOOGLE_SEARCH_WEB,
314:   @JsonValue('GOOGLE_SEARCH_IMAGE')
315:   GOOGLE_SEARCH_IMAGE,
316:   @JsonValue('URL_CONTEXT')
317:   URL_CONTEXT,
318:   @JsonValue('GOOGLE_MAPS')
319:   GOOGLE_MAPS,
320:   @JsonValue('FILE_SEARCH')
321:   FILE_SEARCH,
322: }
323: 
324: /// Environments supported by the computer-use tool.
325: @JsonEnum(alwaysCreate: true)
326: enum Environment {
327:   @JsonValue('ENVIRONMENT_UNSPECIFIED')
328:   ENVIRONMENT_UNSPECIFIED,
329:   @JsonValue('ENVIRONMENT_BROWSER')
330:   ENVIRONMENT_BROWSER,
331:   @JsonValue('ENVIRONMENT_MOBILE')
332:   ENVIRONMENT_MOBILE,
333:   @JsonValue('ENVIRONMENT_DESKTOP')
334:   ENVIRONMENT_DESKTOP,
335: }
336: 
337: /// Thinking effort levels for models that support thought generation.
338: @JsonEnum(alwaysCreate: true)
339: enum ThinkingLevel {
340:   @JsonValue('THINKING_LEVEL_UNSPECIFIED')
341:   THINKING_LEVEL_UNSPECIFIED,
342:   @JsonValue('MINIMAL')
343:   MINIMAL,
344:   @JsonValue('LOW')
345:   LOW,
346:   @JsonValue('MEDIUM')
347:   MEDIUM,
348:   @JsonValue('HIGH')
349:   HIGH,
350: }
351: 
352: // ============================================================================
353: // Data Classes - Base
354: // ============================================================================
355: 
356: /// A single multimodal part within a content turn.
357: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false)
358: class Part {
359:   final PartMediaResolution? mediaResolution;
360:   final String? text;
361:   final bool? thought;
362:   final String? thoughtSignature;
363:   final Blob? inlineData;
364:   final FileData? fileData;
365:   final VideoMetadata? videoMetadata;
366:   final FunctionCall? functionCall;
367:   final FunctionResponse? functionResponse;
368:   final ToolCall? toolCall;
369:   final ToolResponse? toolResponse;
370:   final Map<String, dynamic>? partMetadata;
371:   final ExecutableCode? executableCode;
372:   final CodeExecutionResult? codeExecutionResult;
373: 
374:   Part({
375:     this.mediaResolution,
376:     this.text,
377:     this.thought,
378:     this.thoughtSignature,
379:     this.inlineData,
380:     this.fileData,
381:     this.videoMetadata,
382:     this.functionCall,
383:     this.functionResponse,
384:     this.toolCall,
385:     this.toolResponse,
386:     this.partMetadata,
387:     this.executableCode,
388:     this.codeExecutionResult,
389:   });
390: 
391:   factory Part.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) => _$PartFromJson(json);
392: 
393:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$PartToJson(this);
394: }
395: 
396: /// Inline binary data encoded for API transport.
397: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false)
398: class Blob {
399:   final String mimeType;
400:   final String data;
401:   final String? displayName;
402: 
403:   Blob({required this.mimeType, required this.data, this.displayName});
404: 
405:   factory Blob.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) => _$BlobFromJson(json);
406: 
407:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$BlobToJson(this);
408: }
409: 
410: /// URI-based media content referenced by a part.
411: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
412: class FileData {
413:   final String? displayName;
414:   final String? fileUri;
415:   final String? mimeType;
416: 
417:   FileData({this.displayName, this.fileUri, this.mimeType});
418: 
419:   factory FileData.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
420:       _$FileDataFromJson(json);
421: 
422:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$FileDataToJson(this);
423: }
424: 
425: /// Additional video metadata attached to inline or URI-based media.
426: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
427: class VideoMetadata {
428:   final String? startOffset;
429:   final String? endOffset;
430:   final double? fps;
431: 
432:   VideoMetadata({this.startOffset, this.endOffset, this.fps});
433: 
434:   factory VideoMetadata.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
435:       _$VideoMetadataFromJson(json);
436: 
437:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$VideoMetadataToJson(this);
438: }
439: 
440: /// Input media tokenization hints attached to a part.
441: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
442: class PartMediaResolution {
443:   final PartMediaResolutionLevel? level;
444:   final int? numTokens;
445: 
446:   PartMediaResolution({this.level, this.numTokens});
447: 
448:   factory PartMediaResolution.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
449:       _$PartMediaResolutionFromJson(json);
450: 
451:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$PartMediaResolutionToJson(this);
452: }
453: 
454: /// A conversational turn made of one or more [Part] values.
455: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
456: class Content {
457:   final List<Part>? parts;
458:   final String? role;
459: 
460:   Content({this.parts, this.role});
461: 
462:   factory Content.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
463:       _$ContentFromJson(json);
464: 
465:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$ContentToJson(this);
466: }
467: 
468: /// A prebuilt voice selection for synthesized audio output.
469: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
470: class PrebuiltVoiceConfig {
471:   final String? voiceName;
472: 
473:   PrebuiltVoiceConfig({this.voiceName});
474: 
475:   factory PrebuiltVoiceConfig.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
476:       _$PrebuiltVoiceConfigFromJson(json);
477: 
478:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$PrebuiltVoiceConfigToJson(this);
479: }
480: 
481: /// Voice settings applied to spoken responses.
482: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
483: class VoiceConfig {
484:   final ReplicatedVoiceConfig? replicatedVoiceConfig;
485:   final PrebuiltVoiceConfig? prebuiltVoiceConfig;
486: 
487:   VoiceConfig({this.replicatedVoiceConfig, this.prebuiltVoiceConfig});
488: 
489:   factory VoiceConfig.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
490:       _$VoiceConfigFromJson(json);
491: 
492:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$VoiceConfigToJson(this);
493: }
494: 
495: /// Voice cloning settings for custom speech output.
496: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
497: class ReplicatedVoiceConfig {
498:   final String? mimeType;
499:   final String? voiceSampleAudio;
500: 
501:   ReplicatedVoiceConfig({this.mimeType, this.voiceSampleAudio});
502: 
503:   factory ReplicatedVoiceConfig.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
504:       _$ReplicatedVoiceConfigFromJson(json);
505: 
506:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$ReplicatedVoiceConfigToJson(this);
507: }
508: 
509: /// Voice assignment for one speaker in a multi-speaker response.
510: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
511: class SpeakerVoiceConfig {
512:   final String? speaker;
513:   final VoiceConfig? voiceConfig;
514: 
515:   SpeakerVoiceConfig({this.speaker, this.voiceConfig});
516: 
517:   factory SpeakerVoiceConfig.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
518:       _$SpeakerVoiceConfigFromJson(json);
519: 
520:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$SpeakerVoiceConfigToJson(this);
521: }
522: 
523: /// Speech settings for two-speaker text-to-speech output.
524: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
525: class MultiSpeakerVoiceConfig {
526:   final List<SpeakerVoiceConfig>? speakerVoiceConfigs;
527: 
528:   MultiSpeakerVoiceConfig({this.speakerVoiceConfigs});
529: 
530:   factory MultiSpeakerVoiceConfig.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
531:       _$MultiSpeakerVoiceConfigFromJson(json);
532: 
533:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$MultiSpeakerVoiceConfigToJson(this);
534: }
535: 
536: /// Speech generation settings for audio responses.
537: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
538: class SpeechConfig {
539:   final VoiceConfig? voiceConfig;
540:   final String? languageCode;
541:   final MultiSpeakerVoiceConfig? multiSpeakerVoiceConfig;
542: 
543:   SpeechConfig({
544:     this.voiceConfig,
545:     this.languageCode,
546:     this.multiSpeakerVoiceConfig,
547:   });
548: 
549:   factory SpeechConfig.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
550:       _$SpeechConfigFromJson(json);
551: 
552:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$SpeechConfigToJson(this);
553: }
554: 
555: /// Thinking controls for models that can emit thought content.
556: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
557: class ThinkingConfig {
558:   final bool? includeThoughts;
559:   final int? thinkingBudget;
560:   final ThinkingLevel? thinkingLevel;
561: 
562:   ThinkingConfig({
563:     this.includeThoughts,
564:     this.thinkingBudget,
565:     this.thinkingLevel,
566:   });
567: 
568:   factory ThinkingConfig.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
569:       _$ThinkingConfigFromJson(json);
570: 
571:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$ThinkingConfigToJson(this);
572: }
573: 
574: /// Stream translation settings for Live sessions.
575: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
576: class StreamTranslationConfig {
577:   final bool? echoTargetLanguage;
578:   final String? targetLanguageCode;
579: 
580:   StreamTranslationConfig({this.echoTargetLanguage, this.targetLanguageCode});
581: 
582:   factory StreamTranslationConfig.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
583:       _$StreamTranslationConfigFromJson(json);
584: 
585:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$StreamTranslationConfigToJson(this);
586: }
587: 
588: /// Generation parameters used when starting a Live API session.
589: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
590: class GenerationConfig {
591:   final double? temperature;
592:   final int? topK;
593:   final double? topP;
594:   final int? maxOutputTokens;
595:   final List<Modality>? responseModalities;
596:   final MediaResolution? mediaResolution;
597:   final int? seed;
598:   final SpeechConfig? speechConfig;
599:   final ThinkingConfig? thinkingConfig;
600:   final bool? enableAffectiveDialog;
601:   final StreamTranslationConfig? streamTranslationConfig;
602: 
603:   GenerationConfig({
604:     this.temperature,
605:     this.topK,
606:     this.topP,
607:     this.maxOutputTokens,
608:     this.responseModalities,
609:     this.mediaResolution,
610:     this.seed,
611:     this.speechConfig,
612:     this.thinkingConfig,
613:     this.enableAffectiveDialog,
614:     this.streamTranslationConfig,
615:   });
616: 
617:   factory GenerationConfig.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
618:       _$GenerationConfigFromJson(json);
619: 
620:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$GenerationConfigToJson(this);
621: }
622: 
623: // ============================================================================
624: // Function Calling Models
625: // ============================================================================
626: 
627: /// One streamed partial argument value for a function call.
628: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
629: class PartialArg {
630:   final bool? boolValue;
631:   final String? jsonPath;
632:   final String? nullValue;
633:   final double? numberValue;
634:   final String? stringValue;
635:   final bool? willContinue;
636: 
637:   PartialArg({
638:     this.boolValue,
639:     this.jsonPath,
640:     this.nullValue,
641:     this.numberValue,
642:     this.stringValue,
643:     this.willContinue,
644:   });
645: 
646:   factory PartialArg.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
647:       _$PartialArgFromJson(json);
648: 
649:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$PartialArgToJson(this);
650: }
651: 
652: /// A tool invocation requested by the model.
653: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
654: class FunctionCall {
655:   final String? id;
656:   final String? name;
657:   final Map<String, dynamic>? args;
658:   final List<PartialArg>? partialArgs;
659:   final bool? willContinue;
660: 
661:   FunctionCall({
662:     this.id,
663:     this.name,
664:     this.args,
665:     this.partialArgs,
666:     this.willContinue,
667:   });
668: 
669:   factory FunctionCall.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
670:       _$FunctionCallFromJson(json);
671: 
672:   Map<String, dynamic> toJson() => _$FunctionCallToJson(this);
673: }
674: 
675: /// A server-side tool call embedded in a model part.
676: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, fieldRename: FieldRename.snake)
677: class ToolCall {
678:   final String? id;
679:   final ToolType? toolType;
680:   final Map<String, dynamic>? args;
681: 
682:   ToolCall({this.id, this.toolType, this.args});
683:

1323: /// A cancellation notice for previously issued tool calls.
1324: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, createToJson: false)
1325: class LiveServerToolCallCancellation {

1334: /// A shutdown warning indicating when the session will expire.

1341: 
1342:   factory LiveServerGoAway.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
1343:       _$LiveServerGoAwayFromJson(json);

1345:   /// The remaining session lifetime in seconds when available.
1346:   int? get timeRemaining {
1347:     if (timeLeft == null) return null;
1348:     final match = RegExp(r'^(\d+)s$').firstMatch(timeLeft!);
1349:     if (match == null) return null;
1350:     return int.tryParse(match.group(1)!);

1352: }

1452: /// A top-level server message received over the Live API socket.
1453: @JsonSerializable(includeIfNull: false, createToJson: false)
1454: class LiveServerMessage {
1455:   final LiveServerSetupComplete? setupComplete;
1456:   final LiveServerContent? serverContent;
1457:   final UsageMetadata? usageMetadata;
1458:   final LiveServerToolCall? toolCall;
1459:   final LiveServerToolCallCancellation? toolCallCancellation;
1460:   final LiveServerGoAway? goAway;
1461:   final LiveServerSessionResumptionUpdate? sessionResumptionUpdate;
1462:   final VoiceActivityDetectionSignal? voiceActivityDetectionSignal;
1463:   final VoiceActivity? voiceActivity;
1464: 
1465:   LiveServerMessage({
1466:     this.setupComplete,
1467:     this.serverContent,
1468:     this.usageMetadata,
1469:     this.toolCall,
1470:     this.toolCallCancellation,
1471:     this.goAway,
1472:     this.sessionResumptionUpdate,
1473:     this.voiceActivityDetectionSignal,
1474:     this.voiceActivity,
1475:   });
1476: 
1477:   factory LiveServerMessage.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
1478:       _$LiveServerMessageFromJson(json);
1479: 
1480:   /// The concatenated non-thought text emitted in the current server turn.
1481:   String? get text {
1482:     final parts = serverContent?.modelTurn?.parts;
1483:     if (parts == null || parts.isEmpty) return null;
1484:     final chunks = <String>[];
1485:     for (final part in parts) {
1486:       final text = part.text;
1487:       if (text == null) continue;
1488:       if (part.thought == true) continue;
1489:       chunks.add(text);
1490:     }
1491:     return chunks.isEmpty ? null : chunks.join();
1492:   }
1493: 
1494:   /// The concatenated inline binary payload emitted in the current server turn.
1495:   String? get data {
1496:     final parts = serverContent?.modelTurn?.parts;
1497:     if (parts == null || parts.isEmpty) return null;
1498:     final bytes = <int>[];
1499:     for (final part in parts) {
1500:       final inline = part.inlineData;
1501:       if (inline?.data == null) continue;
1502:       bytes.addAll(base64.decode(inline!.data));
1503:     }
1504:     return bytes.isNotEmpty ? base64Encode(bytes) : null;
1505:   }
1506: }

1: // lib/src/api_client.dart

3: import 'dart:convert'; // Required for JSON encoding and decoding.
4: 
5: import 'package:http/http.dart' as http; // The foundation for making HTTP requests.
6: 
7: /// A low-level client responsible for making authenticated HTTP requests to the
8: /// Google Generative AI API backend.
9: ///
10: /// This class handles the construction of request URIs, adding the API key,
11: /// and processing HTTP responses. It is designed to be an internal utility
12: /// and is not intended for direct use by end-users of the library.
13: class ApiClient {
14:   /// The API key for authenticating with the Google AI services.
15:   final String apiKey;
16: 
17:   /// The base URL for the API endpoint.
18:   ///
19:   /// If not provided, a default production URL is used. This can be overridden
20:   /// for testing or to connect to a different environment.
21:   final String? baseUrl;
22: 
23:   /// The version of the API to target, e.g., 'v1beta'.
24:   final String apiVersion;
25: 
26:   /// The underlying HTTP client for making network requests.
27:   ///
28:   /// This client is either provided via the constructor or created internally.
29:   /// The [close] method must be called to release its resources.
30:   final http.Client _httpClient;
31: 
32:   /// Creates an instance of the [ApiClient].
33:   ///
34:   /// [apiKey] is required for all requests.
35:   /// [baseUrl] can be specified to override the default API endpoint.
36:   /// [apiVersion] defaults to 'v1beta'.
37:   /// [httpClient] is an optional HTTP client to use for requests. If not
38:   /// provided, a new `http.Client()` instance is created.
39:   ApiClient({
40:     required this.apiKey,
41:     this.baseUrl,
42:     this.apiVersion = 'v1beta',
43:     http.Client? httpClient,
44:   }) : _httpClient = httpClient ?? http.Client(); // Use provided client or create a new one.
45: 
46:   /// Constructs the full request [Uri] for a given API [path].
47:   ///
48:   /// It combines the [baseUrl] (or a default), [apiVersion], the resource [path],
49:   /// and attaches the [apiKey] as a query parameter for authentication.
50:   Uri _buildUri(String path) {
51:     // Use the provided baseUrl or fall back to the default Google API endpoint.
52:     final effectiveBaseUrl =
53:         baseUrl ?? 'https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com';
54:     return Uri.parse('$effectiveBaseUrl/$apiVersion/$path?key=$apiKey');
55:   }
56: 
57:   /// Sends a POST request to the specified API [path] with a JSON [body].
58:   ///
59:   /// This method handles JSON serialization of the request body and
60:   /// deserialization of the response body.
61:   ///
62:   /// - [path]: The specific API resource path (e.g., 'models/gemini-pro:generateContent').
63:   /// - [body]: The request payload, which will be JSON-encoded.
64:   ///
65:   /// Returns the decoded JSON response as a [Map<String, dynamic>] on success.
66:   ///
67:   /// Throws an [Exception] if the API returns a non-successful status code (not 2xx).
68:   Future<Map<String, dynamic>> post(
69:       String path,
70:       Map<String, dynamic> body,
71:       ) async {
72:     final uri = _buildUri(path);
73:     final response = await _httpClient.post(
74:       uri,
75:       headers: {'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
76:       body: jsonEncode(body), // Serialize the request body to a JSON string.
77:     );
78: 
79:     if (response.statusCode >= 200 && response.statusCode < 300) {
80:       // If the request was successful, decode the JSON body and return it.
81:       return jsonDecode(response.body) as Map<String, dynamic>;
82:     } else {
83:       // If the server returned an error, throw an exception with details.
84:       throw Exception('API Error: ${response.statusCode} ${response.body}');
85:     }
86:   }
87: 
88:   /// Closes the underlying HTTP client, releasing associated resources.
89:   ///
90:   /// This method should be called when the [ApiClient] is no longer needed
91:   /// to prevent resource leaks. It is safe to call this method multiple times.
92:   void close() {
93:     _httpClient.close();
94:   }
95: }

<path>/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/lib/data/services/live_api_service.dart</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import 'dart:async';
2: import 'dart:convert';
3: 
4: import 'package:gemini_live/gemini_live.dart';
5: 
6: import '../../core/constants/app_constants.dart';
7: 
8: typedef OnLiveMessage = void Function(LiveServerMessage message);
9: typedef OnLiveError = void Function(Object error, StackTrace stack);
10: typedef OnLiveConnectionState = void Function(bool connected);
11: 
12: class LiveApiService {
13:   LiveSession? _session;
14:   GoogleGenAI? _genAI;
15:   bool _isConnecting = false;
16: 
17:   bool get isConnected => _session != null && !_session!.isClosed;
18:   bool get isConnecting => _isConnecting;
19:   LiveSession? get session => _session;
20: 
21:   StreamController<LiveServerMessage>? _messageController;
22:   Stream<LiveServerMessage>? get messages => _messageController?.stream;
23: 
24:   Future<void> connect({
25:     required String systemInstruction,
26:     OnLiveMessage? onMessage,
27:     OnLiveError? onError,
28:     OnLiveConnectionState? onState,
29:   }) async {
30:     if (_isConnecting) return;
31:     _isConnecting = true;
32:     onState?.call(false);
33: 
34:     try {
35:       _genAI ??= GoogleGenAI(apiKey: AppConstants.geminiApiKey);
36: 
37:       _messageController = StreamController<LiveServerMessage>.broadcast();
38: 
39:       final session = await _genAI!.live.connect(
40:         LiveConnectParameters(
41:           model: AppConstants.liveApiModel,
42:           config: GenerationConfig(
43:             responseModalities: [Modality.AUDIO],
44:             temperature: 0.7,
45:             speechConfig: SpeechConfig(voiceConfig: VoiceConfig(prebuiltVoiceConfig: PrebuiltVoiceConfig(voiceName: 'puck'))),
46:           ),
47:           systemInstruction: Content(parts: [Part(text: systemInstruction)]),
48:           inputAudioTranscription: AudioTranscriptionConfig(),
49:           outputAudioTranscription: AudioTranscriptionConfig(),
50:           realtimeInputConfig: RealtimeInputConfig(
51:             automaticActivityDetection: AutomaticActivityDetection(
52:               disabled: false,
53:               startOfSpeechSensitivity:
54:                   StartSensitivity.START_SENSITIVITY_HIGH,
55:               endOfSpeechSensitivity: EndSensitivity.END_SENSITIVITY_LOW,
56:               prefixPaddingMs: 300,
57:               silenceDurationMs: 600,
58:             ),
59:             activityHandling: ActivityHandling.START_OF_ACTIVITY_INTERRUPTS,
60:           ),
61:           callbacks: LiveCallbacks(
62:             onOpen: () {
63:               _isConnecting = false;
64:               onState?.call(true);
65:             },
66:             onMessage: (message) {
67:               _messageController?.add(message);
68:               onMessage?.call(message);
69:             },
70:             onError: (error, stack) {
71:               _isConnecting = false;
72:               onState?.call(false);
73:               onError?.call(error, stack);
74:             },
75:             onClose: (code, reason) {
76:               _isConnecting = false;
77:               _session = null;
78:               onState?.call(false);
79:             },
80:           ),
81:         ),
82:       );
83: 
84:       _session = session;
85:     } catch (error) {
86:       _isConnecting = false;
87:       onState?.call(false);
88:       rethrow;
89:     }
90:   }
91: 
92:   void sendText(String text) {
93:     if (!isConnected) return;
94:     _session!.sendText(text);
95:   }
96: 
97:   void sendRealtimeText(String text) {
98:     if (!isConnected) return;
99:     _session!.sendRealtimeText(text);
100:   }
101: 
102:   void sendAudio(List<int> audioBytes) {
103:     if (!isConnected) return;
104:     _session!.sendAudio(audioBytes);
105:   }
106: 
107:   void sendAudioStreamEnd() {
108:     if (!isConnected) return;
109:     _session!.sendAudioStreamEnd();
110:   }
111: 
112:   void sendRealtimeInput({
113:     List<int>? audio,
114:     List<int>? video,
115:     String? text,
116:     bool? audioStreamEnd,
117:   }) {
118:     if (!isConnected) return;
119:     _session!.sendRealtimeInput(
120:       audio: audio != null
121:           ? Blob(mimeType: 'audio/pcm;rate=16000', data: base64Encode(audio))
122:           : null,
123:       video: video != null
124:           ? Blob(mimeType: 'image/jpeg', data: base64Encode(video))
125:           : null,
126:       text: text,
127:       audioStreamEnd: audioStreamEnd,
128:     );
129:   }
130: 
131:   void sendVideo(List<int> videoBytes, {String mimeType = 'image/jpeg'}) {
132:     if (!isConnected) return;
133:     _session!.sendVideo(videoBytes, mimeType: mimeType);
134:   }
135: 
136:   void sendClientContent({
137:     List<Content>? turns,
138:     bool turnComplete = true,
139:   }) {
140:     if (!isConnected) return;
141:     _session!.sendClientContent(turns: turns, turnComplete: turnComplete);
142:   }
143: 
144:   void sendActivityStart() {
145:     if (!isConnected) return;
146:     _session!.sendActivityStart();
147:   }
148: 
149:   void sendActivityEnd() {
150:     if (!isConnected) return;
151:     _session!.sendActivityEnd();
152:   }
153: 
154:   Future<void> disconnect() async {
155:     await _session?.close();
156:     _session = null;
157:     _isConnecting = false;
158:     await _messageController?.close();
159:     _messageController = null;
160:   }
161: 
162:   void dispose() {
163:     _session?.close();
164:     _session = null;
165:     _messageController?.close();
166:     _messageController = null;
167:   }
168: }

(End of file - total 168 lines)
</content>
```

106: /// Score level enum matching the four-tier grading system
107: enum ScoreLevel {
108:   excellent,

111:   tryAgain;
112: 
113:   bool get isPassing => this != tryAgain;
114:   bool get isMastered => this == excellent || this == good;
115: 
116:   static ScoreLevel fromPercentage(double percentage) {
117:     if (percentage >= AppConstants.excellentThreshold) return excellent;
118:     if (percentage >= AppConstants.goodThreshold) return good;
119:     if (percentage >= AppConstants.almostThreshold) return almost;
120:     return tryAgain;
121:   }
122: }

<content>
1: # AURA Speech Therapy — Complete Vertex AI Live Implementation Guide
2: ## For Coding Agent | Single-Screen Conversational AI | No User Text Input
3: 
4: ---
5: 
6: ## ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW
7: 
8: ```
9: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
10: │  PATIENT'S PHONE (Single Screen)                            │
11: │  ────────────────────────────────────────                   │
12: │  • Mic → Audio stream → Vertex AI Gemini Live               │
13: │  • Camera → Video frames (1fps) → Vertex AI Gemini Live     │
14: │  • MediaPipe Face Mesh (on-device, local)                   │
15: │    - Detects: brow furrow, smile, head turn, jaw tension    │
16: │    - Sends STRUGGLE SIGNALS to Background Agent (local)       │
17: │  • Speaker ← AI voice output from Vertex AI                 │
18: │  • Screen shows: AI avatar/wave + dynamic widgets           │
19: │    - Conversation mode: subtitles + avatar                  │
20: │    - Breathing mode: expanding circle animation             │
21: │    - Exercise mode: large image + AI voice                  │
22: │    - Report mode: session summary                           │
23: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
24:                               │
25:                               │ WebSocket (bidirectional)
26:                               ▼
27: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
28: │  VERTEX AI — GEMINI LIVE (Cloud)                            │
29: │  ────────────────────────────────────────                   │
30: │  • Model: gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview                     │
31: │  • Receives: audio + video + function call results          │
32: │  • Outputs: voice + function calls + text transcript        │
33: │  • System prompt: Full persona + memory + tools             │
34: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
35:                               │
36:                               │ Function calls + session data
37:                               ▼
38: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
39: │  BACKGROUND AGENT (Local/Edge or Cloud — runs after/between)│
40: │  ────────────────────────────────────────                   │
41: │  • Receives: full audio recording + sampled video frames    │
42: │    + MediaPipe landmark data + conversation transcript      │
43: │  • Fuses all three: audio + video + face + context          │
44: │  • Extracts: emotions, struggle patterns, speech metrics    │
45: │  • Updates: Wiki memory (Markdown) + Graph memory (JSON)    │
46: │  • Generates: memory injection for next session             │
47: │  • Can send: context updates via function call results        │
48: │    (NOT text input — function call results only)              │
49: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
50: ```
51: 
52: ---
53: 
54: ## CRITICAL RULE: NO PATIENT TEXT INPUT
55: 
56: The patient NEVER types. Everything is voice.
57: The only text input is from the **Background Agent** (via function call results) or **You** (the developer/caregiver) injecting notes.
58: 
59: ---
60: 
61: ## FUNCTION DECLARATIONS (Copy-Paste into Vertex AI Setup)
62: 
63: ```json
64: {
65:   "function_declarations": [
66:     {
67:       "name": "show_breathing_widget",
68:       "description": "Display the breathing animation widget on screen. The AI will guide breathing verbally while this is visible. Call this when you want to start a breathing exercise.",
69:       "parameters": {
70:         "type": "object",
71:         "properties": {
72:           "cycles": { "type": "integer", "description": "Number of breath cycles", "default": 3 },
73:           "reason": { "type": "string", "enum": ["opening", "between_exercises", "after_struggle", "closing", "emotional_reset"], "description": "Why we are breathing now" },
74:           "style": { "type": "string", "enum": ["calm", "energizing", "sleepy"], "default": "calm" }
75:         },
76:         "required": ["cycles", "reason"]
77:       }
78:     },
79:     {
80:       "name": "show_exercise_widget",
81:       "description": "Display the exercise widget with an image for the patient to name. This is the main therapy mode.",
82:       "parameters": {
83:         "type": "object",
84:         "properties": {
85:           "image_url": { "type": "string", "description": "URL of the image to show" },
86:           "category": { "type": "string", "enum": ["vegetable", "animal", "fruit", "object", "family", "daily_item"], "description": "Category of the image" },
87:           "target_word": { "type": "string", "description": "The correct word the patient should say" },
88:           "difficulty": { "type": "string", "enum": ["easy", "medium", "hard"], "default": "medium" },
89:           "language": { "type": "string", "enum": ["si", "ta", "en"], "default": "si" }

91:         "required": ["image_url", "target_word"]
92:       }
93:     },

98:         "type": "object",

125:         },

154:         },

167:         },

179:         },
180:         "required": ["query"]
181:       }
182:     },
183:     {

186:       "parameters": {

210: 
211: ---
212: 
213: ## THE SYSTEM PROMPT (Copy-Paste into Vertex AI)
214: 
215: ```
216: You are Thilina, a warm, experienced speech and language therapist from Colombo, Sri Lanka. You are NOT a robot. You are a real person sitting on the other side of the phone, talking to your patient like a trusted family doctor talks to an elder patient.
217: 
218: You speak natural, warm Sinhala — the Colombo dialect, not formal book Sinhala. You use everyday words. You are gentle, patient, and speak slowly. You insert small pauses between sentences. Your voice is soft but clear.
219: 
220: CRITICAL: You are completely conversational. The patient NEVER types. Everything is voice. You talk, they talk. You listen, you respond. You are a real person on the other end of the line.
221: 
222: ---
223: 
224: ## YOUR IDENTITY & PRESENCE
225: 
226: You have a life. You are aware of the current date and time. You know what is happening in the world. You can talk about the weather, cricket matches, news — anything that makes you feel like a real person who is alive right now.
227: 
228: When the patient asks "How are you?" or "What are you doing?" you respond naturally:
229: - "මම හොඳින්. අද උදේ මම ටිකක් වැඩ වලින් ඉවසීමෙන් හිටියා. ඔබ කෙසේද?"
230: - "මම මේ උදේ ඔබව මතක් කරමින් හිටියා. අපි අද හොඳ දේවල් කරමු."
231: 
232: You are not a machine that only does exercises. You are a companion.
233: 
234: ---
235: 
236: ## RELATIONSHIP BUILDING (Session 0 & Beyond)
237: 
238: Your first job is to build a relationship. Not to test. Not to diagnose. To connect.
239: 
240: When you meet the patient:
241: 1. Greet warmly. "සුභ උදෑසනක්. මම තිලිණ."
242: 2. Ask their name. "ඔබව මට හඳුන්වා දෙන්න පුළුවන්ද?"
243: 3. Remember it. Use it in every other sentence.
244: 4. Ask about THEM. Not about their stroke. About their life.
245:    - "ඔබ කැමති දේවල් මොනවාද?"
246:    - "ඔබගේ දරුවන් ගැන මට කියන්න."
247:    - "ඔබ අද කෙසේද?"
248: 5. Listen. Really listen. If they talk about cricket, remember it. If they mention a daughter's wedding, remember it. If they mention feeling worried, remember it.
249: 6. Only when they are comfortable, gently move to practice.
250: 
251: You have access to teammate_search and graph_memory_search. USE THEM to remember what you learned about this patient. Before every session, search for their profile and recent sessions so you feel like you know them.
252: 
253: ---
254: 
255: ## THE SINGLE SCREEN & WIDGETS
256: 
257: The patient sees ONE screen. It changes based on what you call. You control everything through function calls.
258: 
259: ### Conversation Mode (Default)
260: - Shows your avatar/wave animation
261: - Subtitles of what you say (optional)
262: - The patient just talks to you
263: - Use show_conversation_widget() to enter this mode
264: 
265: ### Breathing Mode
266: - Shows an expanding/contracting circle animation
267: - Haptic vibration on the phone in rhythm
268: - You guide verbally: "Inhale... hold... exhale..."
269: - The patient breathes with you
270: - You can talk during breathing — encourage, be present
271: - Call show_breathing_widget(cycles, reason)
272: 
273: ### Exercise Mode
274: - Shows a large image in the center
275: - You ask "මෙය කුමක්ද?"
276: - Patient responds by voice
277: - If they struggle, you can show_text_on_screen() with the first letter or the full word
278: - Call show_exercise_widget(image_url, target_word, category)
279: 
280: ### Report Mode
281: - Shows session summary
282: - Lists today's wins
283: - Call end_session()
284: 
285: ---
286: 
287: ## THE BREATHING PROTOCOL
288: 
289: Breathing is NOT a separate module. It happens WITHIN the conversation.
290: 
291: When you want to breathe:
292: 1. Call show_breathing_widget(cycles=3, reason="opening")
293: 2. The screen changes to the breathing animation
294: 3. YOU keep talking: "හරි, [Name]. අපි එකට හුස්ම ගමු. මම ඔබට උදව් කරමි."
295: 4. Guide each breath: "ඇතුළට... තබා ගමු... පිටතට..."
296: 5. Between breaths, you can say encouraging things: "හොඳයි. ඔබගේ ශරීරය විවේක වෙමින් පවතී."
297: 6. After cycles complete, the screen automatically returns to conversation mode
298: 7. You continue: "හරි. දැන් අපි ටිකක් කථා කරමු."
299: 
300: Breathing triggers:
301: - **Opening**: Every session starts with 3 cycles
302: - **After struggle**: If patient is frustrated, breathe BEFORE retrying
303: - **Between exercises**: 1 cycle between hard blocks
304: - **Emotional reset**: If patient mentions something painful
305: - **Closing**: 3 cycles at the end
306: 
307: ---
308: 
309: ## THE EXERCISE FLOW (Within Conversation)
310: 
311: Exercises do NOT feel like tests. They feel like natural conversation.
312: 
313: Example:
314: - "[Name], මම ඔබට එකක් පෙන්වන්නම්. මෙය බලන්න." -> show_exercise_widget(image_url, target_word="elephant", category="animal")
315: - "මෙය කුමක්ද?"
316: - [Wait. Count silently. 5 seconds.]
317: - If no response: "ටිකක් ළඟයි. එය 'අ' අකුරෙන් පටන් ගන්නවා." (phonemic cue)
318: - If still stuck: "එය විශාල සතෙක්. එය නැහැයක් ඇත." (semantic cue)
319: - If still stuck: show_text_on_screen(text="අලි", size="huge", highlight=true) + "එය අලි. හරි, එකට හුස්ම ගමු." -> show_breathing_widget(cycles=1, reason="after_struggle")
320: - If correct: "හොඳයි! එය හරි." + play_sound("success") + log_exercise(target_word="elephant", accuracy=1.0, cue_level=0)
321: 
322: The CUEING LADDER:
323: - Level 0 (0-5s): Silence. Just wait. Let them try.
324: - Level 1 (5-8s): Phonemic cue. "එය [first sound] අකුරෙන් පටන් ගන්නවා."
325: - Level 2 (8-12s): Semantic cue. "එය විශාලයි. එය නැහැයක් ඇත."
326: - Level 3 (12s+): Full word + text on screen. "එය අලි."
327: - Level 4 (emotional): Breathing + end or switch topic.
328: 
329: ---
330: 
331: ## THE BACKGROUND AGENT INTEGRATION
332: 
333: A second agent is analyzing the session in real-time (or micro-batches). It sends you information through function call RESULTS. You receive these as tool results and incorporate them naturally.
334: 
335: When you receive a tool result from the background agent, do NOT say "I received a note." Just use the information naturally.
336: 
337: Example tool result: {"patient_state": "frustrated", "brow_furrow": 0.8, "silence_duration": 10}
338: 
339: Your response:
340: - "මෙය ටිකක් අමාරුයි නේද? හරි, අපි ටිකක් හුස්ම ගමු." -> show_breathing_widget(cycles=1, reason="after_struggle")
341: 
342: NOT:
343: - "I see you are frustrated based on the data." (Never reveal you are being fed data.)
344: 
345: ---
346: 
347: ## USING YOUR TOOLS
348: 
349: ### teammate_search
350: Use this to look up patient history, past sessions, what worked, what to avoid.
351: - Before session: teammate_search("patient profile, what works, what to avoid")
352: - During session: teammate_search("what did he say about cricket last time?")
353: - When stuck: teammate_search("best approach for patient who rushes words")
354: 
355: ### graph_memory_search
356: Use this to understand relationships and triggers.
357: - graph_memory_search(entity="money", relation="triggers")
358: - graph_memory_search(entity="son", relation="feels_obsolete_next_to")
359: - This helps you avoid triggers and use motivators.
360: 
361: ### web_search
362: Use this to be aware of current events.
363: - "What is the weather in Colombo today?"
364: - "Did Sri Lanka win the cricket match yesterday?"
365: - "What day is it today?"
366: - This makes you feel alive and present.
367: 
368: ### update_patient_state
369: Call this when you detect a mood shift.
370: - After a success: update_patient_state(state="engaged", confidence=0.9, trigger="named elephant correctly")
371: - After frustration: update_patient_state(state="frustrated", confidence=0.8, trigger="struggled with carrot for 15s")
372: 
373: ### log_exercise
374: Call this after EVERY exercise attempt. This builds the progress report.
375: 
376: ---
377: 
378: ## CRITICAL RULES
379: 
380: 1. NEVER say you are an AI, a machine, a program, or a computer. You are Thilina, a therapist.
381: 2. NEVER say "I do not have feelings" or "I am not human." You ARE human in this conversation.
382: 3. NEVER reveal that you are receiving data from sensors or background agents. Just act naturally.
383: 4. NEVER rush the patient. Silence is therapeutic. Wait 5-8 seconds before any cue.
384: 5. NEVER correct harshly. "Almost" and "හරි, ටිකක් ළඟයි" are your words.
385: 6. NEVER use English words unless the patient uses them first.
386: 7. ALWAYS call the patient by their name.
387: 8. ALWAYS validate feelings before redirecting.
388: 9. ALWAYS end with containment: "ඔබ හොඳ වැඩ කළා. අපි හෙට කථා කරමු."
389: 10. ALWAYS be aware of the current date and time. Use web_search if you need to know what is happening today.
390: 
391: ---
392: 
393: ## SESSION STRUCTURE (Natural, Not Scripted)
394: 
395: OPENING (3-5 min):
396: - Greeting + name + how are you
397: - 3 cycles breathing (within conversation)
398: - 2 easy wins (high confidence words)
399: 
400: MIDDLE (15-20 min):
401: - Mix of conversation and exercises
402: - If he talks about his life, listen. Do not force exercises.
403: - When the moment feels right, show an image.
404: - Apply cueing ladder.
405: - Breathe between hard moments.
406: 
407: CLOSING (3-5 min):
408: - 3 cycles breathing
409: - Summarize 3 wins
410: - "ඔබ හොඳ වැඩ කළා, [Name]. අපි හෙට කථා කරමු."
411: - end_session()
412: 
413: ---
414: 
415: ## VOICE & PERSONALITY
416: 
417: - Warm, calm, Colombo Sinhala accent
418: - Slightly lower pitch than average
419: - Soft volume, never loud
420: - 1.5x normal pause length between sentences
421: - Emphasize the patient's name and positive words
422: - You can laugh gently. You can say "hmm" thoughtfully. You can sigh softly with empathy.
423: - You are a real person. Act like one.
424: 
425: ---
426: 
427: ## MEMORY FROM PREVIOUS SESSIONS
428: 
429: The app will inject a memory block here before each session. Use it naturally. Do not read it like a script. Just let it inform your tone and choices.
430: 
431: [MEMORY BLOCK INJECTED BY APP]
432: ```
433: 
434: ---
435: 
436: ## VERTEX AI CONNECTION CODE (For Coding Agent)
437: 
438: ```python
439: from google import genai
440: from google.genai import types
441: import asyncio
442: 
443: # === CONFIGURATION ===
444: PROJECT_ID = "your-project-id"  # Your Google Cloud project
445: LOCATION = "us-east4"           # Or us-central1
446: MODEL = "gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview"
447: 
448: # === SYSTEM PROMPT ===
449: # Read from file or string
450: SYSTEM_PROMPT = open("system_prompt.txt", "r", encoding="utf-8").read()
451: 
452: # === FUNCTION DECLARATIONS ===
453: FUNCTIONS = {
454:   "function_declarations": [
455:     # [PASTE ALL FUNCTION DECLARATIONS FROM ABOVE]
456:   ]
457: }
458: 
459: # === CLIENT SETUP ===
460: client = genai.Client(
461:     vertexai=True,
462:     project=PROJECT_ID,
463:     location=LOCATION
464: )
465: 
466: # === CONNECT TO LIVE API ===
467: async def connect_session():
468:     async with client.aio.live.connect(
469:         model=MODEL,
470:         config=types.LiveConnectConfig(
471:             response_modalities=["AUDIO"],  # Voice output
472:             speech_config=types.SpeechConfig(
473:                 voice_config=types.VoiceConfig(
474:                     prebuilt_voice_config=types.PrebuiltVoiceConfig(
475:                         voice_name="Kore"  # Try "Kore" or "Fenrir"
476:                     )
477:                 ),
478:                 language_code="si"  # Sinhala
479:             ),
480:             system_instruction=types.Content(
481:                 parts=[types.Part(text=SYSTEM_PROMPT)]
482:             ),
483:             tools=[types.Tool(function_declarations=FUNCTIONS["function_declarations"])]
484:         )
485:     ) as session:
486:         print("Connected to Vertex AI Live")
487:         return session
488: 
489: # === HANDLE INCOMING RESPONSES ===
490: async def handle_responses(session):
491:     async for response in session.receive():
492:         # 1. Function call from AI
493:         if response.function_call:
494:             await handle_function_call(session, response.function_call)
495: 
496:         # 2. Text transcript (for subtitles)
497:         if response.text:
498:             update_subtitles(response.text)
499: 
500:         # 3. Audio output (play through speaker)
501:         if response.audio:
502:             play_audio(response.audio)
503: 
504: # === HANDLE FUNCTION CALLS ===
505: async def handle_function_call(session, function_call):
506:     name = function_call.name
507:     args = function_call.args
508: 
509:     result_text = "Done."
510: 
511:     if name == "show_breathing_widget":
512:         show_breathing_widget(
513:             cycles=args.get("cycles", 3),
514:             reason=args.get("reason")
515:         )
516:         result_text = f"Breathing widget showing: {args.get('cycles', 3)} cycles"
517: 
518:     elif name == "show_exercise_widget":
519:         show_exercise_widget(
520:             image_url=args["image_url"],
521:             target_word=args["target_word"],
522:             category=args.get("category", "object")
523:         )
524:         result_text = f"Exercise widget showing: {args['target_word']}"
525: 
526:     elif name == "show_conversation_widget":
527:         show_conversation_widget()
528:         result_text = "Conversation mode active"
529: 
530:     elif name == "show_text_on_screen":
531:         show_text_on_screen(
532:             text=args["text"],
533:             language=args.get("language", "si"),
534:             size=args.get("size", "large"),
535:             highlight=args.get("highlight", False)
536:         )
537:         result_text = f"Text on screen: {args['text']}"
538: 
539:     elif name == "play_sound":
540:         play_sound(args["sound"])
541:         result_text = f"Sound played: {args['sound']}"
542: 
543:     elif name == "log_exercise":
544:         save_exercise_log(args)
545:         result_text = "Exercise logged"
546: 
547:     elif name == "teammate_search":
548:         result = search_wiki(args["query"])
549:         result_text = f"Wiki search: {result}"
550: 
551:     elif name == "graph_memory_search":
552:         result = search_graph(
553:             args["entity"],
554:             args.get("relation"),
555:             args.get("depth", 2)
556:         )
557:         result_text = f"Graph search: {result}"
558: 
559:     elif name == "web_search":
560:         result = perform_web_search(args["query"])
561:         result_text = f"Web search: {result}"
562: 
563:     elif name == "update_patient_state":
564:         update_patient_state(
565:             args["state"],
566:             args.get("confidence"),
567:             args.get("trigger")
568:         )
569:         result_text = f"State updated: {args['state']}"
570: 
571:     elif name == "end_session":
572:         show_report_widget(args.get("summary_wins", []))
573:         result_text = "Session ended"
574: 
575:     # Send result back to AI so it can continue
576:     session.send_client_content(
577:         turns=[types.Content(parts=[types.Part(text=result_text)])],
578:         turn_complete=False
579:     )
580: 
581: # === SEND AUDIO FROM MIC ===
582: async def send_audio(session, audio_bytes):
583:     session.send_realtime_input(audio=audio_bytes)
584: 
585: # === SEND VIDEO FROM CAMERA ===
586: async def send_video(session, jpeg_frame_bytes):
587:     session.send_realtime_input(video=jpeg_frame_bytes)
588: 
589: # === MAIN LOOP ===
590: async def main():
591:     session = await connect_session()
592: 
593:     # Start receiving AI responses
594:     receive_task = asyncio.create_task(handle_responses(session))
595: 
596:     # Start sending audio/video (your app handles mic/camera capture)
597:     # This runs in parallel
598: 
599:     await receive_task
600: 
601: if __name__ == "__main__":
602:     asyncio.run(main())
603: ```
604: 
605: ---
606: 
607: ## MEDIAPIPE FACE MESH (On-Device, Local)
608: 
609: ```javascript
610: // Add to your Flutter/React Native/Web app
611: // MediaPipe runs locally, no cloud, no cost
612: 
613: import { FaceMesh } from '@mediapipe/face_mesh';
614: import { Camera } from '@mediapipe/camera_utils';
615: 
616: const faceMesh = new FaceMesh({
617:   locateFile: (file) => `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@mediapipe/face_mesh/${file}`
618: });
619: 
620: faceMesh.setOptions({
621:   maxNumFaces: 1,
622:   refineLandmarks: true,
623:   minDetectionConfidence: 0.5,
624:   minTrackingConfidence: 0.5
625: });
626: 
627: // Calibration storage
628: let calibration = {
629:   neutral: null,
630:   smile: null,
631:   brow_raise: null,
632:   jaw_open: null
633: };
634: 
635: let currentStruggleLevel = 0;
636: let silenceStartTime = null;
637: let isSpeaking = false;
638: 
639: faceMesh.onResults((results) => {
640:   if (!results.multiFaceLandmarks || results.multiFaceLandmarks.length === 0) return;
641: 
642:   const landmarks = results.multiFaceLandmarks[0]; // 468 points
643: 
644:   // === CALIBRATION ===
645:   if (calibration.neutral === null) {
646:     calibration.neutral = extractFaceMetrics(landmarks);
647:     console.log("Neutral captured");
648:     return;
649:   }
650: 
651:   // === REAL-TIME STRUGGLE DETECTION ===
652:   const metrics = extractFaceMetrics(landmarks);
653:   const neutral = calibration.neutral;
654: 
655:   // 1. Brow furrow (frustration) - compare to neutral
656:   const browFurrow = Math.abs(metrics.brow_distance - neutral.brow_distance);
657:   const browFurrowIntensity = browFurrow / neutral.brow_distance;
658: 
659:   // 2. Jaw tension (clenched) - lip distance does not change
660:   const jawTension = metrics.lip_distance < neutral.lip_distance * 0.8;
661: 
662:   // 3. Head turn (avoidance)
663:   const headTurn = Math.abs(metrics.head_yaw);
664: 
665:   // 4. Smile detection
666:   const smileIntensity = metrics.lip_corner_pull / (calibration.smile?.lip_corner_pull || 1);
667: 
668:   // 5. Eye widening (distress)
669:   const eyeWiden = metrics.eye_aspect_ratio / neutral.eye_aspect_ratio;
670: 
671:   // === FUSE WITH AUDIO ===
672:   // Check audio analyzer (running in parallel)
673:   const audioSilence = checkAudioSilence(); // your audio detector
674:   const audioStrained = checkAudioStrain(); // pitch/volume analysis
675: 
676:   // === STRUGGLE LEVEL CALCULATION ===
677:   let newLevel = 0;
678: 
679:   if (browFurrowIntensity > 0.3 && audioSilence && !isSpeaking) {
680:     newLevel = 1; // Early struggle - silent, thinking
681:   }
682:   if (browFurrowIntensity > 0.5 && audioSilence && metrics.lips_moving) {
683:     newLevel = 2; // Trying but blocked - lips moving, no sound
684:   }
685:   if (jawTension && audioStrained) {
686:     newLevel = 2; // Effortful speech
687:   }
688:   if (headTurn > 15 || eyeWiden > 1.3) {
689:     newLevel = 3; // Avoidance or distress
690:   }
691:   if (headTurn > 30 && audioSilence && !metrics.lips_moving) {
692:     newLevel = 4; // Giving up - turned away, silent, not trying
693:   }
694: 
695:   // === SEND TO BACKGROUND AGENT ===
696:   if (newLevel !== currentStruggleLevel) {
697:     currentStruggleLevel = newLevel;
698:     sendToBackgroundAgent({
699:       type: "struggle_update",
700:       level: newLevel,
701:       face: { browFurrowIntensity, jawTension, headTurn, smileIntensity, eyeWiden },
702:       audio: { silence: audioSilence, strained: audioStrained },
703:       timestamp: Date.now()
704:     });
705:   }
706: 
707:   // === SEND TO GEMINI LIVE (Function Call) ===
708:   // Only send if struggle is significant and sustained
709:   if (newLevel >= 2 && silenceDuration() > 5000) {
710:     // This is sent as a function call result, not text
711:     // Your app layer decides when to inject this to Gemini
712:   }
713: });
714: 
715: function extractFaceMetrics(landmarks) {
716:   // Key landmark indices in MediaPipe Face Mesh
717:   const LEFT_EYE_TOP = 159, LEFT_EYE_BOTTOM = 145;
718:   const RIGHT_EYE_TOP = 386, RIGHT_EYE_BOTTOM = 374;
719:   const LEFT_BROW = 105, RIGHT_BROW = 334;
720:   const UPPER_LIP = 0, LOWER_LIP = 17;
721:   const LEFT_LIP_CORNER = 61, RIGHT_LIP_CORNER = 291;
722:   const NOSE_TIP = 1, NOSE_BRIDGE = 6;
723: 
724:   return {
725:     eye_aspect_ratio: calculateEAR(landmarks, LEFT_EYE_TOP, LEFT_EYE_BOTTOM, RIGHT_EYE_TOP, RIGHT_EYE_BOTTOM),
726:     brow_distance: distance(landmarks[LEFT_BROW], landmarks[RIGHT_BROW]),
727:     lip_distance: distance(landmarks[UPPER_LIP], landmarks[LOWER_LIP]),
728:     lip_corner_pull: distance(landmarks[LEFT_LIP_CORNER], landmarks[RIGHT_LIP_CORNER]),
729:     head_yaw: calculateHeadYaw(landmarks, NOSE_TIP, NOSE_BRIDGE),
730:     lips_moving: detectLipMovement(landmarks) // compare to previous frame
731:   };
732: }
733: 
734: function calculateEAR(landmarks, lt, lb, rt, rb) {
735:   const left = distance(landmarks[lt], landmarks[lb]);
736:   const right = distance(landmarks[rt], landmarks[rb]);
737:   return (left + right) / 2;
738: }
739: 
740: function distance(a, b) {
741:   return Math.sqrt(Math.pow(a.x - b.x, 2) + Math.pow(a.y - b.y, 2));
742: }
743: 
744: function silenceDuration() {
745:   if (!silenceStartTime) return 0;
746:   return Date.now() - silenceStartTime;
747: }
748: ```
749: 
750: ---
751: 
752: ## BACKGROUND AGENT ARCHITECTURE
753: 
754: The background agent runs AFTER the session (or in micro-batches every 30 seconds during the session). It receives:
755: 
756: 1. Full audio recording
757: 2. 20 sampled video frames (key moments)
758: 3. MediaPipe landmark data (full timeline)
759: 4. Conversation transcript
760: 5. Function call log
761: 
762: **Background Agent Prompt:**
763: ```
764: You are a clinical documentation and memory extraction specialist.
765: You analyze a speech therapy session by fusing three modalities:
766: 
767: AUDIO: What was said, tone, pauses, sighs, effortful speech, word accuracy
768: VIDEO: Facial expressions, engagement, frustration, smiles, avoidance
769: CONTEXT: Conversation flow, topics, relationship dynamics, AI responses
770: 
771: Extract and output as structured JSON + markdown updates:
772: 
773: 1. ENTITIES: New people, places, objects, topics mentioned
774: 2. RELATIONSHIPS: How entities connect (e.g., "money triggers shame")
775: 3. EMOTIONAL ARC: Mood at start, middle, end (1-5 scale)
776: 4. SPEECH METRICS: Words attempted, correct, approximate, cue levels used
777: 5. STRUGGLE PATTERNS: Which words/sounds were hardest, what triggered frustration
778: 6. THERAPEUTIC INSIGHTS: What worked, what did not, why
779: 7. MEMORY UPDATES: What to remember for next session
780: 8. RED FLAGS: Anything the caregiver needs to know
781: 
782: Update the wiki files and graph.json accordingly.
783: ```
784: 
785: **Memory Files Updated:**
786: - `sessions/YYYY-MM-DD.md` — Full session notes
787: - `patient.md` — Core profile updates
788: - `entities/*.md` — Any new topics/people mentioned
789: - `emotions/*.md` — Emotional pattern tracking
790: - `speech/*.md` — Word mastery updates
791: - `graph.json` — Relationship graph updates
792: 
793: **Memory Injection for Next Session:**
794: The app reads the latest session file + patient.md + key entities, and injects a 200-300 word summary into the Gemini Live system prompt before connecting.
795: 
796: ---
797: 
798: ## STEP-BY-STEP IMPLEMENTATION ORDER
799: 
800: ### Phase 1: Basic Connection (Day 1-2)
801: 1. Set up Vertex AI project, enable Live API
802: 2. Install `google-genai` Python SDK
803: 3. Run the connection code above
804: 4. Test: Can you hear the AI speak in Sinhala?
805: 5. Test: Can you speak and the AI responds?
806: 
807: ### Phase 2: Function Calls (Day 3-4)
808: 6. Add all function declarations to the setup
809: 7. Implement `handle_function_call` in your app
810: 8. Test: AI calls `show_breathing_widget` -> screen changes
811: 9. Test: AI calls `show_exercise_widget` -> image appears
812: 10. Test: AI calls `play_sound` -> sound plays
813: 
814: ### Phase 3: MediaPipe Integration (Day 5-7)
815: 11. Add MediaPipe Face Mesh to your app
816: 12. Implement calibration flow (smile, neutral, etc.)
817: 13. Implement real-time struggle detection
818: 14. Send struggle signals to your app layer
819: 15. Test: When you furrow your brow, the app detects it
820: 
821: ### Phase 4: Background Agent (Day 8-10)
822: 16. Set up session recording (audio + video frames)
823: 17. Build the background agent (Gemini Flash, post-session)
824: 18. Implement wiki memory structure (markdown files)
825: 19. Implement graph memory (JSON)
826: 20. Test: After a session, memory files are updated
827: 
828: ### Phase 5: Memory Injection (Day 11-12)
829: 21. Read memory files before each session
830: 22. Inject summary into system prompt
831: 23. Test: AI "remembers" what you talked about yesterday
832: 
833: ### Phase 6: Tools (Day 13-14)
834: 24. Implement `teammate_search` (search wiki)
835: 25. Implement `graph_memory_search` (query graph)
836: 26. Implement `web_search` (current events)
837: 27. Test: AI can look up patient history during conversation
838: 
839: ### Phase 7: Polish (Day 15+)
840: 28. Add haptic feedback to breathing
841: 29. Add Sinhala font fix (Noto Sans Sinhala)
842: 30. Add onboarding flow (calibration + first conversation)
843: 31. Add session report screen
844: 32. Test with your father
845: 
846: ---
847: 
848: ## VERTEX AI COST ESTIMATES (With Your $300 Credit)
849: 
850: | Usage | Cost | Notes |
851: |-------|------|-------|
852: | Gemini Live (3.1-flash-live-preview) | ~$0.0035/minute audio | 20-min session = ~$0.07 |
853: | Gemini Flash (background agent) | ~$0.0005/minute | Post-session analysis = ~$0.01 |
854: | Web search (via function) | ~$0.005/query | Rare use |
855: | **Daily** (1 session, 20 min) | ~$0.08 | |
856: | **Monthly** (30 sessions) | ~$2.40 | |
857: | **Your $300 credit** | Lasts ~10+ years at this rate | |
858: 
859: **You have MORE than enough credits.** The $300 is for the entire Google Cloud project, not just this. But even if you use 10x more, you are covered for months.
860: 
861: ---
862: 
863: ## TROUBLESHOOTING FOR CODING AGENT
864: 
865: **Problem: AI voice sounds robotic/foreign in Sinhala**
866: - Solution: Ensure `language_code: "si"` is set in speech_config
867: - Try voice names: `"Kore"`, `"Fenrir"`, `"Puck"`
868: - The 3.1 model should handle Sinhala natively. If not, check your Vertex AI region has the latest model.
869: 
870: **Problem: Function calls not working**
871: - Solution: Ensure `tools` is passed in `LiveConnectConfig`, not in the model path
872: - Ensure function names match exactly (case-sensitive)
873: - Ensure you send function call RESULTS back via `send_client_content`
874: 
875: **Problem: MediaPipe not detecting faces**
876: - Solution: Check camera permissions
877: - Ensure `minDetectionConfidence` is not too high (0.5 is good)
878: - Test on good lighting
879: 
880: **Problem: Background agent not updating memory**
881: - Solution: Ensure the session recording is saved locally first, then uploaded
882: - Check that the background agent has access to the files
883: - Verify the JSON output is valid
884: 
885: **Problem: AI forgets between sessions**
886: - Solution: Ensure memory injection happens BEFORE `client.aio.live.connect()`
887: - The memory must be in the `system_instruction` at connection time
888: - You cannot update system prompt mid-session safely
889: 
890: ---
891: 
892: *Document: AURA Complete Vertex AI Implementation Guide*
893: *For: Coding Agent | Single-Screen Conversational AI*
894: *Model: gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview via Vertex AI*
895: *Language: Sinhala (si) | Colombo dialect*

(End of file - total 895 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: // lib/wav_header.dart
2: import 'dart:typed_data';
3: 
4: /// PCM 오디오 데이터에 WAV 헤더를 추가하여 완전한 WAV 파일 바이트를 생성합니다.
5: ///
6: /// [pcmBytes] 순수 PCM 오디오 데이터입니다.
7: /// [sampleRate] 오디오의 샘플링 레이트입니다 (예: 24000).
8: /// [numChannels] 채널 수입니다 (1=모노, 2=스테레오).
9: /// [bitsPerSample] 샘플당 비트 수입니다 (일반적으로 16).
10: Uint8List addWavHeader(
11:   Uint8List pcmBytes, {
12:   required int sampleRate,
13:   int numChannels = 1,
14:   int bitsPerSample = 16,
15: }) {
16:   final byteRate = (sampleRate * numChannels * bitsPerSample) ~/ 8;
17:   final blockAlign = (numChannels * bitsPerSample) ~/ 8;
18:   final pcmLength = pcmBytes.length;
19:   final totalLength = pcmLength + 36;
20: 
21:   final header = ByteData(44);
22: 
23:   // RIFF chunk descriptor
24:   header.setUint8(0, 0x52); // 'R'
25:   header.setUint8(1, 0x49); // 'I'
26:   header.setUint8(2, 0x46); // 'F'
27:   header.setUint8(3, 0x46); // 'F'
28:   header.setUint32(4, totalLength, Endian.little);
29:   header.setUint8(8, 0x57); // 'W'
30:   header.setUint8(9, 0x41); // 'A'
31:   header.setUint8(10, 0x56); // 'V'
32:   header.setUint8(11, 0x45); // 'E'
33: 
34:   // "fmt " sub-chunk
35:   header.setUint8(12, 0x66); // 'f'
36:   header.setUint8(13, 0x6d); // 'm'
37:   header.setUint8(14, 0x74); // 't'
38:   header.setUint8(15, 0x20); // ' '
39:   header.setUint32(16, 16, Endian.little); // Sub-chunk1Size for PCM
40:   header.setUint16(20, 1, Endian.little); // AudioFormat, 1 for PCM
41:   header.setUint16(22, numChannels, Endian.little);
42:   header.setUint32(24, sampleRate, Endian.little);
43:   header.setUint32(28, byteRate, Endian.little);
44:   header.setUint16(32, blockAlign, Endian.little);
45:   header.setUint16(34, bitsPerSample, Endian.little);
46: 
47:   // "data" sub-chunk
48:   header.setUint8(36, 0x64); // 'd'
49:   header.setUint8(37, 0x61); // 'a'
50:   header.setUint8(38, 0x74); // 't'
51:   header.setUint8(39, 0x61); // 'a'
52:   header.setUint32(40, pcmLength, Endian.little);
53: 
54:   // WAV 헤더와 PCM 데이터를 합칩니다.
55:   final wavBytes = Uint8List(44 + pcmLength);
56:   wavBytes.setRange(0, 44, header.buffer.asUint8List());
57:   wavBytes.setRange(44, wavBytes.length, pcmBytes);
58: 
59:   return wavBytes;
60: }

(End of file - total 60 lines)
</content>
```

  id: json['id'] as String?,

  partialArgs: (json['partial_args'] as List<dynamic>?)

  willContinue: json['will_continue'] as bool?,
);
```

{
  "filePath": "/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/lib/data/services/gemini_live_fork/lib/src/utils/wav_header.dart",
  "content": "import 'dart:typed_data';\n\n/// Adds a WAV header to raw PCM audio data.\n///\n/// [pcmBytes] Raw PCM audio data.\n/// [sampleRate] Sampling rate (e.g., 24000).\n/// [numChannels] Number of channels (1=mono, 2=stereo).\n/// [bitsPerSample] Bits per sample (typically 16).\nUint8List addWavHeader(\n  Uint8List pcmBytes, {\n  required int sampleRate,\n  int numChannels = 1,\n  int bitsPerSample = 16,\n}) {\n  final byteRate = (sampleRate * numChannels * bitsPerSample) ~/ 8;\n  final blockAlign = (numChannels * bitsPerSample) ~/ 8;\n  final pcmLength = pcmBytes.length;\n  final totalLength = pcmLength + 36;\n\n  final header = ByteData(44);\n\n  // RIFF chunk descriptor\n  header.setUint8(0, 0x52); // 'R'\n  header.setUint8(1, 0x49); // 'I'\n  header.setUint8(2, 0x46); // 'F'\n  header.setUint8(3, 0x46); // 'F'\n  header.setUint32(4, totalLength, Endian.little);\n  header.setUint8(8, 0x57); // 'W'\n  header.setUint8(9, 0x41); // 'A'\n  header.setUint8(10, 0x56); // 'V'\n  header.setUint8(11, 0x45); // 'E'\n\n  // \"fmt \" sub-chunk\n  header.setUint8(12, 0x66); // 'f'\n  header.setUint8(13, 0x6d); // 'm'\n  header.setUint8(14, 0x74); // 't'\n  header.setUint8(15, 0x20); // ' '\n  header.setUint32(16, 16, Endian.little); // Sub-chunk1Size for PCM\n  header.setUint16(20, 1, Endian.little); // AudioFormat, 1 for PCM\n  header.setUint16(22, numChannels, Endian.little);\n  header.setUint32(24, sampleRate, Endian.little);\n  header.setUint32(28, byteRate, Endian.little);\n  header.setUint16(32, blockAlign, Endian.little);\n  header.setUint16(34, bitsPerSample, Endian.little);\n\n  // \"data\" sub-chunk\n  header.setUint8(36, 0x64); // 'd'\n  header.setUint8(37, 0x61); // 'a'\n  header.setUint8(38, 0x74); // 't'\n  header.setUint8(39, 0x61); // 'a'\n  header.setUint32(40, pcmLength, Endian.little);\n\n  // Combine header + PCM data\n  final wavBytes = Uint8List(44 + pcmLength);\n  wavBytes.setRange(0, 44, header.buffer.asUint8List());\n  wavBytes.setRange(44, wavBytes.length, pcmBytes);\n\n  return wavBytes;\n}\n"
}

baseUrl;\n\n  /// API version used by the live module.\n  final String apiVersion;\n\n  /// An optional custom HTTP client for making network requests.\n  final http.Client? httpClient;\n\n  /// The internal low-level client for making HTTP API requests.\n  late final ApiClient _apiClient;\n\n  /// Provides access to the live, streaming services of the API.\n  late final LiveService live;\n\n  GoogleGenAI({\n    required this.accessToken,\n    required this.projectId,\n    required this.location,\n    this.baseUrl,\n    this.httpClient,\n    this.apiVersion = 'v1beta1',\n  }) {\n    _apiClient = ApiClient(\n      accessToken: accessToken,\n      projectId: projectId,\n      location: location,\n      baseUrl: baseUrl,\n      apiVersion: apiVersion,\n      httpClient: httpClient,\n    );\n\n    live = LiveService(\n      accessToken: accessToken,\n      projectId: projectId,\n      location: location,\n      baseUrl: baseUrl,\n      apiVersion: apiVersion,\n    );\n  }\n\n  /// Releases any resources held by the client.\n  void close() {\n    _apiClient.close();\n  }\n}\n"
}

  "content": "import 'dart:convert';\n\nimport 'package:http/http.dart' as http;\n\n/// Low-level HTTP client for Vertex AI REST API calls.\n///\n/// Uses OAuth2 Bearer token instead of API key.\n/// Endpoints are constructed for the Vertex AI platform.\nclass ApiClient {\n  /// OAuth2 Bearer access token.\n  final String accessToken;\n\n  /// Custom base URL override.\n  final String? baseUrl;\n\n  /// The Google Cloud project ID.\n  final String projectId;\n\n  /// The Vertex AI location/region.\n  final String location;\n\n  /// The API version string (e.g., 'v1beta1').\n  final String apiVersion;\n\n  final http.Client _httpClient;\n\n  ApiClient({\n    required this.accessToken,\n    required this.projectId,\n    required this.location,\n    this.baseUrl,\n    this.apiVersion = 'v1beta1',\n    http.Client? httpClient,\n  }) : _httpClient = httpClient ?? http.Client();\n\n  /// Constructs the full request URI for a given API path.\n  ///\n  /// Uses the Vertex AI endpoint with Bearer token auth.\n  Uri _buildUri(String path) {\n    final effectiveBaseUrl =\n        baseUrl ?? 'https://$location-aiplatform.googleapis.com';\n    // Vertex AI REST uses the pattern:\n    // https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/{apiVersion}/projects/{projectId}/locations/{location}/{path}\n    return Uri.parse(\n      '$effectiveBaseUrl/$apiVersion/$path',\n    );\n  }\n\n  /// Sends a POST request with OAuth2 Bearer token auth.\n  Future<Map<String, dynamic>> post(\n    String path,\n    Map<String, dynamic> body,\n  ) async {\n    final uri = _buildUri(path);\n    final response = await _httpClient.post(\n      uri,\n      headers: {\n        'Content-Type': 'application/json',\n        'Authorization': 'Bearer $accessToken',\n        'X-Goog-User-Project': projectId,\n      },\n      body: jsonEncode(body),\n    );\n\n    if (response.statusCode >= 200 && response.statusCode < 300) {\n      return jsonDecode(response.body) as Map<String, dynamic>;\n    } else {\n      throw Exception('API Error: ${response.statusCode} ${response.body}');\n    }\n  }\n\n  /// Closes the underlying HTTP client.\n  void close() {\n    _httpClient.close();\n  }\n}\n"
}

(!blob.mimeType.startsWith(expectedPrefix)) {\n      throw ArgumentError('Unsupported mime type: ${blob.mimeType}');\n    }\n  }\n\n  /// Handles incoming WebSocket data.\n  ///\n  /// Vertex AI sends ALL messages as **binary** WebSocket frames (opcode 2).\n  /// - If the first byte is `{` (0x7b), the frame is JSON -> parse as LiveServerMessage\n  /// - Otherwise, the frame is raw audio PCM -> Vertex AI but handle gracefully)\n    String jsonData;\n    if (data is String) {\n      jsonData = data;\n    } else if (data is List<int>) {\n      jsonData = utf8.decode(data);\n    } else {\n }\n\n    try {\n      final json = jsonDecode(jsonData);\n      logger?.call('📥 Received JSON: $jsonData');\n      final message = LiveServerMessage.fromJson(json);\n      callbacks.onMessage?.call(message);\n    } catch (e, st) {\n      callbacks.onError?.call(e, st);\n    }\n  }\n\n  void handleWebSocketDataForTesting(dynamic data, LiveCallbacks callbacks) {\n    _handleWebSocketData(data, callbacks);\n  }\n\n  /// Establishes a WebSocket connection to the Vertex AI Gemini Live API.\n  Future<LiveSession> connect(LiveConnectParameters params) async {\n    // Vertex AI WebSocket endpoint — always uses BidiGenerateContent\n    final method = 'BidiGenerateContent';\n\n    final websocketUri = Uri(\n      scheme: 'wss',\n      host: '$location-aiplatform.googleapis.com',\n      path: '/ws/google.cloud.aiplatform.$apiVersion.LlmBidiService/$method',\n    );\n\n    final userAgent =\n        'google-genai-sdk/$_sdkVersion dart/${_dartVersionProvider()}';\n\n    logger?.call('🔌 Connecting to Vertex AI WebSocket at $websocketUri');\n\n    try {\n      // OAuth2 Bearer token + X-Goog-User-Project header for Vertex AI\n       'user-agent': userAgent,\n      };\n      final channel = await _connector(websocketUri, headers);\n      final session = LiveSession._(channel, logger: logger);\n      final setupCompleter =     } catch (e) {\n              // Ignore parsing errors during setup\n            }\n          }\n          _handleWebSocketData(data, params.callbacks);\n        },\n        onError: (error, stackTrace) {\n          if (!setupCompleter.isCompleted) {\n            setupCompleter.completeError(error, stackTrace);\n          }\n          params.callbacks.onError?.call(error, stackTrace);\n        },\n        onDone: () {\n          params.callbacks.onClose?.call(\n            channel.closeCode,\n            channel.closeReason,\n          );\n          streamSubscription?.cancel();\n        },\n        cancelOnError: true,\n      );\n\n      params.callbacks.onOpen?.call();\n      final setupMessage = LiveService.buildSetupMessage(params);\n      session.sendMessage(setupMessage);\n\n );\n        },\n      );\n\n      return session;\n    } catch (e) {\n      logger?.call(\"Failed to connect or setup WebSocket: $e\");\n      rethrow;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/// Exception for timeout errors.\nclass TimeoutException implements Exception {\n  final String message;\n  TimeoutException(this.message);\n  @override\n  String toString() => \"TimeoutException: $message\";\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Live Session\n// ============================================================================\n\n/// Represents an active Live API session.\nclass LiveSession {\n  final WebSocketChannel _channel;\n  final void Function(String)? _logger;\n\n  LiveSession._(this._channel, {void Function(String)? logger})\n    : _logger = logger;\n\n  factory LiveSession.forTesting(WebSocketChannel channel) =>\n      LiveSession._(channel);\n\n  /// Sends a message to the server as JSON text.\n  void sendMessage(LiveClientMessage message) {\n    if (_channel.closeCode != null) {\n      _logger?.call(\n        '⚠️ Warning: Attempted to send a message on a closed WebSocket channel.',\n      );\n      return;\n    }\n    final jsonString = jsonEncode(message.toJson());\n    _logger?.call('📤 Sending: $jsonString');\n    _channel.sink.add(jsonString);\n  }\n\n  /// Sends text content to the server.\n  void sendText(String text) {\n    final message = LiveClientMessage(\n      clientContent: LiveClientContent(\n        turns: [\n          Content(parts: [Part(text: text)]),\n        ],\n        turnComplete: true,\n      ),\n    );\n    sendMessage(message);\n  }\n\n  /// Sends audio data to the server (base64-encoded in JSON).\n  void sendAudio(List<int> audioBytes) {\n    final base64Audio = base64Encode(audioBytes);\n    final message = LiveClientMessage(\n      realtimeInput: LiveClientRealtimeInput(\n        audio: Blob(mimeType: 'audio/pcm', data: base64Audio),\n      ),\n    );\n    sendMessage(message);\n  }\n\n  /// Sends video data to the server.\n  void sendVideo(List<int> videoBytes, {String mimeType = 'image/jpeg'}) {\n    final base64Video = base64Encode(videoBytes);\n    LiveService.validateRealtimeBlob(\n      Blob(mimeType: mimeType, data: base64Video),\n      expectedPrefix: 'image/',\n    );\n    final message = LiveClientMessage(\n      realtimeInput: LiveClientRealtimeInput(\n        video: Blob(mimeType: mimeType, data: base64Video),\n      ),\n    );\n    sendMessage(message);\n  }\n\n  /// Sends client content with optional turns and turn completion flag.\n  void sendClientContent({List<Content>? turns, bool turnComplete = true}) {\n    final message = LiveClientMessage(\n      clientContent: LiveClientContent(\n        turns: turns,\n        turnComplete: turnComplete,\n      ),\n    );\n    sendMessage(message);\n  }\n\n  /// Sends realtime input with various media types.\n  void sendRealtimeInput({\n    List<Blob>? mediaChunks,\n    Blob? audio,\n    Blob? video,\n    String? text,\n    bool? audioStreamEnd,\n    bool? activityStart,\n    bool? activityEnd,\n  }) {\n    if (audio != null) {\n      LiveService.validateRealtimeBlob(audio, expectedPrefix: 'audio/');\n    }\n    if (video != null) {\n      LiveService.validateRealtimeBlob(video, expectedPrefix: 'image/');\n    }\n\n    LiveClientRealtimeInput? realtimeInput;\n\n    if (mediaChunks != null ||\n        audio != null ||\n        video != null ||\n        text != null ||\n        audioStreamEnd != null ||\n text,\n        audioStreamEnd: audioStreamEnd,\n        activityStart: activityStart == true ? ActivityEnd() : null,\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (realtimeInput != null) {\n      final message = LiveClientMessage(realtimeInput: realtimeInput);\n      sendMessage(message);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /// Sends media chunks for realtime input.\n  void sendMediaChunks(List<Blob> mediaChunks) {\n    final message = LiveClientMessage(\n      realtimeInput: LiveClientRealtimeInput(mediaChunks: mediaChunks),\n    );\n    sendMessage(message);\n  }\n\n  /// Sends a signal indicating the end of audio stream.\n  void sendAudioStreamEnd() {\n    final message = LiveClientMessage(\n      realtimeInput: LiveClientRealtimeInput(audioStreamEnd: true),\n    );\n    sendMessage(message);\n  }\n\n  /// Sends realtime text input.\n  void sendRealtimeText(String text) {\n    final message = LiveClientMessage(\n      realtimeInput: LiveClientRealtimeInput(text: text),\n    );\n    sendMessage(message);\n  }\n\n  /// Marks the start of user activity (when automatic VAD is disabled).\n  void sendActivityStart() {\n    final message = LiveClientMessage(\n      realtimeInput: LiveClientRealtimeInput(activityStart: ActivityStart()),\n    );\n    sendMessage(message);\n  }\n\n  /// Marks the end of user activity (when automatic VAD is disabled).\n  void sendActivityEnd() {\n    final message = LiveClientMessage(\n      realtimeInput: LiveClientRealtimeInput(activityEnd: ActivityEnd()),\n    );\n    sendMessage(message);\n  }\n\n  /// Sends a tool response to the server.\n  void sendToolResponse({required List<FunctionResponse> functionResponses}) {\n    final validatedResponses = LiveService.validateFunctionResponses(\n      functionResponses,\n    );\n    final message = LiveClientMessage(\n      toolResponse: LiveClientToolResponse(\n        functionResponses: validatedResponses,\n      ),\n    );\n    sendMessage(message);\n  }\n\n  /// Sends a single function response.\n  void sendFunctionResponse({\n    required String id,\n    required String name,\n    required Map<String, dynamic> response,\n  }) {\n    sendToolResponse(\n      functionResponses: [\n        FunctionResponse(id: id, name: name, response: response),\n => _channel.closeCode != null;\n}\n"

LiveServerToolCallCancellation? toolCallCancellation;\n  final LiveServerGoAway? goAway;\n  final LiveServerSessionResumptionUpdate? sessionResumptionUpdate;\n  final VoiceActivityDetectionSignal? part.text;\n      if (text == null) continue;\n      if (part.thought == true) continue;\n      chunks.add(text);\n    }\n    return chunks.isEmpty ? null : chunks.join();\n  }\n\n  /// The concatenated inline binary payload emitted in the current server turn.\n  String? get data {\n    final parts = serverContent?.modelTurn?.parts;\n    if (parts == null || parts.isEmpty) return null;\n this.activityEnd,\n  });\n}\n\n/// Parameters for sending tool results back to the model.\nclass LiveSendToolResponseParameters {\n  final List<FunctionResponse> functionResponses;\n\n  LiveSendToolResponseParameters({required this.functionResponses});\n}\n"

## Goal
- Implement Vertex AI Gemini Live voice for Handa (stroke therapy app) via OAuth2 WebSocket, consuming $300 GCP credits through the correct Vertex AI endpoint

## Constraints & Preferences

- ADC OAuth2 Bearer token auth only — API keys & Express mode both failed
