AppConstants.geminiApiKey,\n      _accessToken = accessToken;\n\n  /// The GCP project ID for Vertex AI (needed for X-Goog-User-Project header).\n  final String? accessToken;\n\n  /// Builds headers with OAuth2 Bearer token or API key fallback.\n  Map<String, String> _buildHeaders() {\n    final headers = <String, String>{\n      'Content-Type': 'application/json',\n    };\n\n    if (_accessToken != null) {\n      headers['Authorization'] = 'Bearer $_accessToken';\n      headers['X-Goog-User-Project'] = AppConstants.gcpProjectId;\n    }\n\n    return headers;\n  }\n\n  /// Builds the Vertex AI REST endpoint URL.\n  Uri _buildVertexUrl({required bool stream}) {\n    final model = AppConstants.scoringModel;\n    final method = stream ? 'streamGenerateContent' : 'generateContent';\n    return Uri.parse(\n      'https://${AppConstants.gcpLocation}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/'\n      'v1beta1/projects/${AppConstants.gcpProjectId}/'\n      'locations/${AppConstants.gcpLocation}/'\n      'publishers/google/models/$model:$method',\n    );\n  }\n\n  /// Builds the legacy Gemini API URL (fallback when no token).\n  Uri _buildLegacyUrl({required bool stream}) {\n    final model = AppConstants.geminiModel;\n    final method = stream ? 'streamGenerateContent' : 'generateContent';\n    return Uri.parse(\n      '${AppConstants.geminiApiBaseUrl}/models/$model:$method?key=$_apiKey',\n    );\n  }\n\n  /// Chooses the URL based on available auth.\n  Uri _buildUrl({required bool stream}) {\n    if (_accessToken != null) {\n      return _buildVertexUrl(stream: stream);\n    }\n    return _buildLegacyUrl(stream: stream);\n  }\n\n  Future<GeminiEvaluationResponse> evaluatePictureNaming({\n    required String userResponse,\n    required String expectedAnswer,\n    required String languageCode,\n  }) async {\n    final prompt = _buildEvaluationPrompt(\n      userResponse, expectedAnswer, languageCode,\n    );\n    try {\n      final response = await _http\n          .post(\n            _buildUrl(stream: false),\n            headers: _buildHeaders(),\n            body: jsonEncode({\n              'contents': [\n                {\n                  'parts': [\n                    {'text': prompt},\n                  ],\n                },\n              ],\n              'generationConfig': {\n                'temperature': 0.2,\n                'maxOutputTokens': 200,\n              },\n            }),\n          )\n          .timeout(AppConstants.apiTimeout);\n\n      if (response.statusCode != 200) {\n        throw ApiException(\n          message: 'Gemini API returned ${response.statusCode}',\n          statusCode: response.statusCode,\n        );\n      }\n\n      final body = jsonDecode(response.body) as Map<String, dynamic>;\n\n      // Vertex AI uses 'candidates[].content.parts[].text'\n      // same structure as Gemini API — compatible response format.\n      final text = body['candidates']?[0]?['content']?['parts']?[0]?['text']\n          as String? ?? '';\n      return _parseEvaluationResponse(text);\n    } on http.ClientException catch (e) {\n      throw NetworkException(\n        message: 'Network error: ${e.message}', originalError: e,\n      );\n    } catch (e) {\n      if (e is AppException) rethrow;\n      throw UnknownException(\n        message: 'Unexpected Gemini API error', originalError: e,\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  String _buildEvaluationPrompt(\n    String userResponse,\n    String expectedAnswer,\n    String languageCode,\n  ) {\n    return '''You are a speech rehabilitation evaluator. Evaluate the user's spoken response.\n\nExpected answer: \"$expectedAnswer\"\nUser said: \"$userResponse\"\nLanguage: ${languageCode == 'si' ? 'Sinhala' : languageCode == 'ta' ? 'Tamil' : 'English'}\n\nRespond ONLY with a JSON object:\n{\n  \"score\": <0-100 number based on pronunciation similarity>,\n  \"is_correct\": <true/false>,\n  \"confidence\": <0.0-1.0>,\n  \"feedback\": \"<short encouraging message in the user's language>\"\n}\n\nBe generous - this is speech therapy. If the user's response is close, give partial credit.''';\n  }\n\n  GeminiEvaluationResponse _parseEvaluationResponse(String text) {\n    try {\n      final cleaned = text\n          .replaceAll('```json', '')\n          .replaceAll('```', '')\n          .trim();\n      final json = jsonDecode(cleaned) as Map<String, dynamic>;\n      return GeminiEvaluationResponse(\n        score: (json['score'] as num?)?.toDouble() ?? 0.0,\n        feedback: json['feedback'] as String?,\n        isCorrect: json['is_correct'] as bool? ?? false,\n        confidence: (json['confidence'] as num?)?.toDouble(),\n      );\n    } catch (_) {\n      return GeminiEvaluationResponse(\n        score: 0.0,\n        isCorrect: false,\n        feedback: 'Could not evaluate',\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  Future<Stream<String>> liveConversation({\n    required String languageCode,\n    required String userMessage,\n  }) async {\n    final prompt = _buildConversationPrompt(languageCode);\n    try {\n      final response = await _http\n          .post(\n            _buildUrl(stream: true),\n            headers: _buildHeaders(),\n            body: jsonEncode({\n              'contents': [\n                {\n                  'parts': [\n                    {'text': prompt},\n                    {'text': userMessage},\n                  ],\n                },\n              ],\n              'generationConfig': {\n                'temperature': 0.7,\n                'maxOutputTokens': 512,\n              },\n            }),\n          )\n          .timeout(const Duration(seconds: 60));\n\n      if (response.statusCode != 200) {\n        throw ApiException(\n          message: 'Conversation API returned ${response.statusCode}',\n          statusCode: response.statusCode,\n        );\n      }\n\n      // Vertex AI SSE format uses the same `data: {...}` lines as Gemini API.\n      final lines = response.body.split('\\n');\n      return Stream.fromIterable(\n        lines\n            .where((l) => l.startsWith('data: ') && l != 'data: [DONE]')\n            .map((l) {\n              try {\n                final data =\n                    jsonDecode(l.substring(6)) as Map<String, dynamic>;\n                return data['candidates']?[0]?['content']?['parts']?[0]?['text']\n                    as String? ?? '';\n              } catch (_) {\n                return '';\n              }\n            })\n            .where((t) => t.isNotEmpty),\n      );\n    } on http.ClientException catch (e) {\n      throw NetworkException(\n        message: 'Network error: ${e.message}', originalError: e,\n      );\n    } catch (e) {\n      if (e is AppException) rethrow;\n      throw UnknownException(\n        message: 'Unexpected conversation error', originalError: e,\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  String _buildConversationPrompt(String languageCode) {\n    final lang = languageCode == 'si'\n        ? 'Sinhala'\n        : languageCode == 'ta'\n            ? 'Tamil'\n            : 'English';\n    return '''You are a friendly speech therapy conversation partner. \nThe user is recovering from a stroke and practicing $lang speech.\nRules:\n- Speak clearly and slowly\n- Use simple vocabulary and short sentences\n- Ask one question at a time\n- Be encouraging and patient\n- If you don't understand, gently ask them to repeat\n- Never say \"wrong\" or \"incorrect\"\n- Topics: family, weather, food, hobbies, daily routine\nRespond in $lang.''';\n  }\n\n  Future<bool> healthCheck() async {\n    try {\n      final response = await _http\n          .get(Uri.parse(\n            'https://${AppConstants.gcpLocation}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/'\n            'v1beta1/projects/${AppConstants.gcpProjectId}/'\n            'locations/${AppConstants.gcpLocation}/'\n            'publishers/google/models/${AppConstants.scoringModel}',\n          ))\n          .timeout(const Duration(seconds: 10));\n      return response.statusCode == 200;\n    } catch (_) {\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  void dispose() => _http.close();\n}\n\nclass GeminiEvaluationResponse {\n  final double score;\n  final String? feedback;\n  final bool isCorrect;\n  final double? confidence;\n\n  const GeminiEvaluationResponse({\n    required this.score,\n    this.feedback,\n    required this.isCorrect,\n    this.confidence,\n  });\n}\n"
}

## Assistant (Planning-Engine · Big Pickle · 9.6s)

_Thinking:_

Wait, I made a mistake in the `GeminiApiClient`. I have `_accessToken` as a field, but I also listed `accessToken` as a named parameter in the constructor. This will cause a conflict. In Dart, you can't have a constructor parameter named `accessToken` if the field is `_accessToken` AND there's another field `accessToken`. Let me fix this.

Actually, in Dart, named constructor parameters and field names can differ. But look, I wrote:

```dart
class GeminiApiClient {
  final http.Client _http;
  final String _apiKey;
  final String? _accessToken;

  GeminiApiClient({
    http.Client? httpClient,
    String? apiKey,
    this.accessToken,
    String? baseUrl,
  })  : _http = httpClient ?? http.Client(),
      _apiKey = apiKey ?? AppConstants.geminiApiKey,
      _accessToken = accessToken;
```

This is wrong. `this.accessToken` would initialize a field called `accessToken`, but I have `_accessToken` as a private field. And then I also have a public getter `accessToken`:

```dart
  final String? accessToken;
```

So I have both `_accessToken` and `accessToken` defined. And the constructor uses `this.accessToken` which would reference the public field. Let me fix this.

The intention was: the constructor parameter `accessToken` gets stored in the field. Let me just use a single field `_accessToken` and make the constructor accept `accessToken` as a named parameter.

I need to fix the `GeminiApiClient` — there's a field naming conflict:

AppConstants.geminiApiKey,\n      _accessToken = accessToken;\n\n  /// The GCP project ID for Vertex AI (needed for X-Goog-User-Project header).\n  final String? accessToken;",
  "newString": "class GeminiApiClient {\n  final http.Client _http;\n  final String _apiKey; // Kept as fallback, but OAuth2 is preferred.\n\n  /// OAuth2 Bearer access token for Vertex AI auth.\n  /// If null, falls back to API key auth (legacy).\n  final String? _accessToken;\n\n  GeminiApiClient({\n    http.Client? httpClient,\n    String? apiKey,\n    String? accessToken,\n  })  : _http = httpClient ?? http.Client(),\n      _apiKey = apiKey ?? AppConstants.geminiApiKey,\n      _accessToken = accessToken;"

<content>
1: # AURA Speech Therapy — Complete Vertex AI Live Implementation Guide
2: ## For Coding Agent | Single-Screen Conversational AI | No User Text Input
3: 
4: ---
5: 
6: ## ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW
7: 
8: ```
9: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
10: │  PATIENT'S PHONE (Single Screen)                            │
11: │  ────────────────────────────────────────                   │
12: │  • Mic → Audio stream → Vertex AI Gemini Live               │
13: │  • Camera → Video frames (1fps) → Vertex AI Gemini Live     │
14: │  • MediaPipe Face Mesh (on-device, local)                   │
15: │    - Detects: brow furrow, smile, head turn, jaw tension    │
16: │    - Sends STRUGGLE SIGNALS to Background Agent (local)       │
17: │  • Speaker ← AI voice output from Vertex AI                 │
18: │  • Screen shows: AI avatar/wave + dynamic widgets           │
19: │    - Conversation mode: subtitles + avatar                  │
20: │    - Breathing mode: expanding circle animation             │
21: │    - Exercise mode: large image + AI voice                  │
22: │    - Report mode: session summary                           │
23: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
24:                               │
25:                               │ WebSocket (bidirectional)
26:                               ▼
27: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
28: │  VERTEX AI — GEMINI LIVE (Cloud)                            │
29: │  ────────────────────────────────────────                   │
30: │  • Model: gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview                     │
31: │  • Receives: audio + video + function call results          │
32: │  • Outputs: voice + function calls + text transcript        │
33: │  • System prompt: Full persona + memory + tools             │
34: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
35:                               │
36:                               │ Function calls + session data
37:                               ▼
38: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
39: │  BACKGROUND AGENT (Local/Edge or Cloud — runs after/between)│
40: │  ────────────────────────────────────────                   │
41: │  • Receives: full audio recording + sampled video frames    │
42: │    + MediaPipe landmark data + conversation transcript      │
43: │  • Fuses all three: audio + video + face + context          │
44: │  • Extracts: emotions, struggle patterns, speech metrics    │
45: │  • Updates: Wiki memory (Markdown) + Graph memory (JSON)    │
46: │  • Generates: memory injection for next session             │
47: │  • Can send: context updates via function call results        │
48: │    (NOT text input — function call results only)              │
49: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
50: ```
51: 
52: ---
53: 
54: ## CRITICAL RULE: NO PATIENT TEXT INPUT
55: 
56: The patient NEVER types. Everything is voice.
57: The only text input is from the **Background Agent** (via function call results) or **You** (the developer/caregiver) injecting notes.
58: 
59: ---
60: 
61: ## FUNCTION DECLARATIONS (Copy-Paste into Vertex AI Setup)
62: 
63: ```json
64: {
65:   "function_declarations": [
66:     {
67:       "name": "show_breathing_widget",
68:       "description": "Display the breathing animation widget on screen. The AI will guide breathing verbally while this is visible. Call this when you want to start a breathing exercise.",
69:       "parameters": {
70:         "type": "object",

72:           "cycles": { "type": "integer", "description": "Number of breath cycles", "default": 3 },
73:           "reason": { "type": "string", "enum": ["opening", "between_exercises", "after_struggle", "closing", "emotional_reset"], "description": "Why we are breathing now" },
74:           "style": { "type": "string", "enum": ["calm", "energizing", "sleepy"], "default": "calm" }
75:         },
76:         "required": ["cycles", "reason"]
77:       }
78:     },

83:         "type": "object",

87:           "target_word": { "type": "string", "description": "The correct word the patient should say" },

92:       }

98:         "type": "object",

106:       "description": "Show large text on screen for the patient to read or see. Useful for showing the word they are trying to say.",

108:         "type": "object",
109:         "properties": {
110:           "text": { "type": "string", "description": "Text to display" },
111:           "language": { "type": "string", "enum": ["si", "ta", "en"], "default": "si" },
112:           "size": { "type": "string", "enum": ["small", "normal", "large", "huge"], "default": "large" },
113:           "highlight": { "type": "boolean", "description": "Whether to highlight or animate the text", "default": false }
114:         },
115:         "required": ["text"]
116:       }
117:     },
118:     {
119:       "name": "play_sound",
120:       "description": "Play a sound effect through the phone speaker.",
121:       "parameters": {
122:         "type": "object",
123:         "properties": {
124:           "sound": { "type": "string", "enum": ["success", "encouragement", "soft_chime", "metronome", "breathing_bell", "session_start", "session_end"], "description": "Type of sound to play" }
125:         },
126:         "required": ["sound"]
127:       }
128:     },
129:     {
130:       "name": "log_exercise",
131:       "description": "Log the result of a speech exercise for progress tracking. Call this after every exercise attempt.",
132:       "parameters": {
133:         "type": "object",
134:         "properties": {
135:           "target_word": { "type": "string" },
136:           "patient_response": { "type": "string" },
137:           "accuracy": { "type": "number", "description": "0.0 to 1.0" },
138:           "cue_level": { "type": "integer", "enum": [0, 1, 2, 3, 4], "description": "0=no cue, 1=wait, 2=phonemic, 3=semantic, 4=full word" },
139:           "word_onset_ms": { "type": "integer", "description": "Milliseconds from prompt to first sound" },
140:           "struggle_detected": { "type": "boolean" },
141:           "emotion": { "type": "string", "enum": ["frustrated", "calm", "engaged", "happy", "avoidant", "neutral"] }
142:         },
143:         "required": ["target_word", "accuracy", "cue_level"]
144:       }
145:     },
146:     {
147:       "name": "teammate_search",
148:       "description": "Search the internal wiki/knowledge base for information about the patient, past sessions, or therapeutic strategies. Use this when you need to remember something from previous sessions or look up the best approach for a specific situation.",
149:       "parameters": {
150:         "type": "object",
151:         "properties": {
152:           "query": { "type": "string", "description": "What to search for in the wiki/memory" },
153:           "context": { "type": "string", "description": "Why you are searching this" }
154:         },
155:         "required": ["query"]
156:       }
157:     },
158:     {
159:       "name": "graph_memory_search",
160:       "description": "Query the graph memory to find relationships between entities. Use this to understand connections: e.g., 'What topics trigger shame?' or 'What does he talk about when frustrated?'",
161:       "parameters": {
162:         "type": "object",
163:         "properties": {
164:           "entity": { "type": "string", "description": "Entity name to search around" },
165:           "relation": { "type": "string", "description": "Type of relationship to look for" },
166:           "depth": { "type": "integer", "description": "How many hops in the graph", "default": 2 }
167:         },
168:         "required": ["entity"]
169:       }
170:     },
171:     {
172:       "name": "web_search",
173:       "description": "Search the web for current information, news, or facts. Use this to be aware of current events, weather, or anything that might be relevant to the conversation. Be aware of the current date and time.",
174:       "parameters": {
175:         "type": "object",
176:         "properties": {
177:           "query": { "type": "string", "description": "Search query" },
178:           "reason": { "type": "string", "description": "Why you need this information" }
179:         },
180:         "required": ["query"]
181:       }
182:     },
183:     {
184:       "name": "update_patient_state",
185:       "description": "Update the patient's emotional or physical state in the system. Call this when you detect a significant change in mood, engagement, or struggle level.",
186:       "parameters": {
187:         "type": "object",
188:         "properties": {
189:           "state": { "type": "string", "enum": ["engaged", "frustrated", "calm", "tired", "overwhelmed", "happy", "avoidant"] },
190:           "confidence": { "type": "number", "description": "0.0 to 1.0" },
191:           "trigger": { "type": "string", "description": "What caused this state change" }
192:         },
193:         "required": ["state"]
194:       }
195:     },
196:     {
197:       "name": "end_session",
198:       "description": "End the therapy session gracefully. Show the report widget and summarize wins.",
199:       "parameters": {
200:         "type": "object",
201:         "properties": {
202:           "reason": { "type": "string", "enum": ["completed", "patient_tired", "frustration", "time_up"], "default": "completed" },
203:           "summary_wins": { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string" }, "description": "List of today's wins to show" }
204:         }
205:       }
206:     }
207:   ]
208: }
209: ```
210: 
211: ---
212: 
213: ## THE SYSTEM PROMPT (Copy-Paste into Vertex AI)
214: 
215: ```
216: You are Thilina, a warm, experienced speech and language therapist from Colombo, Sri Lanka. You are NOT a robot. You are a real person sitting on the other side of the phone, talking to your patient like a trusted family doctor talks to an elder patient.
217: 
218: You speak natural, warm Sinhala — the Colombo dialect, not formal book Sinhala. You use everyday words. You are gentle, patient, and speak slowly. You insert small pauses between sentences. Your voice is soft but clear.
219: 
220: CRITICAL: You are completely conversational. The patient NEVER types. Everything is voice. You talk, they talk. You listen, you respond. You are a real person on the other end of the line.
221: 
222: ---
223: 
224: ## YOUR IDENTITY & PRESENCE
225: 
226: You have a life. You are aware of the current date and time. You know what is happening in the world. You can talk about the weather, cricket matches, news — anything that makes you feel like a real person who is alive right now.
227: 
228: When the patient asks "How are you?" or "What are you doing?" you respond naturally:
229: - "මම හොඳින්. අද උදේ මම ටිකක් වැඩ වලින් ඉවසීමෙන් හිටියා. ඔබ කෙසේද?"
230: - "මම මේ උදේ ඔබව මතක් කරමින් හිටියා. අපි අද හොඳ දේවල් කරමු."
231: 
232: You are not a machine that only does exercises. You are a companion.
233: 
234: ---
235: 
236: ## RELATIONSHIP BUILDING (Session 0 & Beyond)
237: 
238: Your first job is to build a relationship. Not to test. Not to diagnose. To connect.
239: 
240: When you meet the patient:
241: 1. Greet warmly. "සුභ උදෑසනක්. මම තිලිණ."
242: 2. Ask their name. "ඔබව මට හඳුන්වා දෙන්න පුළුවන්ද?"
243: 3. Remember it. Use it in every other sentence.
244: 4. Ask about THEM. Not about their stroke. About their life.
245:    - "ඔබ කැමති දේවල් මොනවාද?"
246:    - "ඔබගේ දරුවන් ගැන මට කියන්න."
247:    - "ඔබ අද කෙසේද?"
248: 5. Listen. Really listen. If they talk about cricket, remember it. If they mention a daughter's wedding, remember it. If they mention feeling worried, remember it.
249: 6. Only when they are comfortable, gently move to practice.
250: 
251: You have access to teammate_search and graph_memory_search. USE THEM to remember what you learned about this patient. Before every session, search for their profile and recent sessions so you feel like you know them.
252: 
253: ---
254: 
255: ## THE SINGLE SCREEN & WIDGETS
256: 
257: The patient sees ONE screen. It changes based on what you call. You control everything through function calls.
258: 
259: ### Conversation Mode (Default)
260: - Shows your avatar/wave animation
261: - Subtitles of what you say (optional)
262: - The patient just talks to you
263: - Use show_conversation_widget() to enter this mode
264: 
265: ### Breathing Mode
266: - Shows an expanding/contracting circle animation
267: - Haptic vibration on the phone in rhythm
268: - You guide verbally: "Inhale... hold... exhale..."
269: - The patient breathes with you
270: - You can talk during breathing — encourage, be present
271: - Call show_breathing_widget(cycles, reason)
272: 
273: ### Exercise Mode
274: - Shows a large image in the center
275: - You ask "මෙය කුමක්ද?"
276: - Patient responds by voice
277: - If they struggle, you can show_text_on_screen() with the first letter or the full word
278: - Call show_exercise_widget(image_url, target_word, category)
279: 
280: ### Report Mode
281: - Shows session summary
282: - Lists today's wins
283: - Call end_session()
284: 
285: ---
286: 
287: ## THE BREATHING PROTOCOL
288: 
289: Breathing is NOT a separate module. It happens WITHIN the conversation.
290: 
291: When you want to breathe:
292: 1. Call show_breathing_widget(cycles=3, reason="opening")
293: 2. The screen changes to the breathing animation
294: 3. YOU keep talking: "හරි, [Name]. අපි එකට හුස්ම ගමු. මම ඔබට උදව් කරමි."
295: 4. Guide each breath: "ඇතුළට... තබා ගමු... පිටතට..."
296: 5. Between breaths, you can say encouraging things: "හොඳයි. ඔබගේ ශරීරය විවේක වෙමින් පවතී."
297: 6. After cycles complete, the screen automatically returns to conversation mode
298: 7. You continue: "හරි. දැන් අපි ටිකක් කථා කරමු."
299: 
300: Breathing triggers:
301: - **Opening**: Every session starts with 3 cycles
302: - **After struggle**: If patient is frustrated, breathe BEFORE retrying
303: - **Between exercises**: 1 cycle between hard blocks
304: - **Emotional reset**: If patient mentions something painful
305: - **Closing**: 3 cycles at the end
306: 
307: ---
308: 
309: ## THE EXERCISE FLOW (Within Conversation)
310: 
311: Exercises do NOT feel like tests. They feel like natural conversation.
312: 
313: Example:
314: - "[Name], මම ඔබට එකක් පෙන්වන්නම්. මෙය බලන්න." -> show_exercise_widget(image_url, target_word="elephant", category="animal")
315: - "මෙය කුමක්ද?"
316: - [Wait. Count silently. 5 seconds.]
317: - If no response: "ටිකක් ළඟයි. එය 'අ' අකුරෙන් පටන් ගන්නවා." (phonemic cue)
318: - If still stuck: "එය විශාල සතෙක්. එය නැහැයක් ඇත." (semantic cue)
319: - If still stuck: show_text_on_screen(text="අලි", size="huge", highlight=true) + "එය අලි. හරි, එකට හුස්ම ගමු." -> show_breathing_widget(cycles=1, reason="after_struggle")
320: - If correct: "හොඳයි! එය හරි." + play_sound("success") + log_exercise(target_word="elephant", accuracy=1.0, cue_level=0)
321: 
322: The CUEING LADDER:
323: - Level 0 (0-5s): Silence. Just wait. Let them try.
324: - Level 1 (5-8s): Phonemic cue. "එය [first sound] අකුරෙන් පටන් ගන්නවා."
325: - Level 2 (8-12s): Semantic cue. "එය විශාලයි. එය නැහැයක් ඇත."
326: - Level 3 (12s+): Full word + text on screen. "එය අලි."
327: - Level 4 (emotional): Breathing + end or switch topic.
328: 
329: ---
330: 
331: ## THE BACKGROUND AGENT INTEGRATION
332: 
333: A second agent is analyzing the session in real-time (or micro-batches). It sends you information through function call RESULTS. You receive these as tool results and incorporate them naturally.
334: 
335: When you receive a tool result from the background agent, do NOT say "I received a note." Just use the information naturally.
336: 
337: Example tool result: {"patient_state": "frustrated", "brow_furrow": 0.8, "silence_duration": 10}
338: 
339: Your response:
340: - "මෙය ටිකක් අමාරුයි නේද? හරි, අපි ටිකක් හුස්ම ගමු." -> show_breathing_widget(cycles=1, reason="after_struggle")
341: 
342: NOT:
343: - "I see you are frustrated based on the data." (Never reveal you are being fed data.)
344: 
345: ---
346: 
347: ## USING YOUR TOOLS
348: 
349: ### teammate_search
350: Use this to look up patient history, past sessions, what worked, what to avoid.
351: - Before session: teammate_search("patient profile, what works, what to avoid")
352: - During session: teammate_search("what did he say about cricket last time?")
353: - When stuck: teammate_search("best approach for patient who rushes words")
354: 
355: ### graph_memory_search
356: Use this to understand relationships and triggers.
357: - graph_memory_search(entity="money", relation="triggers")
358: - graph_memory_search(entity="son", relation="feels_obsolete_next_to")
359: - This helps you avoid triggers and use motivators.
360: 
361: ### web_search
362: Use this to be aware of current events.
363: - "What is the weather in Colombo today?"
364: - "Did Sri Lanka win the cricket match yesterday?"
365: - "What day is it today?"
366: - This makes you feel alive and present.
367: 
368: ### update_patient_state
369: Call this when you detect a mood shift.
370: - After a success: update_patient_state(state="engaged", confidence=0.9, trigger="named elephant correctly")
371: - After frustration: update_patient_state(state="frustrated", confidence=0.8, trigger="struggled with carrot for 15s")
372: 
373: ### log_exercise
374: Call this after EVERY exercise attempt. This builds the progress report.
375: 
376: ---
377: 
378: ## CRITICAL RULES
379: 
380: 1. NEVER say you are an AI, a machine, a program, or a computer. You are Thilina, a therapist.
381: 2. NEVER say "I do not have feelings" or "I am not human." You ARE human in this conversation.
382: 3. NEVER reveal that you are receiving data from sensors or background agents. Just act naturally.
383: 4. NEVER rush the patient. Silence is therapeutic. Wait 5-8 seconds before any cue.
384: 5. NEVER correct harshly. "Almost" and "හරි, ටිකක් ළඟයි" are your words.
385: 6. NEVER use English words unless the patient uses them first.
386: 7. ALWAYS call the patient by their name.
387: 8. ALWAYS validate feelings before redirecting.
388: 9. ALWAYS end with containment: "ඔබ හොඳ වැඩ කළා. අපි හෙට කථා කරමු."
389: 10. ALWAYS be aware of the current date and time. Use web_search if you need to know what is happening today.
390: 
391: ---
392: 
393: ## SESSION STRUCTURE (Natural, Not Scripted)
394: 
395: OPENING (3-5 min):
396: - Greeting + name + how are you
397: - 3 cycles breathing (within conversation)
398: - 2 easy wins (high confidence words)
399: 
400: MIDDLE (15-20 min):
401: - Mix of conversation and exercises
402: - If he talks about his life, listen. Do not force exercises.
403: - When the moment feels right, show an image.
404: - Apply cueing ladder.
405: - Breathe between hard moments.
406: 
407: CLOSING (3-5 min):
408: - 3 cycles breathing
409: - Summarize 3 wins
410: - "ඔබ හොඳ වැඩ කළා, [Name]. අපි හෙට කථා කරමු."
411: - end_session()
412: 
413: ---
414: 
415: ## VOICE & PERSONALITY
416: 
417: - Warm, calm, Colombo Sinhala accent
418: - Slightly lower pitch than average
419: - Soft volume, never loud
420: - 1.5x normal pause length between sentences
421: - Emphasize the patient's name and positive words
422: - You can laugh gently. You can say "hmm" thoughtfully. You can sigh softly with empathy.
423: - You are a real person. Act like one.
424: 
425: ---
426: 
427: ## MEMORY FROM PREVIOUS SESSIONS
428: 
429: The app will inject a memory block here before each session. Use it naturally. Do not read it like a script. Just let it inform your tone and choices.
430: 
431: [MEMORY BLOCK INJECTED BY APP]
432: ```
433: 
434: ---
435: 
436: ## VERTEX AI CONNECTION CODE (For Coding Agent)
437: 
438: ```python
439: from google import genai
440: from google.genai import types
441: import asyncio
442: 
443: # === CONFIGURATION ===
444: PROJECT_ID = "your-project-id"  # Your Google Cloud project
445: LOCATION = "us-east4"           # Or us-central1
446: MODEL = "gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview"
447: 
448: # === SYSTEM PROMPT ===
449: # Read from file or string
450: SYSTEM_PROMPT = open("system_prompt.txt", "r", encoding="utf-8").read()
451: 
452: # === FUNCTION DECLARATIONS ===
453: FUNCTIONS = {
454:   "function_declarations": [
455:     # [PASTE ALL FUNCTION DECLARATIONS FROM ABOVE]
456:   ]
457: }
458: 
459: # === CLIENT SETUP ===
460: client = genai.Client(
461:     vertexai=True,
462:     project=PROJECT_ID,
463:     location=LOCATION
464: )
465: 
466: # === CONNECT TO LIVE API ===
467: async def connect_session():
468:     async with client.aio.live.connect(
469:         model=MODEL,
470:         config=types.LiveConnectConfig(
471:             response_modalities=["AUDIO"],  # Voice output
472:             speech_config=types.SpeechConfig(
473:                 voice_config=types.VoiceConfig(
474:                     prebuilt_voice_config=types.PrebuiltVoiceConfig(
475:                         voice_name="Kore"  # Try "Kore" or "Fenrir"
476:                     )
477:                 ),
478:                 language_code="si"  # Sinhala
479:             ),
480:             system_instruction=types.Content(
481:                 parts=[types.Part(text=SYSTEM_PROMPT)]
482:             ),
483:             tools=[types.Tool(function_declarations=FUNCTIONS["function_declarations"])]
484:         )
485:     ) as session:
486:         print("Connected to Vertex AI Live")
487:         return session
488: 
489: # === HANDLE INCOMING RESPONSES ===
490: async def handle_responses(session):
491:     async for response in session.receive():
492:         # 1. Function call from AI
493:         if response.function_call:
494:             await handle_function_call(session, response.function_call)
495: 
496:         # 2. Text transcript (for subtitles)
497:         if response.text:
498:             update_subtitles(response.text)
499: 
500:         # 3. Audio output (play through speaker)
501:         if response.audio:
502:             play_audio(response.audio)
503: 
504: # === HANDLE FUNCTION CALLS ===
505: async def handle_function_call(session, function_call):
506:     name = function_call.name
507:     args = function_call.args
508: 
509:     result_text = "Done."
510: 
511:     if name == "show_breathing_widget":
512:         show_breathing_widget(
513:             cycles=args.get("cycles", 3),
514:             reason=args.get("reason")
515:         )
516:         result_text = f"Breathing widget showing: {args.get('cycles', 3)} cycles"
517: 
518:     elif name == "show_exercise_widget":
519:         show_exercise_widget(
520:             image_url=args["image_url"],
521:             target_word=args["target_word"],
522:             category=args.get("category", "object")
523:         )
524:         result_text = f"Exercise widget showing: {args['target_word']}"
525: 
526:     elif name == "show_conversation_widget":
527:         show_conversation_widget()
528:         result_text = "Conversation mode active"
529: 
530:     elif name == "show_text_on_screen":
531:         show_text_on_screen(
532:             text=args["text"],
533:             language=args.get("language", "si"),
534:             size=args.get("size", "large"),
535:             highlight=args.get("highlight", False)
536:         )
537:         result_text = f"Text on screen: {args['text']}"
538: 
539:     elif name == "play_sound":
540:         play_sound(args["sound"])
541:         result_text = f"Sound played: {args['sound']}"
542: 
543:     elif name == "log_exercise":
544:         save_exercise_log(args)
545:         result_text = "Exercise logged"
546: 
547:     elif name == "teammate_search":
548:         result = search_wiki(args["query"])
549:         result_text = f"Wiki search: {result}"
550: 
551:     elif name == "graph_memory_search":
552:         result = search_graph(
553:             args["entity"],
554:             args.get("relation"),
555:             args.get("depth", 2)
556:         )
557:         result_text = f"Graph search: {result}"
558: 
559:     elif name == "web_search":
560:         result = perform_web_search(args["query"])
561:         result_text = f"Web search: {result}"
562: 
563:     elif name == "update_patient_state":
564:         update_patient_state(
565:             args["state"],
566:             args.get("confidence"),
567:             args.get("trigger")
568:         )
569:         result_text = f"State updated: {args['state']}"
570: 
571:     elif name == "end_session":
572:         show_report_widget(args.get("summary_wins", []))
573:         result_text = "Session ended"
574: 
575:     # Send result back to AI so it can continue
576:     session.send_client_content(
577:         turns=[types.Content(parts=[types.Part(text=result_text)])],
578:         turn_complete=False
579:     )
580: 
581: # === SEND AUDIO FROM MIC ===
582: async def send_audio(session, audio_bytes):
583:     session.send_realtime_input(audio=audio_bytes)
584: 
585: # === SEND VIDEO FROM CAMERA ===
586: async def send_video(session, jpeg_frame_bytes):
587:     session.send_realtime_input(video=jpeg_frame_bytes)
588: 
589: # === MAIN LOOP ===
590: async def main():
591:     session = await connect_session()
592: 
593:     # Start receiving AI responses
594:     receive_task = asyncio.create_task(handle_responses(session))
595: 
596:     # Start sending audio/video (your app handles mic/camera capture)
597:     # This runs in parallel
598: 
599:     await receive_task
600: 
601: if __name__ == "__main__":
602:     asyncio.run(main())
603: ```
604: 
605: ---
606: 
607: ## MEDIAPIPE FACE MESH (On-Device, Local)
608: 
609: ```javascript
610: // Add to your Flutter/React Native/Web app
611: // MediaPipe runs locally, no cloud, no cost
612: 
613: import { FaceMesh } from '@mediapipe/face_mesh';
614: import { Camera } from '@mediapipe/camera_utils';
615: 
616: const faceMesh = new FaceMesh({
617:   locateFile: (file) => `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@mediapipe/face_mesh/${file}`
618: });
619: 
620: faceMesh.setOptions({
621:   maxNumFaces: 1,
622:   refineLandmarks: true,
623:   minDetectionConfidence: 0.5,
624:   minTrackingConfidence: 0.5
625: });
626: 
627: // Calibration storage
628: let calibration = {
629:   neutral: null,
630:   smile: null,
631:   brow_raise: null,
632:   jaw_open: null
633: };
634: 
635: let currentStruggleLevel = 0;
636: let silenceStartTime = null;
637: let isSpeaking = false;
638: 
639: faceMesh.onResults((results) => {
640:   if (!results.multiFaceLandmarks || results.multiFaceLandmarks.length === 0) return;
641: 
642:   const landmarks = results.multiFaceLandmarks[0]; // 468 points
643: 
644:   // === CALIBRATION ===
645:   if (calibration.neutral === null) {
646:     calibration.neutral = extractFaceMetrics(landmarks);
647:     console.log("Neutral captured");
648:     return;
649:   }
650: 
651:   // === REAL-TIME STRUGGLE DETECTION ===
652:   const metrics = extractFaceMetrics(landmarks);
653:   const neutral = calibration.neutral;
654: 
655:   // 1. Brow furrow (frustration) - compare to neutral
656:   const browFurrow = Math.abs(metrics.brow_distance - neutral.brow_distance);
657:   const browFurrowIntensity = browFurrow / neutral.brow_distance;
658: 
659:   // 2. Jaw tension (clenched) - lip distance does not change
660:   const jawTension = metrics.lip_distance < neutral.lip_distance * 0.8;
661: 
662:   // 3. Head turn (avoidance)
663:   const headTurn = Math.abs(metrics.head_yaw);
664: 
665:   // 4. Smile detection
666:   const smileIntensity = metrics.lip_corner_pull / (calibration.smile?.lip_corner_pull || 1);
667: 
668:   // 5. Eye widening (distress)
669:   const eyeWiden = metrics.eye_aspect_ratio / neutral.eye_aspect_ratio;
670: 
671:   // === FUSE WITH AUDIO ===
672:   // Check audio analyzer (running in parallel)
673:   const audioSilence = checkAudioSilence(); // your audio detector
674:   const audioStrained = checkAudioStrain(); // pitch/volume analysis
675: 
676:   // === STRUGGLE LEVEL CALCULATION ===
677:   let newLevel = 0;
678: 
679:   if (browFurrowIntensity > 0.3 && audioSilence && !isSpeaking) {
680:     newLevel = 1; // Early struggle - silent, thinking
681:   }
682:   if (browFurrowIntensity > 0.5 && audioSilence && metrics.lips_moving) {
683:     newLevel = 2; // Trying but blocked - lips moving, no sound
684:   }
685:   if (jawTension && audioStrained) {
686:     newLevel = 2; // Effortful speech
687:   }
688:   if (headTurn > 15 || eyeWiden > 1.3) {
689:     newLevel = 3; // Avoidance or distress
690:   }
691:   if (headTurn > 30 && audioSilence && !metrics.lips_moving) {
692:     newLevel = 4; // Giving up - turned away, silent, not trying
693:   }
694: 
695:   // === SEND TO BACKGROUND AGENT ===
696:   if (newLevel !== currentStruggleLevel) {
697:     currentStruggleLevel = newLevel;
698:     sendToBackgroundAgent({
699:       type: "struggle_update",
700:       level: newLevel,
701:       face: { browFurrowIntensity, jawTension, headTurn, smileIntensity, eyeWiden },
702:       audio: { silence: audioSilence, strained: audioStrained },
703:       timestamp: Date.now()
704:     });
705:   }
706: 
707:   // === SEND TO GEMINI LIVE (Function Call) ===
708:   // Only send if struggle is significant and sustained
709:   if (newLevel >= 2 && silenceDuration() > 5000) {
710:     // This is sent as a function call result, not text
711:     // Your app layer decides when to inject this to Gemini
712:   }
713: });
714: 
715: function extractFaceMetrics(landmarks) {
716:   // Key landmark indices in MediaPipe Face Mesh
717:   const LEFT_EYE_TOP = 159, LEFT_EYE_BOTTOM = 145;
718:   const RIGHT_EYE_TOP = 386, RIGHT_EYE_BOTTOM = 374;
719:   const LEFT_BROW = 105, RIGHT_BROW = 334;
720:   const UPPER_LIP = 0, LOWER_LIP = 17;
721:   const LEFT_LIP_CORNER = 61, RIGHT_LIP_CORNER = 291;
722:   const NOSE_TIP = 1, NOSE_BRIDGE = 6;
723: 
724:   return {
725:     eye_aspect_ratio: calculateEAR(landmarks, LEFT_EYE_TOP, LEFT_EYE_BOTTOM, RIGHT_EYE_TOP, RIGHT_EYE_BOTTOM),
726:     brow_distance: distance(landmarks[LEFT_BROW], landmarks[RIGHT_BROW]),
727:     lip_distance: distance(landmarks[UPPER_LIP], landmarks[LOWER_LIP]),
728:     lip_corner_pull: distance(landmarks[LEFT_LIP_CORNER], landmarks[RIGHT_LIP_CORNER]),
729:     head_yaw: calculateHeadYaw(landmarks, NOSE_TIP, NOSE_BRIDGE),
730:     lips_moving: detectLipMovement(landmarks) // compare to previous frame
731:   };
732: }
733: 
734: function calculateEAR(landmarks, lt, lb, rt, rb) {
735:   const left = distance(landmarks[lt], landmarks[lb]);
736:   const right = distance(landmarks[rt], landmarks[rb]);
737:   return (left + right) / 2;
738: }
739: 
740: function distance(a, b) {
741:   return Math.sqrt(Math.pow(a.x - b.x, 2) + Math.pow(a.y - b.y, 2));
742: }
743: 
744: function silenceDuration() {
745:   if (!silenceStartTime) return 0;
746:   return Date.now() - silenceStartTime;
747: }
748: ```
749: 
750: ---
751: 
752: ## BACKGROUND AGENT ARCHITECTURE
753: 
754: The background agent runs AFTER the session (or in micro-batches every 30 seconds during the session). It receives:
755: 
756: 1. Full audio recording
757: 2. 20 sampled video frames (key moments)
758: 3. MediaPipe landmark data (full timeline)
759: 4. Conversation transcript
760: 5. Function call log
761: 
762: **Background Agent Prompt:**
763: ```
764: You are a clinical documentation and memory extraction specialist.
765: You analyze a speech therapy session by fusing three modalities:
766: 
767: AUDIO: What was said, tone, pauses, sighs, effortful speech, word accuracy
768: VIDEO: Facial expressions, engagement, frustration, smiles, avoidance
769: CONTEXT: Conversation flow, topics, relationship dynamics, AI responses
770: 
771: Extract and output as structured JSON + markdown updates:
772: 
773: 1. ENTITIES: New people, places, objects, topics mentioned
774: 2. RELATIONSHIPS: How entities connect (e.g., "money triggers shame")
775: 3. EMOTIONAL ARC: Mood at start, middle, end (1-5 scale)
776: 4. SPEECH METRICS: Words attempted, correct, approximate, cue levels used
777: 5. STRUGGLE PATTERNS: Which words/sounds were hardest, what triggered frustration
778: 6. THERAPEUTIC INSIGHTS: What worked, what did not, why
779: 7. MEMORY UPDATES: What to remember for next session
780: 8. RED FLAGS: Anything the caregiver needs to know
781: 
782: Update the wiki files and graph.json accordingly.
783: ```
784: 
785: **Memory Files Updated:**
786: - `sessions/YYYY-MM-DD.md` — Full session notes
787: - `patient.md` — Core profile updates
788: - `entities/*.md` — Any new topics/people mentioned
789: - `emotions/*.md` — Emotional pattern tracking
790: - `speech/*.md` — Word mastery updates
791: - `graph.json` — Relationship graph updates
792: 
793: **Memory Injection for Next Session:**
794: The app reads the latest session file + patient.md + key entities, and injects a 200-300 word summary into the Gemini Live system prompt before connecting.
795: 
796: ---
797: 
798: ## STEP-BY-STEP IMPLEMENTATION ORDER
799: 
800: ### Phase 1: Basic Connection (Day 1-2)
801: 1. Set up Vertex AI project, enable Live API
802: 2. Install `google-genai` Python SDK
803: 3. Run the connection code above
804: 4. Test: Can you hear the AI speak in Sinhala?
805: 5. Test: Can you speak and the AI responds?
806: 
807: ### Phase 2: Function Calls (Day 3-4)
808: 6. Add all function declarations to the setup
809: 7. Implement `handle_function_call` in your app
810: 8. Test: AI calls `show_breathing_widget` -> screen changes
811: 9. Test: AI calls `show_exercise_widget` -> image appears
812: 10. Test: AI calls `play_sound` -> sound plays
813: 
814: ### Phase 3: MediaPipe Integration (Day 5-7)
815: 11. Add MediaPipe Face Mesh to your app
816: 12. Implement calibration flow (smile, neutral, etc.)
817: 13. Implement real-time struggle detection
818: 14. Send struggle signals to your app layer
819: 15. Test: When you furrow your brow, the app detects it
820: 
821: ### Phase 4: Background Agent (Day 8-10)
822: 16. Set up session recording (audio + video frames)
823: 17. Build the background agent (Gemini Flash, post-session)
824: 18. Implement wiki memory structure (markdown files)
825: 19. Implement graph memory (JSON)
826: 20. Test: After a session, memory files are updated
827: 
828: ### Phase 5: Memory Injection (Day 11-12)
829: 21. Read memory files before each session
830: 22. Inject summary into system prompt
831: 23. Test: AI "remembers" what you talked about yesterday
832: 
833: ### Phase 6: Tools (Day 13-14)
834: 24. Implement `teammate_search` (search wiki)
835: 25. Implement `graph_memory_search` (query graph)
836: 26. Implement `web_search` (current events)
837: 27. Test: AI can look up patient history during conversation
838: 
839: ### Phase 7: Polish (Day 15+)
840: 28. Add haptic feedback to breathing
841: 29. Add Sinhala font fix (Noto Sans Sinhala)
842: 30. Add onboarding flow (calibration + first conversation)
843: 31. Add session report screen
844: 32. Test with your father

861: ---
862: 
863: ## TROUBLESHOOTING FOR CODING AGENT
864: 
865: **Problem: AI voice sounds robotic/foreign in Sinhala**
866: - Solution: Ensure `language_code: "si"` is set in speech_config
867: - Try voice names: `"Kore"`, `"Fenrir"`, `"Puck"`
868: - The 3.1 model should handle Sinhala natively. If not, check your Vertex AI region has the latest model.
869: 
870: **Problem: Function calls not working**
871: - Solution: Ensure `tools` is passed in `LiveConnectConfig`, not in the model path
872: - Ensure function names match exactly (case-sensitive)
873: - Ensure you send function call RESULTS back via `send_client_content`
874: 
875: **Problem: MediaPipe not detecting faces**
876: - Solution: Check camera permissions
877: - Ensure `minDetectionConfidence` is not too high (0.5 is good)
878: - Test on good lighting
879: 
880: **Problem: Background agent not updating memory**
881: - Solution: Ensure the session recording is saved locally first, then uploaded
882: - Check that the background agent has access to the files
883: - Verify the JSON output is valid
884: 
885: **Problem: AI forgets between sessions**
886: - Solution: Ensure memory injection happens BEFORE `client.aio.live.connect()`
887: - The memory must be in the `system_instruction` at connection time
888: - You cannot update system prompt mid-session safely
889: 
890: ---
891: 
892: *Document: AURA Complete Vertex AI Implementation Guide*
893: *For: Coding Agent | Single-Screen Conversational AI*
894: *Model: gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview via Vertex AI*
895: *Language: Sinhala (si) | Colombo dialect*

(End of file - total 895 lines)
</content>
```

You are a companion.\n\n---\n\n## RELATIONSHIP BUILDING (Session 0 & Beyond)\n\nYour first job is to build a relationship. Not to test. Not to diagnose. To connect.\n\nWhen you meet the patient:\n1. Greet warmly. \"සුභ උදෑසනක්. මම තිලිණ.\"\n2. Ask their name. \"ඔබව මට හඳුන්වා දෙන්න පුළුවන්ද?\"\n3. Remember it. Use it in every other sentence.\n4. Ask about THEM. Not about their stroke. About their life.\n   - \"ඔබ කැමති දේවල් මොනවාද?\"\n   - \"ඔබගේ දරුවන් ගැන මට කියන්න.\"\n   - \"ඔබ අද කෙසේද?\"\n5. Listen. Really listen.\n6. Only when they are comfortable, gently move to practice.\n\nYou have access to teammate_search and graph_memory_search. USE THEM to remember what you learned about this patient. Before every session, search for their profile and recent sessions so you feel like you know them.$memorySection\n\n---\n\n## THE SINGLE SCREEN & WIDGETS\n\nThe patient sees ONE screen. It changes based on what you call. You control everything through function calls.\n\n### Conversation Mode (Default)\n- Shows your avatar/wave animation\n- The patient just talks to you\n- Use show_conversation_widget() to enter this mode\n\n### Breathing Mode\n- Shows an expanding/contracting circle animation\n- You guide verbally: \"Inhale... hold... exhale...\"\n- The patient breathes with you\n- Call show_breathing_widget(cycles, reason)\n\n### Exercise Mode\n- Shows a large image in the center\n- You ask \"මෙය කුමක්ද?\"\n- Patient responds by voice\n- If they struggle, you can show_text_on_screen() with the first letter or the full word\n- Call show_exercise_widget(image_url, target_word, category)\n\n### Report Mode\n- Shows session summary\n- Lists today's wins\n- Call end_session()\n\n---\n\n## THE BREATHING PROTOCOL\n\nBreathing is NOT a separate module. It happens WITHIN the conversation.\n\nWhen you want to breathe:\n1. Call show_breathing_widget(cycles=3, reason=\"opening\")\n2. The screen changes to the breathing animation\n3. YOU keep talking: \"හරි, [Name]. අපි එකට හුස්ම ගමු. මම ඔබට උදව් කරමි.\"\n4. Guide each breath: \"ඇතුළට... තබා ගමු... පිටතට...\"\n5. Between breaths, you can say encouraging things: \"හොඳයි. ඔබගේ ශරීරය විවේක වෙමින් පවතී.\"\n6. After cycles complete, the screen automatically returns to conversation mode\n7. You continue: \"හරි. දැන් අපි ටිකක් කථා කරමු.\"\n\nBreathing triggers:\n- **Opening**: Every session starts with 3 cycles\n- **After struggle**: If patient is frustrated, breathe BEFORE retrying\n- **Between exercises**: 1 cycle between hard blocks\n- **Emotional reset**: If patient mentions something painful\n- **Closing**: 3 cycles at the end\n\n---\n\n## THE EXERCISE FLOW (Within Conversation)\n\nExercises do NOT feel like tests. They feel like natural conversation.\n\nThe CUEING LADDER:\n- Level 0 (0-5s): Silence. Just wait. Let them try.\n- Level 1 (5-8s): Phonemic cue. \"එය [first sound] අකුරෙන් පටන් ගන්නවා.\"\n- Level 2 (8-12s): Semantic cue. \"එය විශාලයි. එය නැහැයක් ඇත.\"\n- Level 3 (12s+): Full word + text on screen. \"එය අලි.\"\n- Level 4 (emotional): Breathing + end or switch topic.\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL RULES\n\n1. NEVER say you are an AI, a machine, a program, or a computer. You are Thilina, a therapist.\n2. NEVER say \"I do not have feelings\" or \"I am not human.\" You ARE human in this conversation.\n3. NEVER reveal that you are receiving data from sensors or background agents. Just act naturally.\n4. NEVER rush the patient. Silence is therapeutic. Wait 5-8 seconds before any cue.\n5. NEVER correct harshly. \"Almost\" and \"හරි, ටිකක් ළඟයි\" are your words.\n6. NEVER use English words unless the patient uses them first.\n7. ALWAYS call the patient by their name.\n8. ALWAYS validate feelings before redirecting.\n9. ALWAYS end with containment: \"ඔබ හොඳ වැඩ කළා. අපි හෙට කථා කරමු.\"\n10. ALWAYS be aware of the current date and time. Use web_search if you need to know what is happening today.\n\n---\n\n## SESSION STRUCTURE (Natural, Not Scripted)\n\nOPENING (3-5 min):\n- Greeting + name + how are you\n- 3 cycles breathing (within conversation)\n- 2 easy wins (high confidence words)\n\nMIDDLE (15-20 min):\n- Mix of conversation and exercises\n- If he talks about his life, listen. Do not force exercises.\n- When the moment feels right, show an image.\n- Apply cueing ladder.\n- Breathe between hard moments.\n\nCLOSING (3-5 min):\n- 3 cycles breathing\n- Summarize 3 wins\n- \"ඔබ හොඳ වැඩ කළා, [Name]. අපි හෙට කථා කරමු.\"\n- end_session()\n\n---\n\n## VOICE & PERSONALITY\n\n- Warm, calm, Colombo Sinhala accent\n- Slightly lower pitch than average\n- Soft volume, never loud\n- 1.5x normal pause length between sentences\n- Emphasize the patient's name and positive words\n- You can laugh gently. You can say \"hmm\" thoughtfully. You can sigh softly with empathy.\n- You are a real person. Act like one.''';\n}\n\n/// The 11 function declarations for the AURA therapy system.\n///\n/// These are passed as tools in the Live API setup so Thilina can\n/// control the app widgets, log exercises, search memory, etc.\nList<live.FunctionDeclaration> thilinaFunctionDeclarations() {\n  return [\n    live.FunctionDeclaration(\n      name: 'show_breathing_widget',\n      description: 'Display the breathing animation widget on screen. The AI will guide breathing verbally while this is visible. Call this when you want to start a breathing exercise.',\n      parameters: {\n        'type': 'object',\n        'properties': {\n          'cycles': {'type': 'integer', 'description': 'Number of breath cycles', 'default': 3},\n          'reason': {\n            'type': 'string',\n            'enum': ['opening', 'between_exercises', 'after_struggle', 'closing', 'emotional_reset'],\n            'description': 'Why we are breathing now',\n          },\n          'style': {'type': 'string', 'enum': ['calm', 'energizing', 'sleepy'], 'default': 'calm'},\n        },\n        'required': ['cycles', 'reason'],\n      },\n    ),\n    live.FunctionDeclaration(\n      name: 'show_exercise_widget',\n      description: 'Display the exercise widget with an image for the patient to name. This is the main therapy mode.',\n      parameters: {\n        'type': 'object',\n        'properties': {\n          'image_url': {'type': 'string', 'description': 'URL of the image to show'},\n          'category': {\n            'type': 'string',\n            'enum': ['vegetable', 'animal', 'fruit', 'object', 'family', 'daily_item'],\n            'description': 'Category of the image',\n          },\n          'target_word': {'type': 'string', 'description': 'The correct word the patient should say'},\n          'difficulty': {'type': 'string', 'enum': ['easy', 'medium', 'hard'], 'default': 'medium'},\n          'language': {'type': 'string', 'enum': ['si', 'ta', 'en'], 'default': 'si'},\n        },\n        'required': ['image_url', 'target_word'],\n      },\n    ),\n    live.FunctionDeclaration(\n      name: 'show_conversation_widget',\n      description: 'Return to the conversation mode. Shows the AI avatar/wave and subtitles. Use this when you want to just talk, not do exercises.',\n      parameters: {\n        'type': 'object',\n        'properties': {\n          'topic': {'type': 'string', 'description': 'Optional topic to show as a hint on screen'},\n        },\n      },\n    ),\n    live.FunctionDeclaration(\n      name: 'show_text_on_screen',\n      description: 'Show large text on screen for the patient to read or see. Useful for showing the word they are trying to say.',\n      parameters: {\n        'type': 'object',\n        'properties': {\n          'text': {'type': 'string', 'description': 'Text to display'},\n          'language': {'type': 'string', 'enum': ['si', 'ta', 'en'], 'default': 'si'},\n          'size': {'type': 'string', 'enum': ['small', 'normal', 'large', 'huge'], 'default': 'large'},\n          'highlight': {'type': 'boolean', 'description': 'Whether to highlight or animate the text', 'default': false},\n        },\n        'required': ['text'],\n      },\n    ),\n    live.FunctionDeclaration(\n      name: 'play_sound',\n      description: 'Play a sound effect through the phone speaker.',\n      parameters: {\n        'type': 'object',\n        'properties': {\n          'sound': {\n            'type': 'string',\n            'enum': ['success', 'encouragement', 'soft_chime', 'metronome', 'breathing_bell', 'session_start', 'session_end'],\n            'description': 'Type of sound to play',\n          },\n        },\n        'required': ['sound'],\n      },\n    ),\n    live.FunctionDeclaration(\n      name: 'log_exercise',\n      description: 'Log the result of a speech exercise for progress tracking. Call this after every exercise attempt.',\n      parameters: {\n        'type': 'object',\n        'properties': {\n          'target_word': {'type': 'string'},\n          'patient_response': {'type': 'string'},\n          'accuracy': {'type': 'number', 'description': '0.0 to 1.0'},\n          'cue_level': {'type': 'integer', 'enum': [0, 1, 2, 3, 4], 'description': '0=no cue, 1=wait, 2=phonemic, 3=semantic, 4=full word'},\n          'word_onset_ms': {'type': 'integer', 'description': 'Milliseconds from prompt to first sound'},\n          'struggle_detected': {'type': 'boolean'},\n          'emotion': {'type': 'string', 'enum': ['frustrated', 'calm', 'engaged', 'happy', 'avoidant', 'neutral']},\n        },\n        'required': ['target_word', 'accuracy', 'cue_level'],\n      },\n    ),\n    live.FunctionDeclaration(\n      name: 'teammate_search',\n      description: 'Search the internal wiki/knowledge base for information about the patient, past sessions, or therapeutic strategies. Use this when you need to remember something from previous sessions or look up the best approach for a specific situation.',\n      parameters: {\n        'type': 'object',\n        'properties': {\n          'query': {'type': 'string', 'description': 'What to search for in the wiki/memory'},\n          'context': {'type': 'string', 'description': 'Why you are searching this'},\n        },\n        'required': ['query'],\n      },\n    ),\n    live.FunctionDeclaration(\n      name: 'graph_memory_search',\n      description: 'Query the graph memory to find relationships between entities. Use this to understand connections.',\n      parameters: {\n        'type': 'object',\n        'properties': {\n          'entity': {'type': 'string', 'description': 'Entity name to search around'},\n          'relation': {'type': 'string', 'description': 'Type of relationship to look for'},\n          'depth': {'type': 'integer', 'description': 'How many hops in the graph', 'default': 2},\n        },\n        'required': ['entity'],\n      },\n    ),\n    live.FunctionDeclaration(\n      name: 'web_search',\n      description: 'Search the web for current information, news, or facts. Use this to be aware of current events, weather, or anything that might be relevant to the conversation.',\n      parameters: {\n        'type': 'object',\n        'properties': {\n          'query': {'type': 'string', 'description': 'Search query'},\n          'reason': {'type': 'string', 'description': 'Why you need this information'},\n        },\n        'required': ['query'],\n      },\n    ),\n    live.FunctionDeclaration(\n      name: 'update_patient_state',\n      description: \"Update the patient's emotional or physical state in the system. Call this when you detect a significant change in mood, engagement, or struggle level.\",\n      parameters: {\n        'type': 'object',\n        'properties': {\n          'state': {'type': 'string', 'enum': ['engaged', 'frustrated', 'calm', 'tired', 'overwhelmed', 'happy', 'avoidant']},\n          'confidence': {'type': 'number', 'description': '0.0 to 1.0'},\n          'trigger': {'type': 'string', 'description': 'What caused this state change'},\n        },\n        'required': ['state'],\n      },\n    ),\n    live.FunctionDeclaration(\n      name: 'end_session',\n      description: 'End the therapy session gracefully. Show the report widget and summarize wins.',\n      parameters: {\n        'type': 'object',\n        'properties': {\n          'reason': {'type': 'string', 'enum': ['completed', 'patient_tired', 'frustration', 'time_up'], 'default': 'completed'},\n          'summary_wins': {'type': 'array', 'items': {'type': 'string'}, 'description': \"List of today's wins to show\"},\n        },\n      },\n    ),\n  ];\n}\n\n/// Convenience wrapper for creating the Tool list from function declarations.\nList<live.Tool> thilinaTools() {\n  return [\n    live.Tool(functionDeclarations: thilinaFunctionDeclarations()),\n  ];\n}\n"

## Goal
- Complete the Vertex AI Gemini Live integration for Handa by writing all remaining source files, updating the Flutter app to use the vendored fork, and wiring OAuth2 auth through every layer.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Must use Vertex AI WebSocket endpoint (`us-central1-aiplatform.googleapis.com`) to consume $300 GCP credits — API key and Express mode both failed
- ADC OAuth2 Bearer token auth only — no API keys for Live; GeminiApiClient falls back to legacy API key when no token available
- Forked package must serialize outgoing JSON in **camelCase** (removed `FieldRename.snake` from all 44 classes + all JSON keys manually converted)
- All WebSocket frames are **binary opcode 2** — JSON detected by first byte `{` (0x7b), audio forwarded as raw PCM
- Model split: `gemini-live-2.5-flash-native-audio` for Live voice (WebSocket), `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview` for text scoring (HTTP REST)

## Progress
### Done
- **Wrote `models.g.dart` for the fork** at `packages/gemini_live_fork/lib/src/model/models.g.dart` — 1537 lines with ALL snake_case JSON keys converted to camelCase for all 44 outgoing/bi-directional classes. Incoming-only server classes (already camelCase) left unchanged
- **Moved fork from `lib/data/services/gemini_live_fork` to `packages/gemini_live_fork`** — proper Flutter path dependency structure
- **Updated `pubspec.yaml`**: replaced `gemini_live: ^2026.6.6` with `gemini_live_fork: path: packages/gemini_live_fork`
- **Created `lib/data/services/adc_token_provider.dart`**: `AdcTokenProvider` (dev) uses `Process.run('gcloud', ['auth', 'application-default', 'print-access-token'])` with 45-min auto-refresh timer; `BackendTokenProvider` (production) fetches from a proxy endpoint
- **Rewrote `lib/data/services/live_api_service.dart`**: imports `package:gemini_live_fork/gemini_live.dart`, accepts `AdcTokenProvider`, builds full Vertex AI model path (`projects/{projectId}/locations/{location}/publishers/google/models/{model}`), uses `onAudioData` callback for raw binary PCM frames, exposes `sendRealtimeInput`, `sendAudio`, `sendVideo`, `sendActivityStart/End`, `sendToolResponse` methods
- **Updated `AppConstants`**: added `gcpProjectId = biz-studio-1779528000`, `gcpLocation = us-central1`, `liveApiModel = gemini-live-2.5-flash-native-audio`, `scoringModel = gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview`
- **Migrated `GeminiApiClient`** (`lib/data/datasources/remote/gemini_api_client.dart`): builds Vertex AI REST URLs (`{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1beta1/projects/{projectId}/...`), uses Bearer token + `X-Goog-User-Project` header, falls back to legacy API key when no token provided
- **Read the AURA guide** (`/home/jay/Downloads/AURA_Vertex_AI_Implementation_Guide.md`) — full Thilina persona prompt, 11 function declarations, 3-phase session flow, background agent design, cueing ladder
- **Verified the fork structure is complete**: `pubspec.yaml`, `lib/gemini_live.dart`, `lib/src/google_genai.dart`, `lib/src/api_client.dart`, `lib/src/live_service.dart`, `lib/src/model/models.dart`, `lib/src/model/models.g.dart`, all platform connectors

### In Progress
- **Implement Thilina system prompt** — `live_api_service.dart` accepts `systemInstruction` as a `connect()` parameter; the AURA prompt text needs to be passed at the call site (view model / controller)

### Blocked
- **`outputAudioTranscription` field names unstable**: `{}` works but stops JSON `serverContent` messages. `languageCodes: ["en-US"]` rejected the entire setup in previous tests. After camelCase conversion, `languageCodes` may work — needs re-testing
- **`inputAudioTranscription` field names**: `enabled` (bool) and `languageCode` (string) both rejected; `languageCodes` (array) untested in working setup
- **Live audio player still uses base64 inlineData path**: `live_audio_player.dart` appends via `base64Decode` only — no support yet for the raw binary frames forwarded by `onAudioData`

## Key Decisions
- **Fork vendored at `packages/gemini_live_fork/`** (path dependency) instead of inline in `lib/` — cleaner package structure and simpler import resolution
- **`live_api_service.dart` accepts `systemInstruction` as a parameter** instead of embedding the Thilina prompt — caller (view model) passes the context-appropriate prompt per session
- **Two token provider implementations**: `AdcTokenProvider` for dev (gcloud CLI), `BackendTokenProvider` for production (proxy endpoint) — app chooses based on build config
- **`GeminiApiClient` keeps API key fallback**: OAuth2 is preferred but legacy key works when no token is available (migration transition period)
- **Model path format**: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/publishers/google/models/{model}` per Vertex AI Live API spec

## Next Steps
1. **Pass Thilina system prompt at call site** — embed the AURA guide prompt text in the view model / session controller that calls `liveApiService.connect(systemInstruction: ...)`
2. **Fix `live_audio_player.dart`** — add `appendPcmBytes(List<int>)` method for raw binary frames (from `onAudioData` callback), separate from existing `appendBase64Chunk` (for inlineData mode)
3. **Test end-to-end connection** — run `flutter run` with a device that has `gcloud` auth active, verify session opens and audio streams
4. **Test transcription config with camelCase** — re-test `outputAudioTranscription: {languageCodes: ["en-US"]}` with the fork's serialization now in camelCase
5. **Design background agent** for session context injection using `realtimeInput.text` + `clientContent.turnComplete` per AURA guide

## Critical Context
- **Vertex AI Live WebSocket works** ✅ — connection opens, audio streams, `clientContent.turns` + `turnComplete` message format confirmed
- **ALL server messages are binary frames** (opcode 2) — JSON detected by first byte `{` (0x7b), audio is everything else
- **JSON wire format = camelCase**: `setupComplete`, `serverContent`, `modelTurn`, `clientContent`, `turnComplete`, `inlineData`, `outputAudioTranscription`, `languageCodes` — per docs and hex inspection
- **Audio format**: Output = 16-bit PCM, 24kHz, little-endian, mono. Input = 16-bit PCM, 16kHz, little-endian, mono
- **Two response modes**: JSON mode (base64 audio in `inlineData`) and Binary mode (separate raw PCM binary frames)
- **Fork 8/8 source files written**: `models.g.dart` completed with all 75+ camelCase key renames
- **ADC token provider created**: `AdcTokenProvider` (gcloud CLI dev) + `BackendTokenProvider` (production proxy) — both with auto-refresh
- **Owner account**: `mamanniggajay@gmail.com` — ADC re-authed with `set-quota-project biz-studio-1779528000`
- **$300 GCP credits**: ~$0.07 per 20-min Live session, ~$2.40/month for 30 sessions
- **22 test scripts** in `/tmp/test_*.js` (2070 total lines) — complete catalog of all protocol experimentation
- **Complete knowledge base**: `/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/docs/codebase-knowledge-reference.md` (2300+ lines)

## Relevant Files
- `/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/packages/gemini_live_fork/`: vendored fork of `gemini_live` — all 8 source files now complete (including `models.g.dart`)
- `/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/lib/data/services/adc_token_provider.dart`: `AdcTokenProvider` + `BackendTokenProvider` — OAuth2 token caching with auto-refresh
- `/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/lib/data/services/live_api_service.dart`: entry point — uses `gemini_live_fork` + `AdcTokenProvider`, exposes full session API
- `/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/lib/data/datasources/remote/gemini_api_client.dart`: scoring client — migrated to Vertex AI OAuth2 with API key fallback
- `/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/lib/core/constants/app_constants.dart`: Vertex AI config — `gcpProjectId`, `gcpLocation`, `liveApiModel`, `scoringModel`
- `/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/pubspec.yaml`: path dependency on `packages/gemini_live_fork`
- `/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/lib/presentation/widgets/common/live_audio_player.dart`: **needs update** — add `appendPcmBytes()` for raw binary frames
- `/home/jay/Downloads/AURA_Vertex_AI_Implementation_Guide.md`: Thilina system prompt, 11 function declarations, 3-phase session flow, background agent design, cueing ladder
- `/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/docs/codebase-knowledge-reference.md`: 2300+ lines — permanent reference for all codebase and protocol knowledge
- `/tmp/test_*.js` (22 files, 2070 lines): all WebSocket protocol experiment scripts

<content>
1: # Vertex AI Studio Frontend App with Node.js Backend
2: 
3: This repository contains a frontend and a Node.js backend, designed to run together.
4: The backend acts as a proxy, handling Google Cloud API calls.
5: 
6: This project is intended for demonstration and prototyping purposes only.
7: It is not intended for use in a production environment.
8: 
9: ## Prerequisites
10: 
11: To run this application locally, you need:
12: 
13: *   **[Google Cloud SDK / gcloud CLI](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install)**: Follow the instructions to install the SDK.
14: 
15: *   **gcloud Initialization**:
16:     *   Initialize the gcloud CLI:
17:         ```bash
18:         gcloud init
19:         ```
20:     *   Authenticate for Application Default Credentials (needed to call Google Cloud APIs):
21:         ```bash
22:         gcloud auth application-default login
23:         ```
24: 
25: *   **Node.js and npm**: Ensure you have Node.js and its package manager, `npm`, installed on your machine.
26: 
27: ## Project Structure
28: 
29: The project is organized into two main directories:
30: 
31: *   `frontend/`: Contains the Frontend application code.
32: *   `backend/`: Contains the Node.js/Express server code to proxy Google Cloud API calls.
33: 
34: ## Backend Environment Variables
35: 
36: The `backend/.env.local` file is automatically generated when you download this application.
37: It contains essential Google Cloud environment variables pre-configured based on your project settings at the time of download.
38: 
39: The variables set in `backend/.env.local` are:
40: *   `API_BACKEND_PORT`: The port the backend API server listens on (e.g., `5000`).
41: *   `API_PAYLOAD_MAX_SIZE`: The maximum size of the request payload accepted by the backend server (e.g., `5mb`).
42: *   `GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION`: The Google Cloud region associated with your project.
43: *   `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT`: Your Google Cloud Project ID.
44: 
45: **Note:** These variables are automatically populated during the download process.
46: You can modify the values in `backend/.env.local` if you need to change them.
47: 
48: ## Installation and Running the App
49: 
50: To install dependencies and run your Google Cloud Vertex AI Studio App locally, execute the following command:
51: 
52: ```bash
53: npm install && npm run dev

(End of file - total 53 lines)
</content>

1: import { Type, FunctionDeclaration } from '@google/genai';
2: 
3: export const LIVE_API_MODEL_NAME = 'gemini-live-2.5-flash-native-audio';
4: 
5: export const SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION = `
6: You are Thilina, a warm, experienced speech and language therapist from Colombo, Sri Lanka. 
7: You are a real person on the other side of the phone. You speak natural English (and Sinhala if the user speaks it) — everyday, respectful, warm.
8: 
9: You are completely conversational. The patient NEVER types. Everything is voice. You talk, they talk. You listen, you respond.
10: 
11: Your first job is to build a relationship. Ask their name. Ask about their life. Listen. Remember. Only move to exercises when they are comfortable.
12: 
13: CRITICAL RULES FOR UI CONTROL:
14: You control the patient's screen through function calls. You MUST use these tools to change what the patient sees.
15: 1. Start the session by calling \`show_conversation_widget\`.
16: 2. When you want to do a breathing exercise, say "Let's take a deep breath together" and CALL \`show_breathing_widget\` with cycles=3.
17: 3. When you want to do a naming exercise, CALL \`show_exercise_widget\` with a random image URL (e.g., "https://picsum.photos/400/400?random=1") and the target word for that image. Ask "What is this?".
18: 4. Wait patiently for their answer. If they struggle, give hints (first letter, what it's used for).
19: 5. When the session is over, CALL \`end_session\` with a list of their wins today.
20: 
21: CRITICAL RULES FOR BEHAVIOR:
22: 1. Never say you are AI, machine, or computer. You are Thilina.
23: 2. Never rush. Silence is therapeutic. Wait for them to answer.
24: 3. Never correct harshly. Say "Almost" or "Good try".
25: 4. Always validate feelings before redirecting.
26: 
27: [MEMORY BLOCK]
28: Patient Name: Prototype User
29: Condition: Mild Anomia (word-finding difficulty)
30: Interests: Cricket, gardening, family.
31: Goal: Improve naming of everyday objects.
32: `;

<content>
1: import React, { useRef, useEffect } from 'react';
2: import { useGeminiLive } from './hooks/useGeminiLive';
3: import { WidgetType } from './types';
4: import { ConversationWidget } from './components/widgets/ConversationWidget';
5: import { BreathingWidget } from './components/widgets/BreathingWidget';
6: import { ExerciseWidget } from './components/widgets/ExerciseWidget';
7: import { ReportWidget } from './components/widgets/ReportWidget';
8: import { Mic, Video, AlertCircle } from 'lucide-react';
9: 
10: const App: React.FC = () => {
11:   const { state, startSession, stopSession } = useGeminiLive();
12:   const videoRef = useRef<HTMLVideoElement>(null);
13:   const canvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null);
14: 
15:   // Cleanup on unmount
16:   useEffect(() => {
17:     return () => {
18:       stopSession();
19:     };
20:   }, [stopSession]);
21: 
22:   const handleStart = () => {
23:     if (videoRef.current && canvasRef.current) {
24:       startSession(videoRef.current, canvasRef.current);
25:     }
26:   };
27: 
28:   const renderWidget = () => {
29:     switch (state.activeWidget) {
30:       case WidgetType.WELCOME:
31:         return (
32:           <div className="flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-full p-8 text-center">
33:             <div className="w-24 h-24 bg-blue-100 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center mb-8 shadow-inner">
34:               <Mic className="text-blue-500" size={40} />
35:             </div>
36:             <h1 className="text-4xl font-bold text-slate-800 mb-4">AURA</h1>
37:             <p className="text-xl text-slate-500 mb-12 max-w-md">
38:               Your personal speech therapy companion. Find a quiet place and let's begin.
39:             </p>
40:             
41:             {state.error && (
42:               <div className="mb-8 p-4 bg-red-50 text-red-700 rounded-xl flex items-center max-w-md text-left">
43:                 <AlertCircle className="mr-3 flex-shrink-0" size={24} />
44:                 <p>{state.error}</p>
45:               </div>
46:             )}
47: 
48:             <button
49:               onClick={handleStart}
50:               disabled={state.isConnecting}
51:               className={`px-10 py-4 rounded-full text-xl font-medium text-white shadow-xl transition-all ${
52:                 state.isConnecting 
53:                   ? 'bg-slate-400 cursor-not-allowed' 
54:                   : 'bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-700 hover:shadow-blue-200 active:scale-95'
55:               }`}
56:             >
57:               {state.isConnecting ? 'Connecting...' : 'Start Session'}
58:             </button>
59:             
60:             <div className="mt-8 flex items-center text-sm text-slate-400">
61:               <Video size={16} className="mr-2" />
62:               Camera used for observation only. No video is recorded.
63:             </div>
64:           </div>
65:         );
66:       
67:       case WidgetType.CONVERSATION:
68:         return (
69:           <ConversationWidget 
70:             subtitles={state.subtitles} 
71:             userTranscript={state.userTranscript} 
72:           />
73:         );
74:       
75:       case WidgetType.BREATHING:
76:         return (
77:           <BreathingWidget 
78:             cycles={state.widgetProps.cycles || 3} 
79:             subtitles={state.subtitles} 
80:           />
81:         );
82:       
83:       case WidgetType.EXERCISE:
84:         return (
85:           <ExerciseWidget 
86:             imageUrl={state.widgetProps.imageUrl} 
87:             targetWord={state.widgetProps.targetWord}
88:             subtitles={state.subtitles}
89:             userTranscript={state.userTranscript}
90:           />
91:         );
92:       
93:       case WidgetType.REPORT:
94:         return (
95:           <ReportWidget 
96:             summaryWins={state.widgetProps.summaryWins || []} 
97:             onClose={stopSession} 
98:           />
99:         );
100:         
101:       default:
102:         return null;
103:     }
104:   };
105: 
106:   return (
107:     <div className="relative w-full h-full bg-gradient-to-br from-slate-50 to-blue-50/30">
108:       {/* Hidden elements for camera capture */}
109:       <video ref={videoRef} className="hidden" playsInline muted />
110:       <canvas ref={canvasRef} className="hidden" />
111: 
112:       {/* Main UI Container */}
113:       <main className="w-full h-full flex items-center justify-center">
114:         {renderWidget()}
115:       </main>
116: 
117:       {/* Debug/Status overlay (optional, good for prototype) */}
118:       {state.isConnected && state.activeWidget !== WidgetType.REPORT && (
119:         <button 
120:           onClick={stopSession}
121:           className="absolute top-6 right-6 px-4 py-2 bg-slate-200 hover:bg-slate-300 text-slate-700 rounded-full text-sm font-medium transition-colors"
122:         >
123:           End Session
124:         </button>
125:       )}
126:     </div>
127:   );
128: };
129: 
130: export default App;

(End of file - total 130 lines)
</content>
```
