<path>/home/jay/Workspace/Therapy/.alldocs/aura-extracted/frontend/services/audioUtils.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: export function decode(base64: string): Uint8Array {
2:   const binaryString = atob(base64);
3:   const len = binaryString.length;
4:   const bytes = new Uint8Array(len);
5:   for (let i = 0; i < len; i++) {
6:     bytes[i] = binaryString.charCodeAt(i);
7:   }
8:   return bytes;
9: }
10: 
11: export async function decodeAudioData(
12:   data: Uint8Array,
13:   ctx: AudioContext,
14:   sampleRate: number,
15:   numChannels: number,
16: ): Promise<AudioBuffer> {
17:   const dataInt16 = new Int16Array(data.buffer);
18:   const frameCount = dataInt16.length / numChannels;
19:   const buffer = ctx.createBuffer(numChannels, frameCount, sampleRate);
20: 
21:   for (let channel = 0; channel < numChannels; channel++) {
22:     const channelData = buffer.getChannelData(channel);
23:     for (let i = 0; i < frameCount; i++) {
24:       channelData[i] = dataInt16[i * numChannels + channel] / 32768.0;
25:     }
26:   }
27:   return buffer;
28: }
29: 
30: export function encode(bytes: Uint8Array): string {
31:   let binary = '';
32:   const len = bytes.byteLength;
33:   for (let i = 0; i < len; i++) {
34:     binary += String.fromCharCode(bytes[i]);
35:   }
36:   return btoa(binary);
37: }
38: 
39: export function createBlob(data: Float32Array): { data: string; mimeType: string } {
40:   const l = data.length;
41:   const int16 = new Int16Array(l);
42:   for (let i = 0; i < l; i++) {
43:     int16[i] = data[i] * 32768;
44:   }
45:   return {
46:     data: encode(new Uint8Array(int16.buffer)),
47:     mimeType: 'audio/pcm;rate=16000',
48:   };
49: }
50: 
51: export async function blobToBase64(blob: Blob): Promise<string> {
52:   return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
53:     const reader = new FileReader();
54:     reader.onloadend = () => {
55:       if (typeof reader.result === 'string') {
56:         // Remove the data URL prefix (e.g., "data:image/jpeg;base64,")
57:         const base64 = reader.result.split(',')[1];
58:         resolve(base64);
59:       } else {
60:         reject(new Error('Failed to convert blob to base64'));
61:       }
62:     };
63:     reader.onerror = reject;
64:     reader.readAsDataURL(blob);
65:   });
66: }

(End of file - total 66 lines)
</content>

1: import { useState, useRef, useCallback } from 'react';
2: import { GoogleGenAI, LiveServerMessage, Modality } from '@google/genai';
3: import { WidgetType, AppState } from '../types';
4: import { LIVE_API_MODEL_NAME, SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION, TOOLS } from '../constants';
5: import { decode, decodeAudioData, createBlob, blobToBase64 } from '../services/audioUtils';
6: 
7: const FRAME_RATE = 1; // 1 frame per second as per PRD
8: const JPEG_QUALITY = 0.5;
9: 
10: export function useGeminiLive() {
11:   const [state, setState] = useState<AppState>({
12:     activeWidget: WidgetType.WELCOME,
13:     widgetProps: {},
14:     subtitles: '',
15:     userTranscript: '',
16:     isConnected: false,
17:     isConnecting: false,
18:     error: null,
19:   });
20: 
21:   const sessionRef = useRef<any>(null);
22:   const audioContextsRef = useRef<{ input?: AudioContext; output?: AudioContext }>({});
23:   const streamRef = useRef<MediaStream | null>(null);
24:   const sourcesRef = useRef<Set<AudioBufferSourceNode>>(new Set());
25:   const nextStartTimeRef = useRef<number>(0);
26:   const videoIntervalRef = useRef<number | null>(null);
27:   
28:   // Transcription buffers
29:   const currentInputTranscriptionRef = useRef('');
30:   const currentOutputTranscriptionRef = useRef('');
31: 
32:   const updateState = useCallback((updates: Partial<AppState>) => {
33:     setState((prev) => ({ ...prev, ...updates }));
34:   }, []);
35: 
36:   const handleToolCall = useCallback((fc: any, sessionPromise: Promise<any>) => {
37:     console.log('Tool called:', fc.name, fc.args);
38:     
39:     let result = "ok";
40:     
41:     switch (fc.name) {
42:       case 'show_conversation_widget':
43:         updateState({ activeWidget: WidgetType.CONVERSATION, widgetProps: {} });
44:         break;
45:       case 'show_breathing_widget':
46:         updateState({ activeWidget: WidgetType.BREATHING, widgetProps: fc.args });
47:         break;
48:       case 'show_exercise_widget':
49:         updateState({ activeWidget: WidgetType.EXERCISE, widgetProps: fc.args });
50:         break;
51:       case 'end_session':
52:         updateState({ activeWidget: WidgetType.REPORT, widgetProps: fc.args });
53:         break;
54:       default:
55:         result = "unknown function";
56:         console.warn('Unknown tool call:', fc.name);
57:     }
58: 
59:     // Send response back to model
60:     sessionPromise.then((session) => {
61:       session.sendToolResponse({
62:         functionResponses: {
63:           id: fc.id,
64:           name: fc.name,
65:           response: { result },
66:         }
67:       });
68:     });
69:   }, [updateState]);
70: 
71:   const startSession = useCallback(async (videoEl: HTMLVideoElement, canvasEl: HTMLCanvasElement) => {
72:     if (!process.env.API_KEY) {
73:       updateState({ error: "API_KEY environment variable is missing." });
74:       return;
75:     }
76: 
77:     updateState({ isConnecting: true, error: null });
78: 
79:     try {
80:       const ai = new GoogleGenAI({ apiKey: process.env.API_KEY, vertexai: true });
81:       
82:       // Setup Audio Contexts
83:       const inputAudioContext = new (window.AudioContext || (window as any).webkitAudioContext)({ sampleRate: 16000 });
84:       const outputAudioContext = new (window.AudioContext || (window as any).webkitAudioContext)({ sampleRate: 24000 });
85:       audioContextsRef.current = { input: inputAudioContext, output: outputAudioContext };
86:       const outputNode = outputAudioContext.createGain();
87:       outputNode.connect(outputAudioContext.destination);
88: 
89:       // Get Media Stream (Audio + Video)
90:       const stream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: true, video: true });
91:       streamRef.current = stream;
92:       
93:       // Attach video to element for capturing frames
94:       videoEl.srcObject = stream;
95:       await videoEl.play();
96: 
97:       const sessionPromise = ai.live.connect({
98:         model: LIVE_API_MODEL_NAME,
99:         callbacks: {
100:           onopen: () => {
101:             console.log('Live API Connected');
102:             updateState({ isConnected: true, isConnecting: false, activeWidget: WidgetType.CONVERSATION });
103:             
104:             // 1. Setup Audio Streaming
105:             const source = inputAudioContext.createMediaStreamSource(stream);
106:             const scriptProcessor = inputAudioContext.createScriptProcessor(4096, 1, 1);
107:             scriptProcessor.onaudioprocess = (audioProcessingEvent) => {
108:               const inputData = audioProcessingEvent.inputBuffer.getChannelData(0);
109:               const pcmBlob = createBlob(inputData);
110:               sessionPromise.then((session) => {
111:                 session.sendRealtimeInput({ media: pcmBlob });
112:               });
113:             };
114:             source.connect(scriptProcessor);
115:             scriptProcessor.connect(inputAudioContext.destination);
116: 
117:             // 2. Setup Video Streaming (1 fps)
118:             const ctx = canvasEl.getContext('2d');
119:             if (ctx) {
120:               videoIntervalRef.current = window.setInterval(() => {
121:                 canvasEl.width = videoEl.videoWidth;
122:                 canvasEl.height = videoEl.videoHeight;
123:                 ctx.drawImage(videoEl, 0, 0, videoEl.videoWidth, videoEl.videoHeight);
124:                 canvasEl.toBlob(
125:                   async (blob) => {
126:                     if (blob) {
127:                       const base64Data = await blobToBase64(blob);
128:                       sessionPromise.then((session) => {
129:                         session.sendRealtimeInput({
130:                           media: { data: base64Data, mimeType: 'image/jpeg' }
131:                         });
132:                       });
133:                     }
134:                   },
135:                   'image/jpeg',
136:                   JPEG_QUALITY
137:                 );
138:               }, 1000 / FRAME_RATE);
139:             }
140:           },
141:           onmessage: async (message: LiveServerMessage) => {
142:             // Handle Tool Calls
143:             if (message.toolCall) {
144:               for (const fc of message.toolCall.functionCalls) {
145:                 handleToolCall(fc, sessionPromise);
146:               }
147:             }
148: 
149:             // Handle Transcriptions
150:             if (message.serverContent?.outputTranscription) {
151:               currentOutputTranscriptionRef.current += message.serverContent.outputTranscription.text;
152:               updateState({ subtitles: currentOutputTranscriptionRef.current });
153:             } else if (message.serverContent?.inputTranscription) {
154:               currentInputTranscriptionRef.current += message.serverContent.inputTranscription.text;
155:               updateState({ userTranscript: currentInputTranscriptionRef.current });
156:             }
157: 
158:             if (message.serverContent?.turnComplete) {
159:               currentInputTranscriptionRef.current = '';
160:               currentOutputTranscriptionRef.current = '';
161:             }
162: 
163:             // Handle Audio Output
164:             const base64EncodedAudioString = message.serverContent?.modelTurn?.parts[0]?.inlineData?.data;
165:             if (base64EncodedAudioString) {
166:               nextStartTimeRef.current = Math.max(nextStartTimeRef.current, outputAudioContext.currentTime);
167:               const audioBuffer = await decodeAudioData(
168:                 decode(base64EncodedAudioString),
169:                 outputAudioContext,
170:                 24000,
171:                 1,
172:               );
173:               const source = outputAudioContext.createBufferSource();
174:               source.buffer = audioBuffer;
175:               source.connect(outputNode);
176:               source.addEventListener('ended', () => {
177:                 sourcesRef.current.delete(source);
178:               });
179: 
180:               source.start(nextStartTimeRef.current);
181:               nextStartTimeRef.current += audioBuffer.duration;
182:               sourcesRef.current.add(source);
183:             }
184: 
185:             // Handle Interruption
186:             if (message.serverContent?.interrupted) {
187:               for (const source of sourcesRef.current.values()) {
188:                 source.stop();
189:                 sourcesRef.current.delete(source);
190:               }
191:               nextStartTimeRef.current = 0;
192:             }
193:           },
194:           onerror: (e: any) => {
195:             console.error('Live API Error:', e);
196:             updateState({ error: 'Connection error occurred.', isConnecting: false });
197:           },
198:           onclose: () => {
199:             console.log('Live API Closed');
200:             updateState({ isConnected: false, isConnecting: false });
201:             cleanup();
202:           },
203:         },
204:         config: {
205:           responseModalities: [Modality.AUDIO],
206:           speechConfig: {
207:             voiceConfig: { prebuiltVoiceConfig: { voiceName: 'Kore' } }, // Warm voice
208:           },
209:           systemInstruction: SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION,
210:           tools: [{ functionDeclarations: TOOLS }],
211:           outputAudioTranscription: {},
212:           inputAudioTranscription: {},
213:         },
214:       });
215: 
216:       sessionRef.current = sessionPromise;
217: 
218:     } catch (err: any) {
219:       console.error("Failed to start session:", err);
220:       updateState({ error: err.message || "Failed to start session", isConnecting: false });
221:       cleanup();
222:     }
223:   }, [handleToolCall, updateState]);
224: 
225:   const cleanup = useCallback(() => {
226:     if (videoIntervalRef.current) {
227:       clearInterval(videoIntervalRef.current);
228:       videoIntervalRef.current = null;
229:     }
230:     if (streamRef.current) {
231:       streamRef.current.getTracks().forEach(track => track.stop());
232:       streamRef.current = null;
233:     }
234:     if (audioContextsRef.current.input) {
235:       audioContextsRef.current.input.close();
236:     }
237:     if (audioContextsRef.current.output) {
238:       audioContextsRef.current.output.close();
239:     }
240:     for (const source of sourcesRef.current.values()) {
241:       source.stop();
242:     }
243:     sourcesRef.current.clear();
244:     nextStartTimeRef.current = 0;
245:     
246:     if (sessionRef.current) {
247:        sessionRef.current.then((session: any) => {
248:            if(session && typeof session.close === 'function') {
249:                session.close();
250:            }
251:        }).catch(console.error);
252:        sessionRef.current = null;
253:     }
254:   }, []);
255: 
256:   const stopSession = useCallback(() => {
257:     cleanup();
258:     updateState({ isConnected: false, activeWidget: WidgetType.WELCOME });
259:   }, [cleanup, updateState]);
260: 
261:   return {
262:     state,
263:     startSession,
264:     stopSession
265:   };
266: }

(End of file - total 266 lines)
</content>
```

1: import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
2: 
3: interface Props {
4:   cycles: number;
5:   subtitles: string;
6: }
7: 
8: type Phase = 'inhale' | 'hold' | 'exhale';
9: 
10: export const BreathingWidget: React.FC<Props> = ({ cycles, subtitles }) => {
11:   const [phase, setPhase] = useState<Phase>('inhale');
12:   const [cycleCount, setCycleCount] = useState(0);
13: 
14:   useEffect(() => {
15:     let timeout: number;
16: 
17:     const runCycle = () => {
18:       if (cycleCount >= cycles) return;
19: 
20:       setPhase('inhale');
21:       // Trigger haptic if available
22:       if (navigator.vibrate) navigator.vibrate(200);
23: 
24:       timeout = window.setTimeout(() => {
25:         setPhase('hold');
26:         
27:         timeout = window.setTimeout(() => {
28:           setPhase('exhale');
29:           if (navigator.vibrate) navigator.vibrate([100, 50, 100]);
30:           
31:           timeout = window.setTimeout(() => {
32:             setCycleCount(c => c + 1);
33:           }, 6000); // Exhale duration
34:         }, 4000); // Hold duration
35:       }, 4000); // Inhale duration
36:     };
37: 
38:     runCycle();
39: 
40:     return () => clearTimeout(timeout);
41:   }, [cycleCount, cycles]);
42: 
43:   const getCircleClasses = () => {
44:     switch (phase) {
45:       case 'inhale': return 'scale-150 bg-blue-400 transition-all duration-[4000ms] ease-in-out';
46:       case 'hold': return 'scale-150 bg-green-400 transition-colors duration-500';
47:       case 'exhale': return 'scale-100 bg-blue-200 transition-all duration-[6000ms] ease-in-out';
48:       default: return 'scale-100 bg-blue-200';
49:     }
50:   };
51: 
52:   const getInstruction = () => {
53:     switch (phase) {
54:       case 'inhale': return 'Breathe In...';
55:       case 'hold': return 'Hold...';
56:       case 'exhale': return 'Breathe Out...';
57:     }
58:   };
59: 
60:   return (
61:     <div className="flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-full w-full bg-slate-900 text-white animate-in fade-in duration-700">
62:       <div className="mb-16 text-2xl font-light tracking-widest opacity-80">
63:         Cycle {Math.min(cycleCount + 1, cycles)} of {cycles}
64:       </div>
65:       
66:       <div className="relative w-64 h-64 flex items-center justify-center mb-16">
67:         <div className={`absolute w-48 h-48 rounded-full opacity-50 ${getCircleClasses()}`}></div>
68:         <div className="relative z-10 text-3xl font-medium drop-shadow-md">
69:           {getInstruction()}
70:         </div>
71:       </div>
72: 
73:       <div className="absolute bottom-12 w-full text-center px-8">
74:          <p className="text-2xl text-slate-300">{subtitles}</p>
75:       </div>
76:     </div>
77:   );
78: };

(End of file - total 78 lines)
</content>

<content>
1: /**
2:  * @license
3:  * Copyright 2025 Google LLC
4:  * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
5:  * 
6:  * This script is used to intercept fetch requests to Google Cloud AI APIs and 
7:  * proxy them to the local Node JS server backend server.
8:  */
9: (function() {
10:   const originalFetch = window.fetch;
11:   const originalWebSocket = window.WebSocket;
12: 
13:   // Function to validate VertexGenAi endpoints
14:   function isValidUrl(url) {
15:     try {
16:       const HOST_NAME = 'aiplatform.googleapis.com';
17:       const MODEL_METHODS = ['generateContent', 'predict', 'streamGenerateContent'];
18:       const AGENT_METHODS = ['query', 'streamQuery'];
19:       const isSafePathSegment = (val) => val && encodeURIComponent(val) === val;
20: 
21:       const urlObj = new URL(url);
22:       if (!urlObj.hostname.endsWith(HOST_NAME)) {
23:         return false;
24:       }
25: 
26:       const pathSegments = urlObj.pathname.split('/');
27:       // Publisher models
28:       // Expected structure: ['', '{version}', 'publishers', 'google', 'models', '{model}:{method}']
29:       if (pathSegments.length === 6 &&
30:         pathSegments[0] === '' &&
31:         pathSegments[2] === 'publishers' &&
32:         pathSegments[3] === 'google' &&
33:         pathSegments[4] === 'models' && urlObj.hostname === HOST_NAME) {
34:           if (!isSafePathSegment(pathSegments[1])) {
35:             return false;
36:           }
37:           const modelAndMethod = pathSegments[5].split(':');
38:           return modelAndMethod.length === 2 && isSafePathSegment(modelAndMethod[0]) && MODEL_METHODS.includes(modelAndMethod[1]);
39:       }
40: 
41:       // Reasoning Engines
42:       // Expected structrue: ['', '{version}', 'projects', 'locations', 'reasoningEngines', '{id}:{method}']
43:       if (pathSegments.length === 8 &&
44:         pathSegments[0] === '' &&
45:         pathSegments[2] === 'projects' &&
46:         pathSegments[4] === 'locations' &&
47:         pathSegments[6] === 'reasoningEngines' && urlObj.hostname.endsWith(`-${HOST_NAME}`)) {
48:           if (!isSafePathSegment(pathSegments[1]) || !isSafePathSegment(pathSegments[3]) || !isSafePathSegment(pathSegments[5])) {
49:             return false;
50:           }
51:           const idAndMethod = pathSegments[7].split(':');
52:           return idAndMethod.length === 2 && isSafePathSegment(idAndMethod[0]) && AGENT_METHODS.includes(idAndMethod[1]);
53:       }
54: 
55:       
56:       // Live API (WebSocket)
57:       if (url === 'wss://aiplatform.googleapis.com//ws/google.cloud.aiplatform.v1beta1.LlmBidiService/BidiGenerateContent') {
58:         return true;
59:       }
60: 
61:       return false;
62:     } catch (e) {
63:       return false;
64:     }
65:   }
66: 
67:   console.log('[Vertex AI Proxy Shim] Initialized. Intercepting for Cloud AI API URLs');
68: 
69:   
70:   window.WebSocket = function(url, protocols) {
71:     const inputUrl = typeof url === 'string' ? url : (url instanceof URL ? url.href : null);
72: 
73:     if (inputUrl && isValidUrl(inputUrl)) {
74:       
75:       console.log('[Vertex AI Proxy Shim] Intercepted Vertex WebSocket request:', inputUrl);
76:       const targetUrl = encodeURIComponent(inputUrl);
77:       const protocol = window.location.protocol === 'https:' ? 'wss:' : 'ws:';
78:       const host = window.location.host;
79:       const proxyUrl = `${protocol}//${host}/ws-proxy?target=${targetUrl}`;
80:       return new originalWebSocket(proxyUrl, protocols);
81:     }
82:     return new originalWebSocket(url, protocols);
83:   };
84: 
85:   // Copy propertires to ensure compatibility
86:   window.WebSocket.prototype = originalWebSocket.prototype;
87:   window.WebSocket.CONNECTING = originalWebSocket.CONNECTING;
88:   window.WebSocket.OPEN = originalWebSocket.OPEN;
89:   window.WebSocket.CLOSING = originalWebSocket.CLOSING;
90:   window.WebSocket.CLOSED = originalWebSocket.CLOSED;
91: 
92:   window.fetch = async function(url, options) {
93: 
94:     const inputUrl = typeof url === 'string' ? url : (url instanceof Request ? url.url : null);
95:     const normalizedUrl = (typeof inputUrl === 'string') ? inputUrl.split('?')[0] : null;
96:     // Check if the URL matches the patterns of Vertex AI APIs.
97:     if (normalizedUrl && isValidUrl(normalizedUrl)) {
98:       console.log('[Vertex AI Proxy Shim] Intercepted Vertex API request:', normalizedUrl);
99:       // Prepare the request details to send to the local Node.js backend.
100:       const requestDetails = {
101:         originalUrl: normalizedUrl,
102:         headers: options?.headers ? Object.fromEntries(new Headers(options.headers).entries()) : {},
103:         method: options?.method || 'POST',
104:         // Serialize headers from Headers object or plain object (these should include request auth headers.
105:         // Pass the body as is. The Node backend will handle parsing.
106:         body: options?.body,
107:       };
108: 
109:       try {
110:         // Make a fetch request to the local Node JS proxy endpoint.
111:         const proxyFetchOptions = {
112:           method: 'POST',
113:           headers: {
114:             'Content-Type': 'application/json',
115:             // Add a random header to identify these proxied requests on the Node.js backend.
116:             'X-App-Proxy': 'eHV4Nl8V_da6dItMqTw1ZKYcGwRcKWUo',
117:           },
118:           body: JSON.stringify(requestDetails),
119:         };
120: 
121:         console.log('[Vertex AI Proxy Shim] Fetching from local Node.js backend: /api-proxy');
122:         const proxyResponse = await fetch('/api-proxy', proxyFetchOptions);
123: 
124:         if (proxyResponse.status === 401) {
125:             console.error('[Vertex Proxy Shim] Local Node.js backend returned 401. Authentication may be needed.');
126:             return proxyResponse; // Return the proxy's 401 response.
127:         }
128: 
129: 
130:         if (!proxyResponse.ok) {
131:           console.error(`[Vertex Proxy Shim] Proxy request to /api-proxy failed with status ${proxyResponse.status}: ${proxyResponse.statusText}`);
132:           return proxyResponse; // Propagate other non-ok responses from the proxy.
133:         }
134: 
135:         return proxyResponse;
136:       } catch (error) {
137:         console.error('[Vertex AI Proxy Shim] Error fetching from local Node.js backend:', error);
138:         return new Response(JSON.stringify({
139:             error: 'Proxying failed',
140:             details: error.message, name: error.name,
141:             proxiedUrl: inputUrl
142:           }),
143:           {
144:             status: 503, // Service Unavailable
145:             statusText: 'Local Proxy Unavailable',
146:             headers: { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' },
147:           }
148:         );
149:       }
150:     } else {
151:       // If the URL doesn't match the Vertex API regex, use the original window.fetch.
152:       return originalFetch.apply(this, arguments);
153:     }
154:   }
155: })()

1: 
2: /**
3:  * @license
4:  * Copyright 2025 Google LLC
5:  * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
6:  */
7: import 'dotenv/config';
8: import express from 'express';
9: import { GoogleAuth } from 'google-auth-library';
10: import fetch from 'node-fetch';
11: import rateLimit from 'express-rate-limit';
12: import { WebSocketServer, WebSocket } from 'ws';
13: 
14: const app = express();
15: app.use(express.json({limit: process?.env?.API_PAYLOAD_MAX_SIZE || "7mb"}));
16: 
17: const PORT = process?.env?.API_BACKEND_PORT || 5000;
18: const API_BACKEND_HOST = process?.env?.API_BACKEND_HOST || "127.0.0.1";
19: 
20: const GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION = process?.env?.GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION;
21: const GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT = process?.env?.GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT;
22: if (!GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT || !GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION) {
23:   console.error("Error: Environment variables GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT and GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION must be set.");
24:   process.exit(1);
25: }
26: const PROXY_HEADER = process?.env?.PROXY_HEADER;
27: if (!PROXY_HEADER) {
28:   console.error("Error: Environment variables PROXY_HEADER must be set.");
29:   process.exit(1);
30: }
31: 
32: app.set('trust proxy', 1 /* number of proxies between user and server */);
33: 
34: // IMPORTANT: Vertex AI Studio Rate Limiting
35: // This rate limiting configuration protects your backend APIs from abuse.
36: // Removing it exposes your service to DoS attacks and unexpected costs.
37: const proxyLimiter = rateLimit({
38:     windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, // Set ratelimit window at 15min (in ms)
39:     max: 100, // Limit each IP to 100 requests per window 
40:     standardHeaders: true, // Return rate limit info in the "RateLimit-*" headers
41:     legacyHeaders: false, // no "X-RateLimit-*" headers
42:     message: {
43:       error: 'Too many requests',
44:       message: 'You have exceed the request limit, please try again later.'
45:     },
46: });
47: // Apply the rate limiter to the /api-proxy route before the main proxy logic
48: app.use('/api-proxy', proxyLimiter);
49: 
50: const API_CLIENT_MAP = [
51:  {
52:     name: "VertexGenAi:generateContent",
53:     patternForProxy: "https://aiplatform.googleapis.com/{{version}}/publishers/google/models/{{model}}:generateContent",
54:     getApiEndpoint: (context, params) => {
55:       return `https://aiplatform.clients6.google.com/${params['version']}/projects/${context.projectId}/locations/${context.region}/publishers/google/models/${params['model']}:generateContent`;
56:     },
57:     isStreaming: false,
58:     transformFn: null,
59:   },
60:  {
61:     name: "VertexGenAi:predict",
62:     patternForProxy: "https://aiplatform.googleapis.com/{{version}}/publishers/google/models/{{model}}:predict",
63:     getApiEndpoint: (context, params) => {
64:       return `https://aiplatform.clients6.google.com/${params['version']}/projects/${context.projectId}/locations/${context.region}/publishers/google/models/${params['model']}:predict`;
65:     },
66:     isStreaming: false,
67:     transformFn: null,
68:   },
69:  {
70:     name: "VertexGenAi:streamGenerateContent",
71:     patternForProxy: "https://aiplatform.googleapis.com/{{version}}/publishers/google/models/{{model}}:streamGenerateContent",
72:     getApiEndpoint: (context, params) => {
73:       return `https://aiplatform.clients6.google.com/${params['version']}/projects/${context.projectId}/locations/${context.region}/publishers/google/models/${params['model']}:streamGenerateContent`;
74:     },
75:     isStreaming: true,
76:     transformFn: (response) => {
77:         let normalizedResponse = response.trim();
78:         while (normalizedResponse.startsWith(',') || normalizedResponse.startsWith('[')) {
79:           normalizedResponse = normalizedResponse.substring(1).trim();
80:         }
81:         while (normalizedResponse.endsWith(',') || normalizedResponse.endsWith(']')) {
82:           normalizedResponse = normalizedResponse.substring(0, normalizedResponse.length - 1).trim();
83:         }
84: 
85:         if (!normalizedResponse.length) {
86:           return {result: null, inProgress: false};
87:         }
88: 
89:         if (!normalizedResponse.endsWith('}')) {
90:           return {result: normalizedResponse, inProgress: true};
91:         }
92: 
93:         try {
94:           const parsedResponse = JSON.parse(`${normalizedResponse}`);
95:           const transformedResponse = `data: ${JSON.stringify(parsedResponse)}\n\n`;
96:           return {result: transformedResponse, inProgress: false};
97:         } catch (error) {
98:           throw new Error(`Failed to parse response: ${error}.`);
99:         }
100:     },
101:   },
102: ].map((client) => ({ ...client, patternInfo: parsePattern(client.patternForProxy) }));
103: 
104: // IMPORTANT: Vertex AI Studio SSRF Protection
105: // The set below is the exhaustive allow-list of upstream hostnames this
106: // proxy may forward authenticated requests to. It is sourced at code
107: // generation time from the RestApiClient.getAllowedUpstreamHosts() of every
108: // client embedded in API_CLIENT_MAP. Removing, weakening, or widening this
109: // check (for example, by adding wildcards or computing entries from request
110: // data) re-introduces the SSRF vulnerability that allows the deployed
111: // service account's OAuth access token to be exfiltrated to an
112: // attacker-controlled host.
113: const ALLOWED_UPSTREAM_HOSTS = new Set([
114:   "aiplatform.clients6.google.com",
115: ]);
116: 
117: // Uses Google Application Default Credentials (ADC).
118: // Users need to run "gcloud auth application-default login" in order to use the proxy.
119: const auth = new GoogleAuth({
120:   scopes: ['https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform'],
121: });
122: 
123: function escapeRegex(str) {
124:   return str.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
125: }
126: 
127: function parsePattern(pattern) {
128:   const paramRegex = /\{\{(.*?)\}\}/g;
129:   const params = [];
130:   const parts = [];
131:   let lastIndex = 0;
132:   let match;
133: 
134:   while ((match = paramRegex.exec(pattern)) !== null) {
135:     params.push(match[1]);
136:     const literalPart = pattern.substring(lastIndex, match.index);
137:     parts.push(escapeRegex(literalPart));
138:     parts.push(`(?<${match[1]}>[^/]+)`);
139:     lastIndex = paramRegex.lastIndex;
140:   }
141:   parts.push(escapeRegex(pattern.substring(lastIndex)));
142:   const regexString = parts.join('');
143: 
144:   return {regex: new RegExp(`^${regexString}$`), params};
145: }
146: 
147: function extractParams(patternInfo, url) {
148:   const match = url.match(patternInfo.regex);
149:   if (!match) return null;
150:   const params = {};
151:   patternInfo.params.forEach((paramName, index) => {
152:     params[paramName] = match[index + 1];
153:   });
154:   return params;
155: }
156: 
157: async function getAccessToken(res) {
158:   try {
159:     const authClient = await auth.getClient();
160:     const token = await authClient.getAccessToken();
161:     return token.token;
162:   } catch (error) {
163:     console.error('[Node Proxy] Authentication error:', error);
164:     if (!res) return null;
165:     if (error.code === 'ERR_GCLOUD_NOT_LOGGED_IN' || (error.message && error.message.includes('Could not load the default credentials'))) {
166:       res.status(401).json({
167:         error: 'Authentication Required',
168:         message: 'Google Cloud Application Default Credentials not found or invalid. Please run "gcloud auth application-default login" and try again.',
169:       });
170:     } else {
171:       res.status(500).json({ error: `Authentication failed: ${error.message}` });
172:     }
173:     return null;
174:   }
175: }
176: 
177: function getRequestHeaders(accessToken) {
178:   return {
179:     'Authorization': `Bearer ${accessToken}`,
180:     'X-Goog-User-Project': GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT,
181:     'Content-Type': 'application/json',
182:   };
183: }
184: 
185: // --- Proxy Endpoint ---
186: app.post('/api-proxy', async (req, res) => {
187: 
188:   // Check for the custom header added by the shim
189:   if (req.headers['x-app-proxy'] !== PROXY_HEADER) {
190:     return res.status(403).send('Forbidden: Request must originate from the Vertex App shim.');
191:   }
192: 
193:   const { originalUrl, method, headers, body } = req.body;
194:   if (!originalUrl) {
195:     return res.status(400).send('Bad Request: originalUrl is required.');
196:   }
197: 
198:   // 1. Find the matching API client
199:   const apiClient = API_CLIENT_MAP.find(p => {
200:     // We store extractedParams on req for use later if needed, though getVertexUrl takes it as arg.
201:     req.extractedParams = extractParams(p.patternInfo, originalUrl);
202:     return req.extractedParams !== null;
203:   });
204: 
205:   if (!apiClient) {
206:     console.error(`[Node Proxy] No API client handler found for URL: ${originalUrl}`);
207:     return res.status(404).json({ error: `No proxy handler found for URL: ${originalUrl}` });
208:   }
209: 
210:   const extractedParams = req.extractedParams;
211:   console.log(`[Node Proxy] Matched API client: ${apiClient.name}`);
212:   try {
213:     // 2. Get authenticated access token
214:     const accessToken = await getAccessToken(res);
215:     if (!accessToken) return;
216: 
217:     // 3. Construct the full API URL using env-set GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT/LOCATION and extracted params
218:     const context = {projectId: GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT, region: GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION};
219:     const apiUrl = apiClient.getApiEndpoint(context, extractedParams);
220: 
221:     // IMPORTANT: Vertex AI Studio SSRF Protection
222:     // Parse the constructed apiUrl with the standard URL parser (not a
223:     // regex) and require the resulting hostname to be in the hardcoded
224:     // ALLOWED_UPSTREAM_HOSTS set. This neutralizes attacks that smuggle a
225:     // URL-grammar delimiter (e.g. '#') into a pattern parameter to redirect
226:     // the authenticated upstream request to an attacker-controlled host.
227:     let parsedApiUrl;
228:     try {
229:       parsedApiUrl = new URL(apiUrl);
230:     } catch (e) {
231:       console.error(`[Node Proxy] Invalid API URL: ${apiUrl}`);
232:       return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Invalid API URL.' });
233:     }
234:     if (!ALLOWED_UPSTREAM_HOSTS.has(parsedApiUrl.hostname.toLowerCase())) {
235:       console.error(`[Node Proxy] Upstream host not allowed: ${parsedApiUrl.hostname}`);
236:       return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Upstream host not allowed.' });
237:     }
238:     console.log(`[Node Proxy] Forwarding to Vertex API: ${apiUrl}`);
239: 
240:     // 4. Prepare headers for the API call
241:     const apiHeaders = getRequestHeaders(accessToken);
242: 
243:     const apiFetchOptions = {
244:       method: method || 'POST',
245:       headers: {...apiHeaders, ...headers},
246:       body: body ? body : undefined,
247:     };
248: 
249:     // 5. Make the call to the API
250:     const apiResponse = await fetch(apiUrl, apiFetchOptions);
251: 
252:     // 6. Respond to the client based on stream type
253:     if (apiClient.isStreaming) {
254:       console.log(`[Node Proxy] Sending STREAMING response for ${apiClient.name}`);
255:       // Set headers for a streaming JSON response
256:       res.writeHead(apiResponse.status, {
257:         'Content-Type': 'text/event-stream',
258:         'Transfer-Encoding': 'chunked',
259:         'Connection': 'keep-alive',
260:       });
261:       // Immediately send headers
262:       res.flushHeaders();
263: 
264:       if (!apiResponse.body) {
265:         console.error('[Node Proxy] Streaming response has no body.');
266:         return res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Streaming response body is null' }));
267:       }
268: 
269:       const decoder = new TextDecoder();
270:       let deltaChunk = '';
271:       apiResponse.body.on('data', (encodedChunk) => {
272:         if (res.writableEnded) return; // Prevent writing after res.end()
273: 
274:         try {
275:           if (!apiClient.transformFn) {
276:             res.write(encodedChunk);
277:           } else {
278:             const decodedChunk = decoder.decode(encodedChunk, { stream: true });
279:             deltaChunk = deltaChunk + decodedChunk;
280: 
281:             const {result, inProgress} = apiClient.transformFn(deltaChunk);
282:             if (result && !inProgress) {
283:               deltaChunk = '';
284:               res.write(new TextEncoder().encode(result));
285:             }
286:           }
287:         } catch (error) {
288:           console.error(`[Node Proxy] Error processing streaming response for ${apiClient.name}`);
289:           console.error(error);
290:         }
291:       });
292: 
293:       apiResponse.body.on('end', () => {
294:         deltaChunk = '';
295:         console.log(`[Node Proxy] Vertex stream finished and all data processed for ${apiClient.name}`);
296:         res.end();
297:       });
298: 
299:       apiResponse.body.on('error', (streamError) => {
300:         console.error('[Node Proxy] Error from Vertex stream:', streamError);
301:         if (!res.writableEnded) {
302:           res.end(JSON.stringify({ proxyError: 'Stream error from Vertex AI', details: streamError.message }));
303:         }
304:       });
305: 
306:       res.on('error', (resError) => {
307:         console.error('[Node Proxy] Error writing to client response:', resError);
308:         // The source stream might need to be destroyed if an error occurs here.
309:         if (apiResponse.body && typeof apiResponse.body.destroy === 'function') {
310:              apiResponse.body.destroy(resError);
311:         }
312:       });
313:     } else {
314:       // Non-streaming response handling
315:       console.log(`[Node Proxy] Sending JSON response for ${apiClient.name}`);
316:       const data = await apiResponse.json();
317:       res.status(apiResponse.status).json(data);
318:     }
319:   } catch (error) {
320:     console.error(`[Node Proxy] Error proxying request for ${apiClient.name}`);
321:     console.error(error)
322:     res.status(500).json({ error: error });
323:   }
324: });
325: 
326: const server = app.listen(PORT, API_BACKEND_HOST, () => {
327:   console.log(`Vertex AI Backend listening at http://localhost:${PORT}`);
328: });
329: 
330: 
331: const wss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true });
332: 
333: server.on('upgrade', async (request, socket, head) => {
334:   const url = new URL(request.url, `http://${request.headers.host}`);
335: 
336:   if (url.pathname === '/ws-proxy') {
337:     
338:     let targetUrl = url.searchParams.get('target');
339:     if (!targetUrl) {
340:       console.log('[Node Proxy] Missing target URL');
341:       socket.destroy();
342:       return;
343:     }
344: 
345:     if (targetUrl === 'wss://aiplatform.googleapis.com//ws/google.cloud.aiplatform.v1beta1.LlmBidiService/BidiGenerateContent') {
346:       const location = GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION === 'global' ? 'us-central1' : GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION;
347:       targetUrl = `wss://${location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com//ws/google.cloud.aiplatform.v1beta1.LlmBidiService/BidiGenerateContent`;
348:     } else {
349:       console.log('[Node Proxy] Invalid target URL');
350:       socket.destroy();
351:       return;
352:     }
353: 
354:     let accessToken;
355: 
356:     try {
357:       accessToken = await getAccessToken();
358:       if (!accessToken) throw new Error('No token');
359:     } catch (err) {
360:       console.log('[Node Proxy] Authentication failed');
361:       socket.destroy();
362:       return;
363:     }
364: 
365:     console.log(`[Node Proxy] Initiating upstream connection to: ${targetUrl}`);
366: 
367:     let upstreamWs;
368: 
369:     try {
370:       upstreamWs = new WebSocket(targetUrl, {
371:         headers: getRequestHeaders(accessToken)
372:       });
373:     } catch (e) {
374:       console.error('[Node Proxy] Invalid Upstream URL');
375:       socket.destroy();
376:       return;
377:     }
378: 
379:     const initialErrorHandler = (error) => {
380:       console.error('[Node Proxy] Upstream connection failed:', error);
381:       upstreamWs.removeEventListener('open', onUpstreamOpen);
382: 
383:       if (socket.writable) {
384:         socket.write('HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway\r\n\r\n');
385:         socket.destroy();
386:       }
387:     };
388: 
389:     upstreamWs.once('error', initialErrorHandler);
390: 
391:     // 5. Handle Successful Upstream Connection
392:     const onUpstreamOpen = () => {
393:       // Remove the "bootstrapping" error handler
394:       upstreamWs.removeListener('error', initialErrorHandler);
395: 
396:       // Perform the HTTP -> WebSocket upgrade for the Client
397:       wss.handleUpgrade(request, socket, head, (ws) => {
398: 
399:         upstreamWs.on('message', (data, isBinary) => {
400:           const logMsg = isBinary ? '<Binary Data>' : data.toString();
401:           console.log(`[Upstream -> Client] [${new Date().toISOString()}]: ${logMsg}`);
402: 
403:           if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
404:             if (data === undefined || data === null) {
405:               console.warn('[Node Proxy] Attempted to send undefined/null data to client');
406:               return;
407:             }
408:             ws.send(data, { binary: isBinary });
409:           }
410:         });
411: 
412:         ws.on('message', (data, isBinary) => {
413:           const logMsg = isBinary ? '<Binary Data>' : data.toString();
414: 
415:           let dataJson = {};
416:           try {
417:             dataJson = JSON.parse(data.toString());
418:           } catch (error) {
419:             console.error('[Node Proxy] Failed to parse message from client:', error);
420:             ws.close(1011, 'Failed to parse message');
421:           }
422: 
423:           if (dataJson['setup']) {
424:             dataJson['setup']['model'] = `projects/${GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT}/locations/${GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION}/${dataJson['setup']['model']}`;
425:           }
426: 
427:           if (upstreamWs.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
428:             upstreamWs.send(JSON.stringify(dataJson), { binary: false });
429:           }
430:         });
431: 
432:         upstreamWs.on('error', (error) => {
433:           console.error('[Node Proxy] Upstream error:', error);
434:           ws.close(1011, error.message);
435:         });
436: 
437:         upstreamWs.on('close', (code, reason) => {
438:           console.log(`[Node Proxy] Upstream closed: ${code} ${reason}`);
439:           if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
440:             ws.close(code, reason);
441:           }
442:         });
443: 
444:         ws.on('error', (error) => {
445:           console.error('[Node Proxy] Client error:', error);
446:           upstreamWs.close(1011, error.message);
447:         });
448: 
449:         ws.on('close', (code, reason) => {
450:           console.log(`[Node Proxy] Client closed: ${code} ${reason}`);
451:           if (upstreamWs.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
452:             upstreamWs.close(1000, reason);
453:           }
454:         });
455: 
456:         wss.emit('connection', ws, request);
457:       });
458:     };
459: 
460:     upstreamWs.once('open', onUpstreamOpen);
461: 
462:   } else {
463:     // Path did not match
464:     socket.destroy();
465:   }
466: });
467: 
468: 

(End of file - total 468 lines)
</content>
```

---

_Thinking:_

Now I have a complete picture of the AURA speech therapy zip. Let me analyze what's in it and how it relates to the existing Therapy project. Then I need to run the planning engine to incorporate this new information.

Key findings from the zip:

1. **This is a web-based reference implementation** (React/TypeScript/Vite) of the exact same AURA Speech Therapy system we've been building in Flutter/Dart.

2. **Architecture**: Frontend (React) → Vite proxy → Node.js backend → Vertex AI
   - Frontend at `frontend/` with React + TypeScript + Tailwind
   - Backend at `backend/` with Express + WebSocket + google-auth-library
   - Both under a npm workspace root

3. **Frontend Components** (translation targets for Flutter):
   - `ConversationWidget` → Conversation screen with avatar + subtitles + user transcript
   - `BreathingWidget` → Animated circle, inhale/hold/exhale phases with haptic
   - `ExerciseWidget` → Image display + target word + subtitles + user transcript
   - `ReportWidget` → Session complete with wins list
   - `App.tsx` → Root with routing to widgets via `activeWidget` state
   - `useGeminiLive.ts` → Core hook (equivalent to our `LiveApiService`)
   - `audioUtils.ts` → PCM encode/decode utilities

4. **Backend** (`server.js`, 468 lines):
   - Express server with Google Auth library (ADC)
   - Rate limiting
   - REST API proxy (`/api-proxy`)
   - WebSocket proxy (`/ws-proxy`) with SSRF-safe upstream validation
   - Auto-rewrites `setup.model` to full Vertex path `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/...`
   - Handles both streaming and non-streaming responses
   - SSRF protection with hardcoded `ALLOWED_UPSTREAM_HOSTS`

5. **Key Configuration**:
   - Uses `@google/genai` npm library (not our forked Dart package)
   - `outputAudioTranscription: {}` and `inputAudioTranscription: {}` (empty objects)
   - `responseModalities: [Modality.AUDIO]`
   - Voice: `Kore`
   - Config sent in the `setup` message body (matches our forked `models.g.dart`)

6. **Critical Insight - The empty transcription configs**: The reference uses `outputAudioTranscription: {}` and `inputAudioTranscription: {}` — empty objects! This might be the key to why our `languageCodes` was failing. In the reference, they don't specify any fields inside the transcription configs.

7. **Tool calls**: 4 tools in the reference (Conversation, Breathing, Exercise, EndSession) vs our 11 in `thilina_prompt.dart`. The reference is simpler; our version is more feature-complete (adds `play_sound`, `log_exercise`, `teammate_search`, `graph_memory_search`, `web_search`, `update_patient_state`, `show_text_on_screen`).

8. **Authentication**: The reference frontend uses API Key (`process.env.API_KEY`) + `vertexai: true` in `GoogleGenAI` constructor — but the proxy interceptor hijacks all Vertex URLs and routes them through the backend which adds Bearer token. So effectively it uses ADC OAuth2 via the proxy.

9. **System Prompt**: The reference has a simpler version of Thilina prompt (about 1/3 the length) — ours is more comprehensive with breathing protocol, cueing ladder, session structure, etc.

<skill_content name="planning-research-engine">
# Skill: planning-research-engine

---
id: planning-research-engine
name: Planning & Research Engine
version: 1.0.0
author: OpenCode
requires: [web-search, memory, graph]
tags: [planning, research, architecture, requirements, validation]
---

# Planning & Research Engine

## Purpose
Transform vague ideas into production-ready, research-backed, dependency-aware implementation plans with validation gates. Never allow jumping from idea to code without completing all 15 phases.

## Core Philosophy
> "Most AI systems fail because they jump directly into coding. This skill explicitly prevents that."

## The 15-Phase Workflow

### Phase 1 — Intent Discovery
**Goal:** Understand what the user truly wants, not what they said.

**Questions to answer:**
- What does the user want? (surface request)
- Why do they want it? (underlying motivation)
- Who will use it? (target audience)
- What problem does it solve? (pain point)
- How will success be measured? (KPIs)
- What is the business model? (revenue, cost, sustainability)
- What is the timeline? (urgency, milestones)

**Output:** `docs/intent-report.md`

**Validation Gate:** If any of the 7 questions cannot be answered, STOP and ask the user.

---

### Phase 2 — Gap Analysis
**Goal:** Become aggressively skeptical. Find everything missing.

**Checklist:**
- [ ] Target geography / locale / language?
- [ ] Authentication method?
- [ ] Payment/subscription model?
- [ ] Data storage requirements?
- [ ] Real-time vs async?
- [ ] Mobile vs desktop vs both?
- [ ] Offline support needed?
- [ ] Third-party integrations?
- [ ] Compliance requirements? (GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2)
- [ ] Accessibility requirements? (WCAG level)
- [ ] Performance expectations? (load time, concurrency)
- [ ] Security requirements? (encryption, MFA)
- [ ] Scalability expectations? (users, data volume)
- [ ] AI/ML components? (model, provider, cost)

**Output:** `docs/gap-analysis-report.md`

**Validation Gate:** If >3 gaps found, present to user and resolve before proceeding.

---

### Phase 3 — Contradiction Detection
**Goal:** Find logical impossibilities in requirements.

**Common contradictions:**
- "Real-time collaboration" + "no backend" = CONFLICT
- "Offline-first" + "live sync" = CONFLICT
- "Zero cost" + "AI-powered" = CONFLICT
- "Self-hosted" + "serverless" = CONFLICT
- "No login" + "personalized" = CONFLICT

**Output:** `docs/conflict-report.md`

**Validation Gate:** If unresolved conflicts exist, STOP. Do not proceed to architecture.

---

### Phase 4 — Research Phase
**Goal:** Gather evidence before making decisions.

**Research Categories:**

**4A. Technical Research**
- Frameworks (latest stable versions, LTS status)
- Libraries (bundle size, maintenance, community)
- APIs (rate limits, pricing, reliability)
- Databases (CAP theorem fit, scaling model)
- Infrastructure (cost, complexity, vendor lock-in)
- Security (OWASP, best practices, known vulnerabilities)

**4B. Market Research**
- Competitors (feature matrix, pricing, weaknesses)
- User reviews (pain points, feature requests)
- Business models (subscription, freemium, enterprise)

**4C. UX Research**
- Design patterns (industry standard, emerging)
- Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA minimum)
- Interaction patterns (micro-interactions, feedback loops)
- Device-specific considerations

**4D. Engineering Research**
- Scalability patterns (horizontal vs vertical)
- Performance (caching strategies, CDN, edge)
- Architecture (monolith vs microservices vs modular)
- Testing (unit, integration, e2e, visual regression)

**Research Output Format:**
```
| Topic | Evidence | Source | Reasoning | Recommendation | Tradeoffs |
```

**Output:** `docs/research-report.md`

**Validation Gate:** If research reveals a blocker (e.g., API deprecated, framework abandoned), flag immediately.

---

### Phase 5 — Requirements Documentation
**Goal:** Separate what from how.

**5A. Functional Requirements (FR)**
- What the system DOES
- User stories format: "As a [role], I want [action], so that [benefit]"
- Acceptance criteria for each FR

**5B. Non-Functional Requirements (NFR)**
- How the system BEHAVES
- Performance: load time < 2s, TTI < 3.5s
- Reliability: 99.9% uptime, graceful degradation
- Security: OWASP Top 10 mitigation, encryption at rest + in transit
- Scalability: handle 10x current load without redesign
- Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA
- Compatibility: target browsers, devices, OS versions

**Output:** `docs/requirements-document.md`

**Validation Gate:** Every FR must have acceptance criteria. Every NFR must be measurable.

---

### Phase 6 — Product Definition
**Goal:** Define the product identity.

**Document:**
- Vision (1 sentence, inspirational)
- Mission (1 sentence, actionable)
- Purpose (why this exists)
- Target Audience (demographics, psychographics, tech-savviness)
- User Personas (3-5 primary personas with goals, pain points, scenarios)
- User Journey (end-to-end flow, touchpoints, emotions)
- KPIs (5-7 metrics, with measurement method)
- Success Metrics (quantitative + qualitative)

**Output:** `docs/product-definition.md`

---

### Phase 7 — UX Planning
**Goal:** Design the experience before the interface.

**Flow:**
1. User Goals -> 2. User Tasks -> 3. User Flows -> 4. Wireframes -> 5. Information Architecture -> 6. UI Design

**Document:**
- Navigation structure (primary, secondary, footer, mobile hamburger)
- User flows (happy path, edge cases, error paths)
- Page/component inventory
- Content hierarchy
- Interaction patterns (hover, click, swipe, keyboard)
- Feedback loops (loading, success, error, empty states)
- Pain points and mitigation

**Output:** `docs/ux-plan.md`

**Validation Gate:** No UI component should be designed without a corresponding user task.

---

### Phase 8 — System Architecture
**Goal:** Design the system structure.

**8A. Frontend Architecture**
- Framework (React, Vue, Svelte, etc. with version)
- State management (Redux, Zustand, Context, etc.)
- Routing (file-based, config-based, nested)
- Form handling (React Hook Form, Formik, native)
- Component architecture (atomic design, compound components)
- Styling approach (CSS-in-JS, Tailwind, CSS modules, single CSS file)
- Animation strategy (CSS transitions, Framer Motion, GSAP)

**8B. Backend Architecture**
- Service pattern (MVC, Clean Architecture, Hexagonal)
- API design (REST, GraphQL, tRPC, gRPC)
- Authentication (JWT, OAuth2, Session, SSO)
- Authorization (RBAC, ABAC, ACL)
- Business logic layer
- Middleware (logging, rate limiting, CORS, compression)

**8C. Database Architecture**
- Database type (SQL, NoSQL, graph, time-series)
- Schema design (ER diagram, normalization level)
- Indexes (query patterns, composite indexes)
- Constraints (foreign keys, unique, check)
- Migration strategy
- Backup/recovery plan

**8D. Infrastructure Architecture**
- Hosting (VPS, serverless, edge, self-hosted)
- CDN (Cloudflare, AWS CloudFront, Vercel Edge)
- Storage (S3, local, distributed)
- Monitoring (uptime, performance, errors, analytics)
- CI/CD (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, manual)
- Environment strategy (dev, staging, prod, preview)

**Output:** `docs/architecture-document.md`

**Validation Gate:** Architecture must address all NFRs from Phase 5.

---

### Phase 9 — Device Analysis
**Goal:** Determine all target devices and their implications.

**Device Matrix:**
| Device | Priority | Screen Size | Input Method | Constraints | Special Considerations |
|--------|----------|-------------|--------------|-------------|------------------------|
| Desktop | | | | | |
| Tablet | | | | | |
| Mobile | | | | | |
| Web | | | | | |
| PWA | | | | | |
| Android Native | | | | | |
| iOS Native | | | | | |
| Kiosk | | | | | |
| Smart TV | | | | | |
| Wearable | | | | | |

**Output:** `docs/device-analysis.md`

**Validation Gate:** If mobile is target, mobile-first design is mandatory. If PWA, offline strategy is mandatory.

---

### Phase 10 — Dependency Mapping
**Goal:** Create implementation order based on actual dependencies.

**Dependency Tree Format:**
```
Database Schema
 \ Authentication Service
      \ User API
           \ User Profile UI
                \ Dashboard UI
                     \ Analytics
```

**Rules:**
- A parent must be implemented before its children
- Shared dependencies (Database, Auth) are P0
- Leaf nodes (UI features) are P1/P2
- Circular dependencies must be resolved (break with interfaces/mocks)

**Output:** `docs/dependency-map.md`

---

### Phase 11 — Priority Matrix
**Goal:** Categorize everything by must/should/could/won't.

**MoSCoW Method:**
- **M - Must Have:** System cannot function without this. (P0)
- **S - Should Have:** Important but system works without. (P1)
- **C - Could Have:** Nice to have, deliver if time permits. (P2)
- **W - Won't Have:** Explicitly excluded (document why).

**Output:** `docs/priority-matrix.md`

**Validation Gate:** Must Haves must be <40% of total scope. If >40%, scope is too large for MVP.

---

### Phase 12 — Implementation Roadmap
**Goal:** Convert architecture into time-bound milestones.

**Milestone Format:**
```
## Milestone N: [Name]
**Duration:** X days/weeks
**Goal:** [One sentence]
**Deliverables:**
- [ ] Feature A
- [ ] Feature B
**Success Criteria:**
- [ ] Criterion 1
- [ ] Criterion 2
**Dependencies:** Milestone N-1
**Risks:** [What could go wrong]
**Rollback Plan:** [How to undo if failed]
```

**Output:** `docs/implementation-roadmap.md`

---

### Phase 13 — Progress Tracking System
**Goal:** Every task must be trackable.

**Task Format:**
```
## Task: [Name]
**ID:** T###
**Status:** [Not Started | In Progress | Blocked | Review | Complete]
**Priority:** [P0 | P1 | P2]
**Milestone:** [M#]
**Dependencies:** [T###, T###]
**Estimated Effort:** [hours/days/story points]
**Actual Effort:** [hours/days/story points]
**Owner:** [AI agent / human / external]
**Completion %:** [0-100]
**Blockers:** [None | T### | external factor]
**Validation:** [What confirms this is done correctly]
**Files Modified:** [list]
**Tests Required:** [list]
```

**Output:** `docs/progress-tracker.md` (live document, updated after every task)

---

### Phase 14 — Validation Layer
**Goal:** Block implementation until ready.

**Pre-Implementation Checklist:**
- [ ] Intent Report complete and approved
- [ ] Gap Analysis resolved (<3 open gaps)
- [ ] Conflict Report resolved (0 open conflicts)
- [ ] Research Report complete (all categories)
- [ ] Requirements Document complete (FR + NFR)
- [ ] Product Definition complete
- [ ] UX Plan complete (flows + wireframes)
- [ ] Architecture Document complete (all 4 layers)
- [ ] Device Analysis complete
- [ ] Dependency Map complete (no cycles)
- [ ] Priority Matrix complete (Must Haves <40%)
- [ ] Implementation Roadmap complete (all milestones)
- [ ] Progress Tracker initialized

**If ANY checkbox is unchecked:**
> "Implementation blocked. [Phase Name] is incomplete. Completing now..."

**Output:** `docs/validation-gate-report.md`

---

### Phase 15 — Coding Order (Hierarchical Implementation)
**Goal:** Define the exact order of implementation to minimize rework.

**The Sacred Order:**

```
1.  Requirements          (Phase 5 output)
2.  Architecture          (Phase 8 output)
3.  Database Design       (Schema, migrations, seed data)
4.  Backend Foundation    (Project structure, middleware, config)
5.  Authentication        (Login, register, sessions, JWT)
6.  API Layer             (Endpoints, validation, docs)
7.  Core Business Logic   (The actual value proposition)
8.  Frontend Foundation   (Project structure, routing, state)
9.  UI Components         (Design system, atoms, molecules)
10. Feature Integration   (Wire frontend to backend)
11. Testing               (Unit, integration, e2e, visual)
12. Optimization          (Performance, bundle size, caching)
13. Security Review       (Audit, penetration testing mindset)
14. Deployment            (CI/CD, hosting, domain, SSL)
15. Monitoring            (Errors, performance, analytics, alerts)
```

**NEVER:**
- Design UI before backend API is defined
- Build UI before business logic is implemented
- Deploy before testing is complete
- Skip security review

**Output:** `docs/coding-order.md`

---

## Execution Rules

### Rule 1: No Code Before Phase 14
If the user asks to "just start coding," respond:
> "I cannot implement before validation. Let me complete the planning phases first. This prevents the exact problem you're trying to avoid: scattered files, lost context, and rework."

### Rule 2: Research Before Decisions
Every technology choice must cite:
- Source (official docs, benchmark, case study)
- Reasoning (why this over alternatives)
- Tradeoffs (what you lose by choosing this)

### Rule 3: Document Everything
Every phase produces a markdown file in `docs/`. No phase is complete until its file is written.

### Rule 4: Validate After Every Phase
Before proceeding to N+1, validate phase N against:
- User intent (does this still serve the original goal?)
- Consistency (does this contradict any previous phase?)
- Completeness (are there obvious gaps?)

### Rule 5: Progress is Public
After every task, update `docs/progress-tracker.md` with:
- What was done
- What changed
- What's next
- Blockers

---

## Integration with OpenCode Tools

### Using Pipeline
```bash
# After Phase 1 (Intent), init project
pipeline init <project> -d "<intent summary>"

# After each milestone
pipeline promote <project>
pipeline journal-entry <project> "Milestone N complete: <summary>"

# If blocked
pipeline journal-entry <project> "BLOCKED: <reason>"
```

### Using Memory
```bash
# After each phase, persist learnings
memory remember "<key finding>" --source planning --tags "research,<topic>"

# After technology decision
memory remember "Chose <tech> over <alt> because <reason>" --source planning --tags "decision,<tech>"
```

### Using Graph
```bash
# After architecture phase, build graph
graph build <project> --no-viz

# Query before implementing any feature
graph query "<feature> dependencies" -g <project>/graphify-out/graph.json
```

### Using Scaffold
```bash
# Create project with standard structure
scaffold create <name> --type <type>

# Before modifying any file, backup
scaffold backup <file>  # (if implemented)
```

---

## Output Artifacts Summary

| Phase | Artifact | Location |
|-------|----------|----------|
| 1 | Intent Report | `docs/intent-report.md` |
| 2 | Gap Analysis | `docs/gap-analysis-report.md` |
| 3 | Conflict Report | `docs/conflict-report.md` |
| 4 | Research Report | `docs/research-report.md` |
| 5 | Requirements | `docs/requirements-document.md` |
| 6 | Product Definition | `docs/product-definition.md` |
| 7 | UX Plan | `docs/ux-plan.md` |
| 8 | Architecture | `docs/architecture-document.md` |
| 9 | Device Analysis | `docs/device-analysis.md` |
| 10 | Dependency Map | `docs/dependency-map.md` |
| 11 | Priority Matrix | `docs/priority-matrix.md` |
| 12 | Roadmap | `docs/implementation-roadmap.md` |
| 13 | Progress Tracker | `docs/progress-tracker.md` (live) |
| 14 | Validation Gate | `docs/validation-gate-report.md` |
| 15 | Coding Order | `docs/coding-order.md` |

---

## Emergency Override
If the user explicitly demands to skip planning with "I understand the risks, code now":
1. Warn once: "Skipping planning increases risk of rework, data loss, and context loss."
2. If confirmed: Proceed but create `docs/EMERGENCY_SKIP.md` documenting:
   - What was skipped
   - Why
   - Risk acceptance
   - Mitigation plan
3. Run `pipeline journal-entry <project> "EMERGENCY: Planning phases skipped by user request"`

{

```

**Output:**
```
<skill_content name="self-improvement">
# Skill: self-improvement

---
id: self-improvement
name: Agent Self-Improvement & Learning
version: 1.0.0
author: OpenCode
requires: [meta-cognition, memory]
tags: [learning, improvement, reflection, benchmarking, skill-generation]
---

# Agent Self-Improvement & Learning

## Purpose
The agent must learn from every interaction, success, and failure. Continuous improvement is not optional — it is the path from average to elite.

## Core Philosophy
> "The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing." — Socrates

## Learning Loop

### Step 1: Experience Capture
**After every task, capture:**
```
Task: [What was done]
Approach: [How it was done]
Result: [Success/Partial/Failure]
Time: [How long it took]
Quality: [Code quality, test coverage]
User Satisfaction: [Feedback if any]
```

**Store in:**
- `.Project-Memory/notes/experiences/`
- `memory remember` with tags "experience,<topic>"

---

### Step 2: Success Analysis
**When something works well:**
```
What made it successful?
- [Specific technique that worked]
- [Tool or library that helped]
- [Approach that was effective]

Can this be generalized?
- [Yes/No]
- [How to apply to similar tasks]

Should this become a skill?
- [Yes/No]
- [Skill name and content]
```

**If yes, generate skill:**
```bash
skill generate --from-experience <experience-id>
```

---

### Step 3: Failure Analysis
**When something fails:**
```
What failed?
- [Specific error or issue]

Why did it fail?
- [Root cause]
- [Assumption that was wrong]
- [Knowledge gap]

What was tried?
- [Attempt 1]
- [Attempt 2]
- [Attempt 3]

What finally worked?
- [Solution]
- [Why it worked]

How to prevent next time?
- [Check to add]
- [Skill to update]
- [Process to change]
```

**Document in:**
- `.Project-Memory/notes/failures/`
- `memory remember` with tags "failure,<topic>,lesson"

---

### Step 4: Skill Benchmarking
**Compare skill effectiveness:**

```
Skill: [Name]
Usage Count: [How many times used]
Success Rate: [% of successful outcomes]
Time Saved: [Compared to not using skill]
User Satisfaction: [Feedback score]

Benchmark against alternatives:
- Alternative 1: [Success rate, time]
- Alternative 2: [Success rate, time]
- Current skill: [Success rate, time]

Conclusion: [Keep/Improve/Replace/Retire]
```

**If retire:**
```bash
skill retire <name> --reason "<explanation>"
```

---

### Step 5: Skill Enhancement
**Improve existing skills:**

```
Skill: [Name]
Weaknesses identified:
- [Missing coverage]
- [Outdated information]
- [Inefficient approach]
- [Missing edge cases]

Improvements made:
- [Update 1]
- [Update 2]

Version bump: [vX.Y.Z]
```

**Update process:**
1. Read current skill
2. Identify gaps from recent usage
3. Research latest best practices
4. Update skill content
5. Version bump
6. Test updated skill
7. Document changes

---

### Step 6: New Skill Generation
**When encountering new pattern:**

```
Pattern observed: [What was noticed]
Frequency: [How often this occurs]
Value: [How much time/quality it saves]
Generality: [Can it be applied broadly?]

If valuable and general:
  Generate skill: [Name]
  Document: [Full skill content]
  Test: [Verify it works]
  Publish: [Add to skills directory]
```

**Skill generation template:**
```markdown
---
id: [unique-id]
name: [Human-readable name]
version: 1.0.0
author: [Agent name]
requires: [dependencies]
tags: [categories]
---

# [Name]

## Purpose
[What this skill does]

## When to Use
[Trigger conditions]

## Steps
1. [Step 1]
2. [Step 2]

## Examples
[Code examples]

## Common Pitfalls
[What to avoid]

## Related Skills
[Links to other skills]
```

---

### Step 7: Reflection Loop
**Regular reflection:**

```
Weekly Reflection:
- Tasks completed: [Count]
- Success rate: [%]
- New skills learned: [Count]
- Skills improved: [Count]
- Failures and lessons: [List]
- User feedback: [Summary]

Monthly Reflection:
- Trend analysis: [Getting better/worse/same]
- Skill effectiveness: [Top 5, Bottom 5]
- Knowledge gaps: [What to learn]
- Improvement goals: [Next month targets]
```

**Document in:**
- `.Project-Memory/notes/reflections/weekly/`
- `.Project-Memory/notes/reflections/monthly/`

## Experience Database

### Structure
```
experiences/
├── successes/
│   ├── 2026-06-05-react-optimization.md
│   ├── 2026-06-10-database-indexing.md
│   └── ...
├── failures/
│   ├── 2026-06-03-cors-misconfiguration.md
│   ├── 2026-06-08-memory-leak.md
│   └── ...
├── patterns/
│   ├── common-bugs.md
│   ├── effective-approaches.md
│   └── anti-patterns.md
└── metrics/
    ├── success-rate.json
    ├── skill-usage.json
    └── time-tracking.json
```

### Querying
```bash
experience search "react optimization"
experience search --tag "performance"
experience search --success-only
experience search --failure-only
experience stats --last 30days
experience trends --skill <name>
```

---

## Continuous Improvement

### Daily Habits
1. Review yesterday's work
2. Identify one thing to improve
3. Update relevant skill
4. Log experience

### Weekly Habits
1. Review week's experiences
2. Benchmark skill effectiveness
3. Identify skills to improve
4. Plan learning for next week

### Monthly Habits
1. Comprehensive skill audit
2. Retire obsolete skills
3. Generate new skills from patterns
4. Update skill rankings
5. Set improvement goals

---

## Integration with Other Skills

### With Meta-Cognition:
- Reflection feeds self-awareness
- Failure analysis improves confidence estimation
- Success patterns build expertise

### With Memory:
- Experiences stored in memory
- Skills versioned and tracked
- Learnings searchable and reusable

### With Planning:
- Past experiences inform estimates
- Known patterns speed up planning
- Failure lessons prevent repeats

<skill_content name="graphify">
# Skill: graphify

# Graphify — Knowledge Graph System

## Purpose
Every project must have a knowledge graph. The graph provides a searchable, navigable index of project structure, relationships between components, and planning artifacts.

## Integration Points

| Action | Command | When |
|--------|---------|------|
| Build graph | `graph build <path> [--no-viz]` | After scaffold, after every phase |
| Update graph | `graph build <path> --update --no-viz` | After any file change |
| Query graph | `graph query "<question>" -g <path>/graphify-out/graph.json` | Before reading any code file |
| Find paths | `graph path "<from>" "<to>"` | When tracing dependencies |
| Explain node | `graph explain "<concept>"` | When understanding a component |
| Auto-watch | `autograph watch <path> &` | Continuous updates on file changes |
| Graph stats | `graph stats` | Check graph health and size |

## Phase Binding (from planning-engine)

| Phase | Graph Action |
|-------|-------------|
| Phase 0 (Scaffold) | `graph build <project> --no-viz` + `autograph watch` |
| Phase 1-3 | `graph build <project> --update --no-viz` |
| Phase 4 (Research) | `graph query "<technology>"` before deciding |
| Phase 7 (UX) | `graph query "user flows"` |
| Phase 8 (Architecture) | `graph query "<system>"` for existing patterns |
| Phase 10 (Deps) | `graph path "ModuleA" "ModuleB"` |
| Phase 14 (Gate) | `graph query "all artifacts"` for completeness check |
| Phase 15 (Coding) | `graph build <project>` — full build with visualization |

Base directory for this skill: file:///home/jay/.kimi-code/skills/graphify
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

</skill_files>
</skill_content>

<content>
1: # Master Project Plan: AURA Speech Therapy (Handa)
2: 
3: This master plan outlines the strategic phases for building the AURA Speech Therapy application, an AI-powered speech therapy companion designed for post-stroke patients with aphasia and apraxia.
4: 
5: The plan is divided into 5 phases, moving from a single-patient prototype to a clinically validated platform.
6: 
7: ## Architecture & Core Technologies
8: - **Frontend:** Flutter (Web PWA for Phase 1, Native Mobile later).
9: - **AI Backend:** Google Cloud Vertex AI — Gemini Live API (`gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview`).
10: - **Vision/Face Analysis:** MediaPipe Face Mesh (running locally).
11: - **Therapeutic Approach:** Naming exercises, 4-7-8 breathing protocol, cueing ladders, and emotional regulation.
12: 
13: ## Phases Overview
14: 1. **Phase 1: Web PWA Prototype (Now)** - Focus on the core Gemini Live API integration, basic widgets, and a single patient.
15: 2. **Phase 2: Background Agent Automation (Month 2)** - Automate the digital brain updates and session analysis.
16: 3. **Phase 3: Multi-Patient Scale (Month 3)** - Add therapist dashboard and adaptive difficulty.
17: 4. **Phase 4: Mobile App & Offline Mode (Month 6)** - Full Flutter mobile deployment, offline capabilities.
18: 5. **Phase 5: Clinical Validation (Year 1)** - Hospital partnerships and scale.
19: 
20: Detailed phase documents are available in the `phases/` directory.

(End of file - total 20 lines)
</content>
```

33: ## Completed Phases
34: 
35: ### Phase 1 — Intent Discovery
36: - Asked and answered all 7 intent questions
37: - Identified core need: Sinhala stroke speech therapy for user's father
38: - Key constraint: No login, no monetization, <$20/month, offline-first
39: 
40: ### Phase 2 — Gap Analysis
41: - Identified 30 gaps across 10 categories
42: - 10 critical gaps resolved (Sinhala TTS, streaming STT, image licensing, etc.)
43: - Key finding: Vosk has no Sinhala model — need custom training or Google STT
44: 
45: ### Phase 3 — Contradiction Detection
46: - Found 11 conflicts, resolved 10, 1 ongoing (offline Vosk STT viability)
47: - Key resolution: Hybrid online/offline strategy (Google STT when online, custom Vosk when offline)
48: 
49: ### Phase 4 — Research
50: - Researched: Gemini APIs, STT options, TTS options, databases, Cloudflare Workers, audio streaming
51: - Key findings: Gemini 2.5 Flash ($0.30/1M input), Live API ($0.005/min input, $0.018/min output)
52: - Piper TTS has Sinhala model (UNICEF, ONNX, offline)
53: - Drift (SQLite) best for relational therapy data
54: - Firestore free tier sufficient (50K reads/day)
55: 
56: ### Phase 5 — Requirements
57: - 18 functional requirements with acceptance criteria
58: - 10 non-functional requirements with measurable targets
59: - MVP defined: 10 Must-Haves, 4 Should-Haves, 6 Could-Haves
60: - Critical: 28sp minimum font, warm color palette, no red colors
61: 
62: ### Phase 6 — Product Definition
63: - Name: Handa (හඬ) — "Voice" in Sinhala
64: - 3 user personas: Sunil (patient, 72), Ruwan (caregiver, 42), Dr. Kumari (therapist, 38)
65: - 10 KPIs defined with measurable targets
66: - Success metrics at 3 months and 6 months
67:

69: - Complete information architecture (app map)
70: - 4 user flows defined (daily session, caregiver access, onboarding, typical week)
71: - 20 screens inventoried with key components
72: - Interaction patterns for all user actions with error recovery
73: - Sinhala feedback phrases for all 4 score levels
74: - 10 accessibility design decisions
75: 
76: ### Phase 8 — Architecture
77: - Clean Architecture: data → domain → presentation layers
78: - Drift schema: 6 tables (categories, exercises, sessions, attempts, live_conversations, settings)
79: - Cloudflare Worker proxy for Gemini API key
80: - Firebase AI Logic for Live Conversation (WebSocket)
81: - Scoring engine: Levenshtein (offline) + Gemini (online)
82: - 10 Architecture Decision Records documented
83: 
84: ### Phase 9 — Device Analysis
85: - Primary target: Android tablet (7-12")
86: - Minimum: Android 8.0 (API 26), 3GB RAM
87: - Screen size adaptation strategy with breakpoints (sw600dp)
88: - Device tier detection (low/mid/high) for animation complexity
89: - All Android permissions documented
90: 
91: ### Phase 10 — Dependency Mapping
92: - 5-phase dependency tree (Foundation → Core → Advanced → Caregiver → Polish)
93: - Foundation is entirely sequential (no parallelization)
94: - No circular dependencies
95: - 5 dependency rules defined

154: - **ADC over API Key**: Using Vertex AI with OAuth2 tokens (project `biz-studio-1779528000`) instead of Generative Language API with API key

172: ## Blockers & Risks
173: 
174: | Blocker | Impact | Status | Mitigation |
175: |---------|--------|--------|------------|
176: | ~~`gemini_live: ^2026.6.6` requires Dart SDK >= 3.8.1~~ | ~~Blocks ALL Gemini Live integration~~ | ✅ RESOLVED | Custom Vertex AI Bidi WebSocket client using ADC OAuth2 tokens. No SDK upgrade needed. |
177: | ADC token expiry (1 hour default) | Background agent fails after 1hr | 🟡 MONITOR | Implement token refresh in `VertexAiAuth` class — use refresh token from ADC JSON |
178: | ADC refresh token expires | Full auth failure | 🟡 MONITOR | ~7 day refresh window. `gcloud auth application-default login` re-auth needed |
179: | MediaPipe package compatibility | On-device detection delayed | 🟡 PENDING | Start with silence-only detection, add MediaPipe later |
180: 
181: | Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
182: |------|------------|--------|------------|
183: | Gemini Live API cost higher than estimated | Medium | $15-50/mo | Use session duration limits, text-only fallback |
184: | Sinhala TTS quality (Piper) | Medium | User experience | Pre-recorded audio fallback |
185: | Android haptic variation across devices | Low | UX inconsistency | Use only platform HapticFeedback API |
186: | Time availability for build | Medium | Schedule slip | 7-week plan with buffer |
187: | Background agent latency >60s | Medium | User frustration | Use Gemini Flash (fastest), optimize upload |
188: | AI persona drift over time | Medium | Inconsistent experience | Version-controlled system prompt, consistency tests |
189: | Patient doesn't engage with AI | Medium | Product failure | Test early (Week 3), iterate persona based on father's reactions |

235: ## Sprint 6: Gemini Live Integration via Vertex AI Bidi (M3 — Days 22-27)
236: 
237: **Goal:** Gemini Live WebSocket connection via Vertex AI Bidi streaming endpoint with ADC authentication.
238: 
239: > **Note:** We removed the `gemini_live` package dependency (requires Dart SDK ≥3.8.1; we have 3.7.2). Instead, we implement a custom WebSocket client connecting to Vertex AI's BidiGenerateContent endpoint using OAuth2 tokens from ADC (Application Default Credentials). We have working Vertex AI access verified via `biz-studio-1779528000` project.
240: 
241: | Order | File | Description | Depends On |
242: |-------|------|-------------|------------|
243: | 6.1 | `lib/core/network/vertex_ai_auth.dart` | Manages ADC OAuth2 token acquisition & refresh | — |
244: | 6.2 | `lib/data/datasources/remote/gemini_live_client.dart` | Custom Vertex AI Bidi WebSocket client | 6.1 |
245: | 6.3 | `lib/data/datasources/remote/cloudflare_proxy_source.dart` | Cloudflare Worker for optional server-side auth | — |
246: | 6.4 | **`lib/domain/usecases/sync_data.dart`** | Cloud sync orchestrator | — |
247: | 6.5 | `lib/presentation/providers/live_session_provider.dart` | Live conversation state (Riverpod) | 6.2 |
248: | 6.6 | `lib/presentation/pages/session/live_conversation/live_conversation_page.dart` | Basic live conversation UI | 2.7, 2.10, 6.5 |
249: | 6.7 | `lib/presentation/pages/session/live_conversation/live_summary_page.dart` | Live session results | 2.10, 4.2 |
250: | 6.8 | `workers/gemini-proxy/src/index.ts` | Cloudflare Worker for token relay | — |
251: | 6.9 | `workers/gemini-proxy/wrangler.toml` | Worker configuration | 6.8 |
252: 
253: **Gemini Live Vertex AI Bidi Protocol:**
254: ```
255: Client (Flutter) ←WebSocket→ Vertex AI BidiGenerateContent
256:   Auth: OAuth2 Bearer token from gcloud ADC
257:   URL: wss://us-central1-aiplatform.googleapis.com/ws/...
258:   Model: gemini-2.5-flash
259:   Send: BidiGenerateContentClientMessage (audio chunks + text)
260:   Receive: BidiGenerateContentServerMessage (text + audio)
261: ```
262: 
263: **M3 Validation Gate:**
264: - WebSocket connection establishes with Vertex AI Bidi endpoint
265: - OAuth2 token acquisition from ADC works and refreshes
266: - User speaks → AI hears → AI responds with audio
267: - Live UI shows waveform, transcript, timer
268: - Cloudflare Worker deployed for token relay
269: 
270: ---

272: ## Sprint 7: AI Persona & Therapeutic Protocol (M3a — Days 28-32)
273: 
274: **Goal:** Implement "Thilina" AI persona and the 6-phase session structure.
275: 
276: | Order | File | Description | Depends On |
277: |-------|------|-------------|------------|
278: | 7.1 | `lib/core/ai/ai_persona_config.dart` | "Thilina" persona config: name, voice, behavioral rules | 6.1 |
279: | 7.2 | `lib/core/ai/session_phase_controller.dart` | Tracks which of 6 phases we're in, manages transitions | — |
280: | 7.3 | `lib/core/ai/cueing_ladder.dart` | 5-level cueing ladder logic (0-5) with timer | — |
281: | 7.4 | `lib/core/ai/silence_detector.dart` | Client-side silence timer for cueing triggers | 3.4 |
282: | 7.5 | `lib/core/ai/struggle_classifier.dart` | Combines silence + facial signals → struggle level | 7.3, 7.4 |
283: | 7.6 | `lib/core/ai/system_prompt_builder.dart` | Builds Gemini Live system prompt with memory + persona | — |
284: | 7.7 | `lib/core/ai/psychological_techniques.dart` | Identity reinforcement, validation, containment phrases | — |
285: | 7.8 | `lib/data/datasources/platform/tts_source_sinhala.dart` | Piper TTS for Sinhala (for AI voice + feedback) | 3.6 |
286: | 7.9 | Updated system prompt file | `assets/prompts/thilina_persona_v1.txt` | 7.1, 7.6 |
287: 
288: **Session Phase Structure (implemented in 7.2):**
289: ```
290: Phase 1: Warm-Up (3 min) — breathing + easy wins
291: Phase 2: Naming (8 min) — cueing ladder active
292: Phase 3: Repetition (5 min) — syllable/word drills
293: Phase 4: Fluency (5 min) — pacing exercises
294: Phase 5: Functional (5 min) — conversation/scripts
295: Phase 6: Cool-Down (3 min) — breathing + containment
296: ```
297: 
298: **M3a Validation Gate:**
299: - AI introduces itself as "Thilina, speech therapist"
300: - Cueing ladder triggers at correct intervals
301: - 6-phase session structure flows correctly
302: - Breathing integrates at all phase points
303: - AI maintains persona consistently across 3 test conversations
304:
